question: What was God's reason for making a helper for Adam, per Genesis 2:18?
answer: It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him a help meet for him.

question: What happened after Adam and Eve ate the forbidden fruit, per Genesis 3:7?
answer: The eyes of them both were opened, and they knew that they were naked; and they sewed fig-leaves together and made themselves aprons.

question: What was the serpent cursed with, per Genesis 3:14?
answer: To go upon its belly and eat dust all the days of its life.

question: What was the enmity God put between the serpent and the woman, per Genesis 3:15?
answer: The seed of the woman would bruise the serpent's head, and the serpent would bruise his heel.

question: What did God say would be multiplied for the woman as a result of the fall, per Genesis 3:16?
answer: Her pain in childbirth, and her desire would be for her husband, who would rule over her.

question: What was Adam's curse as a result of the fall, per Genesis 3:17-19?
answer: The ground was cursed; he would eat of it in toil all his days, and return to the dust.

question: Who was Cain's wife, according to what we can infer from Genesis?
answer: A daughter of Adam (unnamed in Scripture; Genesis notes Cain knew his wife).

question: What was the name of Cain's son and the city Cain built?
answer: Enoch; and he built a city and called its name Enoch.

question: How did Enoch (son of Jared) please God, per Genesis 5:24?
answer: Enoch walked with God: and he was not; for God took him.

question: How old was Enoch when God took him, per Genesis 5:23?
answer: Three hundred sixty and five years (365).

question: What did God observe about humanity before the flood, per Genesis 6:5?
answer: Jehovah saw that the wickedness of man was great in the earth, and that every imagination of the thoughts of his heart was only evil continually.

question: What were the dimensions of Noah's ark, per Genesis 6:15?
answer: Three hundred cubits long, fifty cubits wide, and thirty cubits high.

question: What was the ark made of, per Genesis 6:14?
answer: Gopher wood (and pitched within and without with pitch).

question: When did the flood waters begin to abate, per Genesis 8:1?
answer: God made a wind to pass over the earth, and the waters assuaged.

question: Where did the ark rest, per Genesis 8:4?
answer: Upon the mountains of Ararat.

question: What did the dove bring back the second time Noah sent it out?
answer: An olive-leaf (freshly plucked).

question: What did the dove do the third time Noah sent it?
answer: It returned no more to him — a sign the earth was dry.

question: What covenant did God make with all living creatures after the flood?
answer: That He would never again destroy all flesh with a flood; He set the rainbow as a sign.

question: What was God's instruction to Noah after the flood regarding eating?
answer: Every moving thing that liveth shall be food; but flesh with the blood thereof shall not be eaten.

question: Who was Nimrod, per Genesis 10?
answer: He was a mighty hunter before Jehovah; the beginning of his kingdom was Babel, Erech, Accad, and Calneh in the land of Shinar.

question: How old was Abram when Ishmael was born, per Genesis 16:16?
answer: Fourscore and six years old (86).

question: What did the angels do before destroying Sodom and Gomorrah, per Genesis 19?
answer: They struck the men of Sodom with blindness, and told Lot to take his family and flee.

question: What happened to Sodom and Gomorrah, per Genesis 19:24?
answer: Jehovah rained upon Sodom and upon Gomorrah brimstone and fire from Jehovah out of heaven.

question: Who were Lot's two daughters who came out with him from Sodom?
answer: They are unnamed in the text; Genesis refers to them only as his two daughters.

question: What did Jacob say at Peniel about his experience of wrestling, per Genesis 32:30?
answer: I have seen God face to face, and my life is preserved.

question: How did Jacob's hip become injured?
answer: The man (angel) touched the hollow of Jacob's thigh as they wrestled, and it was put out of joint.

question: What was the number of Jacob's household who went into Egypt, per Genesis 46:27?
answer: All the souls of the house of Jacob that came into Egypt were threescore and ten (70).

question: What blessing did Jacob give to Judah, per Genesis 49:10?
answer: The sceptre shall not depart from Judah, nor the ruler's staff from between his feet, until Shiloh come.

question: What blessing did Jacob give to Joseph, per Genesis 49?
answer: Joseph was a fruitful bough; he was helped by the Almighty, and blessings of heaven and earth and breast and womb were his.

question: What did Joseph say to his brothers about their selling him to Egypt, per Genesis 50:20?
answer: Ye meant evil against me; but God meant it for good, to bring to pass, as it is this day, to save much people alive.

question: What were the Israelites forced to build in Egypt, per Exodus 1:11?
answer: Store-cities for Pharaoh: Pithom and Raamses.

question: What did Pharaoh command to be done with Hebrew baby boys, per Exodus 1:22?
answer: Every son that is born ye shall cast into the river.

question: What did Moses do that caused him to flee from Egypt, per Exodus 2:12?
answer: He slew an Egyptian who was beating a Hebrew man.

question: Where did Moses flee after killing the Egyptian?
answer: To the land of Midian.

question: What was Moses doing when God appeared to him in the burning bush?
answer: He was keeping the flock of Jethro his father-in-law.

question: What did God tell Moses the promised land was flowing with?
answer: Milk and honey.

question: What did God say Israel was to Him, per Exodus 4:22?
answer: Israel is my son, my firstborn.

question: Who first hardened their heart against letting Israel go?
answer: Pharaoh hardened his own heart (initially); later God hardened Pharaoh's heart.

question: What was the plague of the boils, per Exodus 9?
answer: The sixth plague — boils breaking out in sores upon man and beast throughout Egypt.

question: What feast was instituted in Exodus 12, in addition to Passover?
answer: The Feast of Unleavened Bread, lasting seven days.

question: What minimum age were the Passover lambs to be, per Exodus 12:5?
answer: A year old — without blemish, a male.

question: What did the Israelites ask of the Egyptians before leaving, per Exodus 12:35-36?
answer: Jewels of silver, jewels of gold, and raiment — and the Egyptians gave them what they asked.

question: How many Israelite men departed Egypt (not counting women and children), per Exodus 12:37?
answer: About six hundred thousand men on foot.

question: How long had the Israelites lived in Egypt, per Exodus 12:40?
answer: Four hundred and thirty years.

question: What did God provide to guide Israel in the wilderness, per Exodus 13:21?
answer: A pillar of cloud by day and a pillar of fire by night.

question: What did God command Moses to do at the Red Sea, per Exodus 14:16?
answer: Lift up thy rod, and stretch out thy hand over the sea, and divide it.

question: What happened to Pharaoh's army in the Red Sea?
answer: Jehovah overthrew the Egyptians in the midst of the sea; the waters returned and covered them.

question: Who led the women in singing and dancing after crossing the Red Sea?
answer: Miriam the prophetess, sister of Aaron.

question: What did the Israelites say when they first tasted the bitter water at Marah?
answer: They murmured against Moses, saying, What shall we drink?

question: How did God sweeten the bitter water at Marah?
answer: Jehovah showed Moses a tree; he cast it into the water and the water was made sweet.

question: What were the Israelites commanded to gather on the sixth day of each week regarding manna?
answer: A double portion (two omers per person), so they would have food for the Sabbath.

question: What happened to any manna left over until morning (except on Sabbath preparation)?
answer: It bred worms and stank.

question: What was an omer of manna to be kept in?
answer: A golden pot, to be kept before Jehovah throughout the generations.

question: How many elders of Israel went up the mountain with Moses and Aaron, per Exodus 24?
answer: Seventy elders of Israel.

question: How long was Moses on the mountain with God, per Exodus 24:18?
answer: Forty days and forty nights.

question: What did the Israelites use to make the golden calf?
answer: Their golden earrings, which they brought to Aaron.

question: How many Israelites were killed after the golden calf incident, per Exodus 32:28?
answer: About three thousand men.

question: What did Moses do with the tablets when he saw the golden calf, per Exodus 32:19?
answer: He cast the tables out of his hands and broke them beneath the mountain.

question: What happened to Moses' face after he spoke with God, per Exodus 34:29?
answer: The skin of his face shone (sent forth beams) because he had been talking with God.

question: What did Moses put on his face when he finished speaking with Israel, per Exodus 34:33?
answer: A veil.

question: What were the five main types of offerings in Leviticus?
answer: Burnt offering, grain (meal) offering, peace offering, sin offering, and guilt (trespass) offering.

question: What was the burnt offering for, per Leviticus 1?
answer: An offering made by fire, of a sweet savor unto Jehovah — for voluntary dedication and atonement.

question: What was the peace offering for, per Leviticus 3?
answer: A freewill offering expressing thanksgiving, fellowship, and well-being before Jehovah.

question: What was the sin offering for, per Leviticus 4?
answer: Atonement for unintentional sins committed against God's commands.

question: What was forbidden to eat from any animal, per Leviticus 3:17?
answer: All fat and all blood were forbidden throughout their generations.

question: What happened to Nadab and Abihu when they offered strange fire, per Leviticus 10?
answer: Fire came out from before Jehovah and devoured them, and they died before Jehovah.

question: What instruction did God give Aaron about not drinking wine before entering the tabernacle, per Leviticus 10:9?
answer: Drink no wine nor strong drink, thou, nor thy sons with thee, when ye go into the tent of meeting.

question: What animals were forbidden to the Israelites as unclean among sea creatures, per Leviticus 11?
answer: Everything in the waters that had not fins and scales — they were an abomination.

question: What birds were listed as unclean in Leviticus 11?
answer: The eagle, ossifrage, ospray, kite, falcon, raven, ostrich, nighthawk, sea-mew, hawk, little owl, cormorant, great owl, horned owl, pelican, vulture, stork, heron, hoopoe, and bat.

question: What was the law of the leper regarding their clothing and crying, per Leviticus 13:45?
answer: The leper was to wear torn clothes, let their hair hang loose, cover their upper lip, and cry, Unclean, unclean.

question: What was the Year of Jubilee's provision for the poor, per Leviticus 25?
answer: All land was returned to its original family; Hebrew slaves were released; debts were forgiven.

question: What does Leviticus 19:18 command regarding neighbors?
answer: Thou shalt love thy neighbor as thyself.

question: What does Leviticus 19:33-34 say about strangers?
answer: The stranger that sojourneth with you shall be unto you as the home-born among you, and thou shalt love him as thyself.

question: What was the total number of Israelites counted in the first census in Numbers 1?
answer: Six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty (603,550) men of fighting age.

question: Why were the Levites not numbered among the fighting men in Numbers 1?
answer: They were set apart to take charge of the tabernacle and its service.

question: How was the Israelite camp arranged around the tabernacle?
answer: By their standards: Judah's division on the east, Reuben's on the south, Ephraim's on the west, and Dan's on the north.

question: What was the Nazirite vow, per Numbers 6?
answer: A vow of separation to Jehovah — no wine or strong drink, no razor on the head, no touching the dead.

question: What did the people complain about regarding their food in Numbers 11?
answer: They were tired of the manna and longed for the fish, cucumbers, melons, leeks, onions, and garlic of Egypt.

question: What seventy men were appointed to share Moses' burden of leadership?
answer: Seventy men of the elders of Israel were brought to the tent of meeting and God put His Spirit upon them.

question: What happened to Miriam after she and Aaron spoke against Moses?
answer: She was struck with leprosy, white as snow; she was shut out of the camp seven days.

question: Why did Moses and Aaron fail to enter the Promised Land, per Numbers 20?
answer: At Meribah, Moses struck the rock twice instead of speaking to it as commanded, dishonoring God before Israel.

question: What was the incident of Baal-peor in Numbers 25?
answer: Israel yoked itself to Baal of Peor through immorality with Moabite women; a plague killed 24,000.

question: Who stopped the plague at Baal-peor by his zealous act?
answer: Phinehas, son of Eleazar, son of Aaron, who speared an Israelite man and the Midianite woman he brought into the camp.

question: What were the daughters of Zelophehad's request, per Numbers 27?
answer: They asked that they receive their father's inheritance since he had no sons; God granted their request.

question: What festival calendar is laid out in Numbers 28-29?
answer: The appointed feasts of Jehovah: daily offerings, Sabbath, new moon, Passover, First Fruits, Trumpets, Day of Atonement, and Tabernacles.

question: What does Deuteronomy 4:2 warn about adding to or taking from God's commands?
answer: Ye shall not add unto the word which I command you, neither shall ye diminish from it.

question: What does Deuteronomy 6:7 say about teaching children?
answer: Thou shalt teach them diligently unto thy children, and shalt talk of them when thou sittest in thy house, and when thou walkest by the way.

question: What does Deuteronomy 8:3 say man does not live by alone?
answer: Man doth not live by bread only, but by everything that proceedeth out of the mouth of Jehovah doth man live.

question: What does Deuteronomy 10:12 say Jehovah requires of Israel?
answer: To fear Jehovah thy God, to walk in all his ways, and to love him, and to serve Jehovah thy God with all thy heart and with all thy soul.

question: What does Deuteronomy 18:15 prophecy about a coming prophet?
answer: Jehovah thy God will raise up unto thee a prophet from the midst of thee, of thy brethren, like unto me; unto him ye shall hearken.

question: What does Deuteronomy 28 outline?
answer: The blessings for obedience and the curses for disobedience of God's covenant.

question: What does Deuteronomy 30:19 set before Israel?
answer: Life and death, the blessing and the curse: therefore choose life, that thou mayest live.

question: What was Moses unable to do in his old age, per Deuteronomy 34:7?
answer: Nothing — his eye was not dim, nor his natural force abated (he was vigorous).

question: How does Deuteronomy 34:10 describe Moses?
answer: There hath not arisen a prophet since in Israel like unto Moses, whom Jehovah knew face to face.

question: What promise did God give Joshua in Joshua 1:5?
answer: As I was with Moses, so I will be with thee; I will not fail thee, nor forsake thee.

question: What did the two Israelite spies promise Rahab in return for her help?
answer: To spare her and all in her house if she tied the scarlet cord in the window and did not report their mission.

question: How did the Israelites cross the Jordan River into Canaan?
answer: The priests carried the ark into the Jordan, the waters parted upstream, and Israel crossed on dry ground.

question: What memorial did Joshua set up at the Jordan River crossing?
answer: Twelve stones taken from the middle of the Jordan, one for each tribe, as a memorial of the crossing.

question: What happened to the Israelites after they entered Canaan regarding circumcision?
answer: Joshua circumcised all the males born in the wilderness who had not been circumcised.

question: What was the manna's status when Israel entered Canaan, per Joshua 5:12?
answer: The manna ceased the day after they ate of the produce of the land; there was manna no more.

question: Who appeared to Joshua near Jericho as the captain of the host of Jehovah?
answer: A man with a drawn sword — the captain of the host of Jehovah (a theophany).

question: How did the Israelites march around Jericho for six days?
answer: The armed men went before, followed by seven priests with trumpets, the ark, and the rearguard — once around per day, in silence.

question: What instruction did Joshua give Israel about the spoil at Jericho?
answer: Keep yourselves from the devoted thing, lest ye make the camp of Israel devoted and trouble it; all the silver, gold, and vessels of brass and iron are holy unto Jehovah.

question: What was the value of the goods Achan took from Jericho?
answer: A goodly Babylonish mantle, two hundred shekels of silver, and a wedge of gold of fifty shekels weight.

question: What happened to Achan and his family after the sin was discovered?
answer: All Israel stoned them with stones and burned them with fire in the valley of Achor.

question: How did Joshua defeat the five Amorite kings in Joshua 10?
answer: The sun and moon stood still; God cast great stones (hailstones) upon them from heaven; they were routed.

question: What summary did Joshua give at the end of the conquest, per Joshua 21:43-45?
answer: Jehovah gave unto Israel all the land; not one thing had failed of all the good things Jehovah had spoken.

question: What did Joshua say at Shechem in Joshua 24:15?
answer: Choose you this day whom ye will serve... but as for me and my house, we will serve Jehovah.

question: What was the cycle repeated in the book of Judges?
answer: Israel sinned, God allowed oppression, Israel cried out, God raised a judge to deliver them, and peace came — then the cycle repeated.

question: What left-handed judge from Benjamin killed King Eglon of Moab?
answer: Ehud.

question: How did Ehud kill King Eglon?
answer: He made a double-edged dagger and thrust it into Eglon's belly while presenting tribute.

question: What was the name of the woman who killed Sisera with a tent peg?
answer: Jael, wife of Heber the Kenite.

question: What song celebrated the victory over Sisera?
answer: The Song of Deborah (Judges 5).

question: What was the name of Gideon's father?
answer: Joash the Abiezrite.

question: What was Gideon's altar called, per Judges 6:24?
answer: Jehovah-shalom (Jehovah is peace).

question: What was Gideon also called, per Judges 6:32?
answer: Jerubbaal (let Baal contend against him).

question: How many men did Gideon start with before God reduced the army?
answer: Thirty-two thousand.

question: How did God reduce Gideon's army to 300?
answer: First 22,000 who were afraid went home; then from 10,000 remaining, those who cupped water to their mouth (300) were kept; the rest were sent away.

question: What did the 300 men of Gideon shout during the battle?
answer: The sword of Jehovah and of Gideon!

question: What did Jephthah vow before the battle against Ammon?
answer: Whatsoever cometh forth from my house to meet me when I return, I will offer it up as a burnt-offering.

question: Who met Jephthah first when he returned home?
answer: His daughter, his only child, coming out to meet him with timbrels and dancing.

question: What riddle did Samson pose at his wedding feast?
answer: Out of the eater came forth food, and out of the strong came forth sweetness (about the lion and honey).

question: What did the Philistines answer to Samson's riddle?
answer: What is sweeter than honey? and what is stronger than a lion? (having gotten the answer from Samson's wife).

question: What did Samson use to burn the Philistines' grain fields?
answer: Three hundred foxes tied tail to tail with torches.

question: What name did Samson call the place where he killed 1000 Philistines with a jawbone?
answer: Ramath-lehi (the hill of the jawbone).

question: What were Samson's final words, per Judges 16:28?
answer: O Lord Jehovah, remember me, I pray thee, and strengthen me, I pray thee, only this once, O God, that I may be at once avenged of the Philistines for my two eyes.

question: How many did Samson kill at his death, per Judges 16:30?
answer: The dead that he slew at his death were more than they that he slew in his life.

question: What was the last verse of Judges and its meaning, per Judges 21:25?
answer: In those days there was no king in Israel: every man did that which was right in his own eyes.

question: What was Ruth's other sister-in-law who returned to her own people, per Ruth 1?
answer: Orpah.

question: What did Naomi rename herself after returning from Moab?
answer: Mara (meaning bitter), because Jehovah had dealt bitterly with her.

question: What does the name 'Naomi' mean?
answer: Pleasant.

question: What was the practice of gleaning, per Ruth 2?
answer: Following behind reapers in the field to gather leftover grain — a provision for the poor in the Mosaic law.

question: How did Boaz instruct his servants regarding Ruth's gleaning?
answer: Let fall also some from the bundles on purpose; leave them that she may glean.

question: What was the kinsman-redeemer's duty?
answer: To marry a deceased relative's widow and maintain the family inheritance.

question: What was the legal custom when a man refused the kinsman-redeemer duty, per Ruth 4:7-8?
answer: He drew off his sandal and gave it to the other man as a token.

question: Why did the closer kinsman decline to redeem Ruth and Naomi's land?
answer: He feared it would ruin his own inheritance; so he gave the right of redemption to Boaz.

question: What was the genealogy at the end of Ruth leading to?
answer: Boaz begat Obed; Obed begat Jesse; Jesse begat David.

question: What did Eli's sons Hophni and Phinehas do that was wicked, per 1 Samuel 2?
answer: They took the best portions of sacrifices by force and lay with women who served at the tent door.

question: What prophecy was given against Eli's house, per 1 Samuel 2:34?
answer: His two sons Hophni and Phinehas would die on the same day as a sign of judgment.

question: What was Samuel's first prophetic message, per 1 Samuel 3?
answer: A message of judgment against Eli's house because his sons blasphemed God and he did not restrain them.

question: What happened to the ark when the Philistines put it in the temple of Dagon?
answer: Dagon fell on his face before the ark and his head and hands were broken off.

question: What happened to the Philistines who held the ark?
answer: Jehovah afflicted them with tumors (emerods); they sent the ark back to Israel.

question: How long was the ark in the house of Abinadab at Kirjath-jearim?
answer: Twenty years.

question: What was the name of Samuel's two corrupt sons whom Israel refused as judges?
answer: Joel and Abiah (Abijah).

question: What did Saul look like physically, per 1 Samuel 9:2?
answer: A choice young man and goodly; there was not among the children of Israel a goodlier person; he was higher than any of the people from his shoulders and upward.

question: What did Samuel pour on Saul's head when anointing him, per 1 Samuel 10:1?
answer: A vial of oil.

question: What did the Spirit of God come upon Saul and cause him to do?
answer: To prophesy among a band of prophets.

question: What is the meaning of the saying 'Is Saul also among the prophets?' per 1 Samuel 10:11?
answer: It expressed surprise — since Saul was known as a warrior/ruler, not a prophet.

question: How does 1 Samuel 15:22 summarize what God desires more than sacrifice?
answer: To obey is better than sacrifice, and to hearken than the fat of rams.

question: Who anointed David as king in Bethlehem, per 1 Samuel 16?
answer: Samuel.

question: What did Jonathan give to David as a covenant gift, per 1 Samuel 18:4?
answer: His robe, armour, sword, bow, and girdle.

question: What was Saul's daughter Michal's role in helping David, per 1 Samuel 19?
answer: She let David down through a window to escape and put an idol in the bed to fool Saul's messengers.

question: What did David cut from Saul while Saul slept in the cave at En-gedi?
answer: The skirt (corner) of Saul's robe.

question: Why did David refuse to kill Saul when he had the chance?
answer: Saul was Jehovah's anointed, and David would not stretch forth his hand against him.

question: Who was Nabal, and what did David almost do because of him?
answer: A wealthy man who refused to help David's men; David was about to kill all males of his household.

question: Who was Nabal's wife who intercepted David and brought provisions?
answer: Abigail.

question: What happened to Nabal after his wife told him what she had done?
answer: His heart died within him and he became as a stone; about ten days later Jehovah smote him and he died.

question: What witch (medium) did Saul consult before his final battle, per 1 Samuel 28?
answer: The woman at En-dor (the witch of Endor).

question: Who did the medium bring up, at Saul's request?
answer: Samuel (his spirit/shade appeared).

question: What was Samuel's message to Saul from beyond the grave?
answer: That Jehovah had departed from Saul; Israel would be delivered to the Philistines; Saul and his sons would die the next day.

question: What was the test by which Solomon discerned the true mother of the baby?
answer: When he proposed to cut the baby in two, the true mother gave up her claim to save the child's life.

question: What did Solomon ask for instead of long life or riches or revenge, per 1 Kings 3?
answer: An understanding heart to judge God's people and discern between good and evil.

question: What did Solomon observe that demonstrated his gift of wisdom, per 1 Kings 4:33?
answer: He spake of trees, from the cedar even unto the hyssop; also of beasts, fowl, creeping things, and fish.

question: How many workers did Solomon draft to work on the temple, per 1 Kings 5?
answer: Thirty thousand laborers sent to Lebanon in shifts of 10,000, plus 70,000 burden-bearers and 80,000 quarrymen.

question: What was in the Holy Place of Solomon's temple?
answer: The golden altar of incense, the table of showbread, and the ten golden lampstands.

question: What was in the Holy of Holies (Most Holy Place) of Solomon's temple?
answer: The ark of the covenant, overshadowed by two golden cherubim.

question: What happened when the ark was brought into the temple, per 1 Kings 8:10-11?
answer: The cloud filled the house of Jehovah so the priests could not stand to minister.

question: What did God tell Solomon after the temple dedication, per 1 Kings 9:3?
answer: I have heard thy prayer and thy supplication; I have hallowed this house which thou hast built, to put my name there for ever.

question: What happened to Solomon's kingdom because of his sin with foreign women?
answer: God tore the kingdom from him after his death, leaving only one tribe (Judah) to his son.

question: Who was the first king of the northern kingdom of Israel?
answer: Jeroboam (son of Nebat).

question: What sin of Jeroboam became a perpetual pattern in the northern kings?
answer: He made two golden calves and said, Behold thy gods, O Israel, which brought thee up out of Egypt — setting up false worship.

question: How did God provide food for Elijah at the brook Cherith?
answer: Ravens brought him bread and flesh in the morning and in the evening; he drank from the brook.

question: What did Elijah pray under the juniper tree?
answer: It is enough; now, O Jehovah, take away my life; for I am not better than my fathers.

question: How did God twice provide food for Elijah under the juniper tree?
answer: An angel touched him and said, Arise and eat; he found a cake baked on coals and a cruse of water, twice.

question: What was the still small voice after the wind, earthquake, and fire at Horeb?
answer: The voice of Jehovah telling Elijah to return and anoint Hazael, Jehu, and Elisha.

question: How did Elisha show that Elijah's spirit had doubled upon him at the Jordan?
answer: He struck the waters with Elijah's mantle and they parted, as they had for Elijah.

question: What did the group of prophets' sons observe about the Jordan crossing?
answer: They said, The spirit of Elijah doth rest on Elisha — and bowed before him.

question: What healing miracle did Elisha perform with salt at Jericho?
answer: He cast salt into the spring of water and said, I have healed these waters; the waters were healed.

question: What happened to the youths who mocked Elisha, per 2 Kings 2:24?
answer: He cursed them in the name of Jehovah; two she-bears came out of the wood and tore forty-two of them.

question: What was the Shunammite woman's gift to Elisha?
answer: She built a small chamber on the wall with a bed, table, stool, and candlestick, so he could rest there.

question: What miracle did Elisha perform with twenty barley loaves for one hundred men?
answer: He said, Give it unto the people that they may eat; they ate and had some left over, according to Jehovah's word.

question: What sign did Isaiah give Hezekiah confirming fifteen more years of life?
answer: The shadow on the sundial of Ahaz went back ten degrees.

question: What prayer of Hezekiah moved God to spare Jerusalem from Sennacherib?
answer: He spread Sennacherib's letter before Jehovah in the temple and prayed for deliverance.

question: What instructions did David give Solomon about building the temple, per 1 Chronicles 22?
answer: David told Solomon to be strong and courageous, to build the house of Jehovah, and gave him all the plans and materials.

question: What does 1 Chronicles 16:11 exhort?
answer: Seek ye Jehovah and his strength; seek his face evermore.

question: What was David's total offering for the temple, per 1 Chronicles 22:14?
answer: An hundred thousand talents of gold, a thousand thousand talents of silver, and brass and iron in abundance.

question: What does 2 Chronicles 20:15 say the battle is?
answer: The battle is not yours, but God's.

question: What did Jehoshaphat do before battle that led to God's deliverance in 2 Chronicles 20?
answer: He appointed singers to praise Jehovah as they went out before the army; Jehovah set ambushments against their enemies.

question: What book does Ezra quote when describing Cyrus's decree?
answer: A message is given in the spirit of Jehovah's word through Jeremiah (the prophecy of Jeremiah).

question: What did the older priests and Levites do when they saw the rebuilt temple foundation, per Ezra 3?
answer: Many who had seen the former temple wept with a loud voice; others shouted for joy.

question: What groups opposed the rebuilding of the temple after the exile?
answer: The adversaries of Judah and Benjamin (the people of the land) — Samaritans and others who wrote letters to stop the work.

question: What two prophets encouraged the rebuilding in Ezra 5?
answer: Haggai and Zechariah.

question: What did Nehemiah do when he heard the report about Jerusalem's ruined walls?
answer: He sat down and wept and mourned for days, fasting and praying before God.

question: What did Nehemiah's enemies Sanballat, Tobiah, and Geshem do to hinder the work?
answer: They mocked, accused Israel of rebellion, and tried to call Nehemiah to a meeting to harm him.

question: What was the Israelites' strategy while rebuilding the wall with enemies threatening?
answer: Half worked while half held spears, shields, bows, and coats of mail; the workers carried their weapons.

question: What feast did Nehemiah and Ezra proclaim when the people wept upon hearing the law read?
answer: They said, This day is holy unto Jehovah your God; go your way... the joy of Jehovah is your strength.

question: Who was Esther's Persian name, and what was her Hebrew name?
answer: Esther was her Persian name; her Hebrew name was Hadassah.

question: What was Esther's position before she became queen?
answer: She was a Jewish orphan raised by her cousin Mordecai.

question: What was the law about approaching the king uninvited, per Esther 4?
answer: Anyone who came to the inner court without being called was to be put to death, unless the king extended his golden sceptre.

question: What banquet did Esther prepare before making her request to the king?
answer: Two banquets — she first invited the king and Haman to a banquet, then to a second banquet where she made her request.

question: How high were the gallows Haman built for Mordecai, per Esther 5:14?
answer: Fifty cubits high.

question: What honor did the king command be given to Mordecai in Esther 6?
answer: Haman led Mordecai on horseback through the city square, proclaiming, Thus shall it be done to the man whom the king delighteth to honor.

question: What did God say to Satan about Job in Job 1:8?
answer: Hast thou considered my servant Job? for there is none like him in the earth, a perfect and an upright man.

question: What was Satan's accusation about why Job feared God, per Job 1:9-10?
answer: Doth Job fear God for nought? Hast thou not made a hedge about him?

question: What were Job's first words after losing everything, per Job 1:21?
answer: Naked came I out of my mother's womb, and naked shall I return thither: Jehovah gave, and Jehovah hath taken away; blessed be the name of Jehovah.

question: What did Job's wife say to him in his suffering, per Job 2:9?
answer: Dost thou still hold fast thine integrity? Renounce God, and die.

question: What was Job's response to his wife's suggestion?
answer: Thou speakest as one of the foolish women speaketh. What? shall we receive good at the hand of God, and shall we not receive evil?

question: Who were Job's three friends who came to comfort him?
answer: Eliphaz the Temanite, Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite.

question: How long did Job's three friends sit with him in silence?
answer: Seven days and seven nights.

question: What was the main argument of Job's friends?
answer: That Job must have sinned greatly to deserve such suffering; God is just and punishes the wicked.

question: What young man entered the debate after the three friends had nothing more to say?
answer: Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite.

question: Why was Elihu angry with Job's friends?
answer: Because they had found no answer, and yet had condemned Job.

question: What did God say to Eliphaz about Job's three friends, per Job 42:7?
answer: My wrath is kindled against thee, and against thy two friends: for ye have not spoken of me the thing that is right, as my servant Job hath.

question: How did God restore Job, per Job 42:10?
answer: Jehovah turned the captivity of Job, when he prayed for his friends; and Jehovah gave Job twice as much as he had before.

question: What does Psalm 2:7 say about Jehovah and His anointed?
answer: Thou art my son; this day have I begotten thee.

question: What does Psalm 8:4 ask?
answer: What is man, that thou art mindful of him? and the son of man, that thou visitest him?

question: What does Psalm 16:10 say about the Holy One?
answer: Thou wilt not leave my soul to Sheol; neither wilt thou suffer thy holy one to see corruption.

question: What does Psalm 24:1 declare?
answer: The earth is Jehovah's, and the fulness thereof; the world, and they that dwell therein.

question: What does Psalm 24:3 ask?
answer: Who shall ascend into the hill of Jehovah? and who shall stand in his holy place?

question: What is Psalm 29 about?
answer: The voice of Jehovah in the thunderstorm — Jehovah's power over creation.

question: What does Psalm 32:1-2 say about the blessed man?
answer: Blessed is he whose transgression is forgiven, whose sin is covered. Blessed is the man unto whom Jehovah imputeth not iniquity.

question: What does Psalm 34:8 invite?
answer: O taste and see that Jehovah is good: blessed is the man that taketh refuge in him.

question: What does Psalm 40:8 say about delighting in God's will?
answer: I delight to do thy will, O my God; yea, thy law is within my heart.

question: What does Psalm 42:1 compare the soul's longing to?
answer: As the hart panteth after the water brooks, so panteth my soul after thee, O God.

question: What does Psalm 51:1-2 ask of God?
answer: Have mercy upon me, O God... blot out my transgressions. Wash me thoroughly from mine iniquity, and cleanse me from my sin.

question: What does Psalm 56:3 say about trust in time of fear?
answer: What time I am afraid, I will put my trust in thee.

question: What does Psalm 68:5 call God?
answer: A father of the fatherless, and a judge of the widows, is God in his holy habitation.

question: What does Psalm 72:11 prophesy about the kings of the earth?
answer: All kings shall fall down before him; all nations shall serve him.

question: What does Psalm 84:10 say about a day in Jehovah's courts?
answer: A day in thy courts is better than a thousand. I had rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God, than to dwell in the tents of wickedness.

question: What does Psalm 90:10 say about the length of human life?
answer: The days of our years are threescore years and ten, or even by reason of strength fourscore years.

question: What does Psalm 90:12 ask God to teach us?
answer: So to number our days, that we may get us a heart of wisdom.

question: What does Psalm 94:11 say Jehovah knows?
answer: Jehovah knoweth the thoughts of man, that they are vanity.

question: What does Psalm 96:3 command?
answer: Declare his glory among the nations, his marvellous works among all the peoples.

question: What does Psalm 107:1 say to give thanks for?
answer: Give thanks unto Jehovah; for he is good; for his lovingkindness endureth for ever.

question: What does Psalm 110:1 say to the Lord?
answer: Jehovah saith unto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool.

question: What does Psalm 111:10 say is the beginning of wisdom?
answer: The fear of Jehovah is the beginning of wisdom.

question: What does Psalm 115:3 say about God?
answer: Our God is in the heavens: he hath done whatsoever he pleased.

question: What does Psalm 116:15 say about the death of the saints?
answer: Precious in the sight of Jehovah is the death of his saints.

question: What does Psalm 121:1-2 say about where help comes from?
answer: I will lift up mine eyes unto the mountains: from whence shall my help come? My help cometh from Jehovah, who made heaven and earth.

question: What does Psalm 122:1 express about going to the house of Jehovah?
answer: I was glad when they said unto me, Let us go unto the house of Jehovah.

question: What does Psalm 126:5-6 say about sowing in tears?
answer: They that sow in tears shall reap in joy. He that goeth forth and weepeth, bearing seed for sowing, shall come again with joy, bringing his sheaves with him.

question: What does Psalm 127:1 say about building a house?
answer: Except Jehovah build the house, they labor in vain that build it.

question: What does Psalm 128:1-2 say about the blessed man?
answer: Blessed is every one that feareth Jehovah, that walketh in his ways. For thou shalt eat the labor of thy hands.

question: What does Psalm 133:1 celebrate?
answer: Behold, how good and how pleasant it is for brethren to dwell together in unity!

question: What does Psalm 136 repeatedly declare?
answer: For his lovingkindness endureth for ever (said after each of 26 lines).

question: What does Psalm 139:1-2 say about God's knowledge of us?
answer: O Jehovah, thou hast searched me, and known me. Thou knowest my downsitting and mine uprising; thou understandest my thought afar off.

question: What does Psalm 145:9 say about Jehovah's goodness?
answer: Jehovah is good to all; and his tender mercies are over all his works.

question: What does Psalm 147:3 say about God and the brokenhearted?
answer: He healeth the broken in heart, and bindeth up their wounds.

question: What does Psalm 148 call to praise God?
answer: All of creation: angels, hosts, sun, moon, stars, waters, sea creatures, hail, snow, mountains, trees, beasts, peoples, kings, and children.

question: What does Proverbs 4:23 say about the heart?
answer: Keep thy heart with all diligence; for out of it are the issues of life.

question: What does Proverbs 6:16-19 list as things Jehovah hates?
answer: A proud look, a lying tongue, hands that shed innocent blood, a heart that deviseth wicked thoughts, feet that are swift to mischief, a false witness, and one who soweth discord.

question: What does Proverbs 11:2 say about pride and wisdom?
answer: When pride cometh, then cometh shame; but with the lowly is wisdom.

question: What does Proverbs 12:15 say about the wise man?
answer: The way of a fool is right in his own eyes; but he that is wise hearkeneth unto counsel.

question: What does Proverbs 13:24 say about discipline?
answer: He that spareth his rod hateth his son; but he that loveth him chasteneth him diligently.

question: What does Proverbs 15:1 say about a soft answer?
answer: A soft answer turneth away wrath; but a grievous word stirreth up anger.

question: What does Proverbs 17:17 say about a friend?
answer: A friend loveth at all times; and a brother is born for adversity.

question: What does Proverbs 17:22 say about a merry heart?
answer: A merry heart doeth good like a medicine; but a broken spirit drieth up the bones.

question: What does Proverbs 20:1 say about wine?
answer: Wine is a mocker, strong drink a brawler; and whosoever erreth thereby is not wise.

question: What does Proverbs 21:2 say about the way of a man?
answer: Every way of a man is right in his own eyes; but Jehovah weigheth the hearts.

question: What does Proverbs 23:7 say about what a man thinks?
answer: As he thinketh within himself, so is he.

question: What does Proverbs 25:11 compare a word fitly spoken to?
answer: Apples of gold in network of silver.

question: What does Proverbs 27:1 warn about boasting?
answer: Boast not thyself of tomorrow; for thou knowest not what a day may bring forth.

question: What does Proverbs 28:13 say about confession of sin?
answer: He that covereth his transgressions shall not prosper; but whoso confesseth and forsaketh them shall obtain mercy.

question: What does Proverbs 29:18 say about vision?
answer: Where there is no vision, the people cast off restraint; but he that keepeth the law, happy is he.

question: What is the Agur passage in Proverbs 30, and what does it seek?
answer: Agur asks that God give him neither poverty nor riches, but feed him with the food that is needful.

question: What does the virtuous woman of Proverbs 31 do that shows her worth?
answer: She oversees her household, works with her hands, provides for family and poor, and fears Jehovah.

question: What does Isaiah 2:4 prophesy about swords and plowshares?
answer: They shall beat their swords into plowshares, and their spears into pruninghooks: nation shall not lift up sword against nation.

question: What does Isaiah 6:6-7 describe happening to Isaiah's lips?
answer: A seraph flew to him with a live coal from the altar and touched his lips, and his iniquity was taken away.

question: What does Isaiah 11:1 prophesy about a shoot from Jesse?
answer: There shall come forth a shoot out of the stock of Jesse, and a branch out of his roots shall bear fruit.

question: What does Isaiah 11:6 say about the wolf and the lamb?
answer: The wolf shall dwell with the lamb, and the leopard shall lie down with the kid.

question: What does Isaiah 14:12 say about the fall of the king of Babylon?
answer: How art thou fallen from heaven, O day-star, son of the morning!

question: What does Isaiah 25:8 promise about death?
answer: He hath swallowed up death for ever; and the Lord Jehovah will wipe away tears from off all faces.

question: What does Isaiah 26:3 promise about those whose mind is stayed on God?
answer: Thou wilt keep him in perfect peace, whose mind is stayed on thee; because he trusteth in thee.

question: What does Isaiah 30:21 say about the voice behind?
answer: Thine ears shall hear a word behind thee, saying, This is the way, walk ye in it, when ye turn to the right hand, and when ye turn to the left.

question: What does Isaiah 40:1-2 begin with?
answer: Comfort ye, comfort ye my people, saith your God. Speak ye comfortably to Jerusalem.

question: What does Isaiah 40:3 say about preparing the way?
answer: The voice of one that crieth, Prepare ye in the wilderness the way of Jehovah; make level in the desert a highway for our God.

question: What does Isaiah 40:8 say about God's word?
answer: The grass withereth, the flower fadeth; but the word of our God shall stand for ever.

question: What does Isaiah 43:1-2 promise about passing through waters and fire?
answer: Fear not; I have redeemed thee; I have called thee by thy name, thou art mine. When thou passest through the waters, I will be with thee.

question: What does Isaiah 45:22 call all the ends of the earth to do?
answer: Look unto me, and be ye saved, all the ends of the earth; for I am God, and there is none else.

question: What does Isaiah 48:22 say about the wicked?
answer: There is no peace, saith Jehovah, to the wicked.

question: What does Isaiah 53:3 say about the Suffering Servant?
answer: He was despised, and rejected of men; a man of sorrows, and acquainted with grief.

question: What does Isaiah 54:17 promise about weapons?
answer: No weapon that is formed against thee shall prosper.

question: What does Isaiah 58:6 say is the fast God chooses?
answer: To loose the bonds of wickedness, to undo the bands of the yoke, and to let the oppressed go free.

question: What does Isaiah 60:1 proclaim?
answer: Arise, shine; for thy light is come, and the glory of Jehovah is risen upon thee.

question: What does Isaiah 61:1 say the Spirit of the Lord anoints for?
answer: To preach good tidings unto the meek... to bind up the broken-hearted, to proclaim liberty to the captives.

question: What does Isaiah 65:17 promise?
answer: For, behold, I create new heavens and a new earth; and the former things shall not be remembered.

question: How did Jehovah call Jeremiah, per Jeremiah 1:5?
answer: Before I formed thee in the belly I knew thee, and before thou camest forth out of the womb I sanctified thee; I have appointed thee a prophet unto the nations.

question: What was Jeremiah's objection to being called, per Jeremiah 1:6?
answer: Ah, Lord Jehovah! behold, I know not how to speak; for I am a child.

question: What two visions did Jehovah show Jeremiah in chapter 1?
answer: An almond rod (meaning God watches over His word) and a boiling caldron facing north (representing disaster from the north).

question: What does Jeremiah 2:13 call the two evils Israel committed?
answer: They forsook Jehovah, the fountain of living waters, and hewed out cisterns, broken cisterns, that can hold no water.

question: What did Jeremiah buy at God's command as a sign of future restoration, per Jeremiah 32?
answer: A field at Anathoth in the land of Benjamin, even while Jerusalem was besieged.

question: What does Jeremiah 33:3 promise?
answer: Call unto me, and I will answer thee, and will show thee great things, and difficult, which thou knowest not.

question: What did Nebuchadnezzar do to Zedekiah, the last king of Judah, per Jeremiah 52?
answer: He slew Zedekiah's sons before his eyes, put out Zedekiah's eyes, bound him in fetters, and carried him to Babylon.

question: What does Jeremiah 31:34 say about knowing Jehovah under the new covenant?
answer: They shall teach no more every man his neighbor, and every man his brother, saying, Know Jehovah; for they shall all know me, from the least of them unto the greatest.

question: What was the commission God gave Ezekiel, per Ezekiel 3:17?
answer: Son of man, I have made thee a watchman unto the house of Israel: therefore hear the word at my mouth, and give them warning from me.

question: What symbolic acts did Ezekiel perform to prophesy the siege of Jerusalem, per Ezekiel 4?
answer: He lay on his left side 390 days for Israel's sin, and on his right side 40 days for Judah's sin, while a model of Jerusalem besieged was before him.

question: What was Ezekiel's vision of the wheel (Ophanim)?
answer: Each wheel was within a wheel; wherever the spirit went, the wheels went; the spirit of the living creatures was in the wheels.

question: What happened in the valley of dry bones when Ezekiel prophesied to the breath?
answer: The breath came into the slain, and they lived and stood upon their feet, an exceeding great army.

question: What does the vision of the valley of dry bones represent, per Ezekiel 37?
answer: The restoration of the whole house of Israel from their graves (exile) to their own land.

question: What does Ezekiel 36:26 promise about Israel's heart?
answer: A new heart also will I give you, and a new spirit will I put within you; and I will take away the stony heart out of your flesh, and I will give you a heart of flesh.

question: What massive temple does Ezekiel describe in chapters 40-48?
answer: A vision of a future, idealized temple in the restored land of Israel, with a river flowing from it.

question: What was the name of the city at the end of Ezekiel's vision, per Ezekiel 48:35?
answer: Jehovah is there (Jehovah-shammah).

question: What did Daniel pray three times a day, per Daniel 6:10?
answer: He kneeled upon his knees three times a day, and prayed, and gave thanks before his God, as he did aforetime.

question: What edict of Darius led to Daniel being cast into the lions' den?
answer: An edict that for thirty days no one could make petition to any god or man except the king.

question: Who plotted against Daniel to get him thrown into the lions' den?
answer: The presidents and satraps who were jealous of Daniel and found no fault in him except regarding his God.

question: What did Daniel resolve regarding his diet, per Daniel 1:8?
answer: He purposed in his heart that he would not defile himself with the king's food, nor with the wine which he drank.

question: What was the result of Daniel and his friends' diet test, per Daniel 1:15?
answer: Their countenances appeared fairer and fatter in flesh than all the youths that ate the king's food.

question: What did Nebuchadnezzar dream that only Daniel could reveal without being told, per Daniel 2?
answer: The dream of the statue made of different materials — gold, silver, bronze, iron, and iron mixed with clay.

question: What was the stone cut without hands that struck the statue, per Daniel 2?
answer: A stone that struck the feet of the statue, broke it in pieces, and became a great mountain filling the earth — representing God's eternal kingdom.

question: What title was Daniel given after interpreting the dream?
answer: He was made ruler over the province of Babylon and chief of the governors over all the wise men.

question: What feast was Belshazzar holding when the writing appeared on the wall?
answer: A great feast for a thousand of his lords, using the vessels taken from the temple in Jerusalem.

question: What did MENE mean, per Daniel 5:26?
answer: God hath numbered thy kingdom and brought it to an end.

question: What did TEKEL mean, per Daniel 5:27?
answer: Thou art weighed in the balances and art found wanting.

question: What did UPHARSIN (PERES) mean, per Daniel 5:28?
answer: Thy kingdom is divided, and given to the Medes and Persians.

question: What happened to Belshazzar that very night, per Daniel 5:30?
answer: Belshazzar the Chaldean king was slain.

question: What vision of the Ancient of Days does Daniel describe in Daniel 7:9?
answer: His garment was white as snow, and the hair of his head like pure wool; his throne was fiery flames.

question: Who was given dominion and glory and a kingdom, per Daniel 7:13-14?
answer: One like a son of man came with the clouds of heaven to the Ancient of Days and was given an everlasting dominion.

question: What was the abomination of desolation referred to in Daniel 9:27?
answer: The stopping of sacrifice and offerings and the setting up of an abomination that makes desolate in the holy place.

question: What angel appeared to Daniel in Daniel 9:21?
answer: Gabriel, whom he had seen in the vision at the beginning, came to him in swift flight.

question: What did Michael do at the time of the end, per Daniel 12:1?
answer: Michael shall stand up, the great prince who standeth for the children of thy people; it is a time of trouble, such as never was since there was a nation.

question: What does Daniel 12:2-3 say about resurrection?
answer: Many of them that sleep in the dust of the earth shall awake, some to everlasting life, and some to shame and everlasting contempt. They that are wise shall shine as the brightness of the firmament.

question: What does Joel 1:4 describe?
answer: That which the palmerworm hath left hath the locust eaten; and that which the locust hath left hath the cankerworm eaten; and that which the cankerworm hath left hath the caterpillar eaten.

question: What does Joel 2:12-13 call Israel to do?
answer: Turn unto Jehovah with all your heart, and with fasting, and with weeping, and with mourning; rend your heart, and not your garments.

question: What does Joel 3:14 call the great multitude?
answer: Multitudes, multitudes in the valley of decision! for the day of Jehovah is near in the valley of decision.

question: What prophecy does Obadiah give about Edom's pride?
answer: The pride of thy heart hath deceived thee, thou that dwellest in the clefts of the rock, whose habitation is high; that saith in his heart, Who shall bring me down to the ground?

question: What does Micah 4:3 say swords and spears will become?
answer: They shall beat their swords into plowshares, and their spears into pruninghooks; nation shall not lift up sword against nation.

question: What does Micah 7:18-19 say about God casting sins away?
answer: Thou wilt cast all their sins into the depths of the sea; thou delightest in lovingkindness.

question: What does Habakkuk 3:17-18 declare even if the harvest fails?
answer: Yet I will rejoice in Jehovah, I will joy in the God of my salvation.

question: What does Zechariah 2:8 say Israel is to God?
answer: He that toucheth you toucheth the apple of his eye.

question: What does Zechariah 3:2 say the Lord rebuked Satan with?
answer: Jehovah rebuke thee, O Satan; yea, Jehovah that hath chosen Jerusalem rebuke thee: is not this a brand plucked out of the fire?

question: What did Zechariah see in the vision of the flying scroll, per Zechariah 5?
answer: A flying scroll twenty cubits long and ten cubits wide, representing the curse going forth over the land.

question: What does Zechariah 12:10 say about looking on the one they pierced?
answer: They shall look unto me whom they have pierced; and they shall mourn for him, as one mourneth for his only son.

question: What does Zechariah 13:7 say about striking the shepherd?
answer: Smite the shepherd, and the sheep shall be scattered.

question: What does Malachi 1:11 say about God's name among the nations?
answer: From the rising of the sun even unto the going down of the same my name shall be great among the Gentiles.

question: What does Malachi 2:16 say Jehovah hates?
answer: For Jehovah, the God of Israel, saith that he hateth putting away (divorce).

question: How does the Sermon on the Mount begin?
answer: With the Beatitudes (Matthew 5:3-12), beginning: Blessed are the poor in spirit.

question: What does Jesus say about the law in Matthew 5:17?
answer: Think not that I came to destroy the law or the prophets: I came not to destroy, but to fulfil.

question: What does Jesus say about adultery in the heart, per Matthew 5:28?
answer: Every one that looketh on a woman to lust after her hath committed adultery with her already in his heart.

question: What does Jesus say about swearing in Matthew 5:37?
answer: Let your speech be, Yea, yea; Nay, nay: and whatsoever is more than these is of the evil one.

question: What does Jesus say about turning the other cheek in Matthew 5:39?
answer: Whosoever smiteth thee on thy right cheek, turn to him the other also.

question: What does Jesus say about fasting in Matthew 6:17-18?
answer: Anoint thy head, and wash thy face; so that thou be not seen of men to fast, but of thy Father who is in secret.

question: What does Jesus say about laying up treasures in Matthew 6:19-20?
answer: Lay not up for yourselves treasures upon the earth; but lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven.

question: What does Jesus say the eye is in Matthew 6:22?
answer: The lamp of the body: if therefore thine eye be single, thy whole body shall be full of light.

question: What does Jesus say about judging in Matthew 7:1?
answer: Judge not, that ye be not judged.

question: What does Jesus say about the narrow and wide gates in Matthew 7:13-14?
answer: Enter ye in by the narrow gate: for wide is the gate... that leadeth to destruction... and narrow is the gate... that leadeth unto life.

question: What warning does Jesus give about false prophets in Matthew 7:15-16?
answer: Beware of false prophets, who come to you in sheep's clothing, but inwardly are ravening wolves. By their fruits ye shall know them.

question: What does Jesus say about those who say 'Lord, Lord' but he never knew, per Matthew 7:21-23?
answer: Not every one that saith unto me, Lord, Lord, shall enter into the kingdom of heaven; but he that doeth the will of my Father... I never knew you: depart from me.

question: What astonished the crowds after the Sermon on the Mount, per Matthew 7:28-29?
answer: He taught them as one having authority, and not as their scribes.

question: Who did Jesus heal of a withered hand on the Sabbath?
answer: A man in the synagogue with a withered hand (Matthew 12).

question: What unforgivable sin did Jesus warn against in Matthew 12:31?
answer: The blasphemy against the Spirit shall not be forgiven men.

question: What happened to John the Baptist while Jesus was ministering?
answer: He was beheaded in prison at the request of Herodias's daughter; his head was brought on a platter.

question: What did Jesus do when he heard John the Baptist was killed?
answer: He withdrew from there in a boat into a desert place apart.

question: What happened when Peter walked on the water toward Jesus?
answer: When he saw the wind, he was afraid; and beginning to sink, he cried out, Lord, save me.

question: What did Jesus rebuke Peter for when he confessed Jesus as the Christ?
answer: Jesus did not rebuke Peter for the confession; he rebuked Peter later for saying he should not suffer and die.

question: What was the coin found in the fish's mouth for, per Matthew 17:27?
answer: To pay the temple tax (didrachma) for Jesus and Peter.

question: What parable did Jesus tell about the unmerciful servant in Matthew 18?
answer: A king forgave a servant a vast debt; the servant then refused to forgive a smaller debt owed to him and was handed over to torturers.

question: What did the rich young ruler ask Jesus, per Matthew 19?
answer: Teacher, what good thing shall I do, that I may have eternal life?

question: What did Jesus say is easier than a rich man entering the kingdom of God?
answer: It is easier for a camel to go through a needle's eye, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.

question: What did the mother of Zebedee's sons request for them, per Matthew 20?
answer: That they sit one on his right hand and one on his left in his kingdom.

question: What parable describes workers hired at different hours all receiving the same wage?
answer: The parable of the laborers in the vineyard (Matthew 20:1-16).

question: What did Jesus do to the moneychangers in the temple, per Matthew 21?
answer: He cast them out, overturned the tables of the money-changers and the seats of those that sold the doves.

question: What did Jesus say the temple had been made, per Matthew 21:13?
answer: My house shall be called a house of prayer; but ye make it a den of robbers.

question: What question about paying taxes to Caesar did Jesus answer with a coin?
answer: Is it lawful to give tribute unto Caesar, or not? Jesus asked whose image was on the coin; they said Caesar's; he said, Render therefore unto Caesar the things that are Caesar's.

question: What did Jesus say about the resurrection to the Sadducees in Matthew 22?
answer: In the resurrection they neither marry, nor are given in marriage, but are as angels in heaven.

question: What are the woes Jesus pronounced against the Pharisees in Matthew 23 called?
answer: The Seven Woes (seven denunciations beginning with Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites).

question: What does Jesus prophesy about the temple in Matthew 24:2?
answer: There shall not be left here one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.

question: What does Jesus say about the day and hour of his return in Matthew 24:36?
answer: Of that day and hour knoweth no one, not even the angels of heaven, neither the Son, but the Father only.

question: What parable of Matthew 25 describes the Son of Man separating the nations?
answer: The parable of the sheep and the goats — the righteous (sheep) who served the least of these inherit the kingdom; the unrighteous (goats) go to eternal punishment.

question: Who anointed Jesus at Bethany, per Matthew 26?
answer: A woman who poured very precious ointment from an alabaster cruse on his head.

question: What did Pilate's wife warn him about Jesus, per Matthew 27:19?
answer: Have thou nothing to do with that righteous man; for I have suffered many things this day in a dream because of him.

question: What did Pilate do to show he was innocent of Jesus' blood?
answer: He washed his hands before the multitude, saying, I am innocent of the blood of this righteous man.

question: What does Mark 2:27 say about the Sabbath?
answer: The sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath.

question: What family of Jesus tried to take charge of him thinking he was out of his mind, per Mark 3?
answer: His family — his mother and brothers came to take him.

question: What parable did Jesus explain to his disciples privately in Mark 4?
answer: The parable of the sower and its meaning.

question: What was the name of the demoniac in the region of the Gerasenes?
answer: He gave his name as Legion: for we are many.

question: Into what did the demons enter after leaving the Gerasene man?
answer: A great herd of swine (pigs), about two thousand, which rushed down the steep bank into the sea and were drowned.

question: Who was the ruler of the synagogue whose daughter Jesus raised from the dead in Mark 5?
answer: Jairus.

question: What did Jesus say to Jairus's daughter when raising her, per Mark 5:41?
answer: Talitha cumi; which is, being interpreted, Damsel, I say unto thee, Arise.

question: Who touched Jesus' garment and was healed of a flow of blood?
answer: A woman who had an issue of blood for twelve years.

question: How did Jesus respond to the woman who touched his garment?
answer: He said, Daughter, thy faith hath made thee whole; go in peace, and be whole of thy plague.

question: What happened when Jesus tried to teach in Nazareth, per Mark 6?
answer: They were offended at him; he marveled at their unbelief and could do there no mighty work, save that he laid his hands upon a few sick folk and healed them.

question: What does Mark 8:36 ask?
answer: For what doth it profit a man, to gain the whole world, and forfeit his life?

question: What does Mark 9:24 say about unbelief?
answer: Lord, I believe; help thou mine unbelief.

question: What does Mark 10:14-15 say about children and the kingdom?
answer: Suffer the little children to come unto me; forbid them not... Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom of God as a little child, he shall in no wise enter therein.

question: What does Mark 11:22-24 say about faith and prayer?
answer: Have faith in God... What things soever ye desire, when ye pray, believe that ye have received them, and ye shall have them.

question: What does Mark 12:30 state as the great commandment?
answer: Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind, and with all thy strength.

question: What did the poor widow put into the treasury, per Mark 12?
answer: Two mites (a farthing).

question: What did Jesus say about the widow's offering?
answer: This poor widow cast in more than all they that are casting into the treasury... she of her want did cast in all that she had.

question: Who was John the Baptist's father, per Luke 1?
answer: Zacharias (Zechariah), a priest of the division of Abijah.

question: Who was John the Baptist's mother, per Luke 1?
answer: Elisabeth (Elizabeth), a descendant of Aaron.

question: What happened to Zacharias because he doubted the angel's announcement?
answer: He was struck dumb (unable to speak) until the child was born.

question: What was the name of the angel who appeared to Zacharias and Mary?
answer: Gabriel.

question: What does the Benedictus (Luke 1:68-79) celebrate?
answer: Zacharias's song of praise prophesying John's role as forerunner and God's redemption through David's line.

question: What instruction did the angels give the shepherds to find the baby Jesus?
answer: Ye shall find a babe wrapped in swaddling clothes, and lying in a manger.

question: What did Mary do with all these things about Jesus' birth, per Luke 2:19?
answer: Mary kept all these sayings, pondering them in her heart.

question: What was Jesus' response when Mary found him in the temple, per Luke 2:49?
answer: How is it that ye sought me? knew ye not that I must be in my Father's house?

question: What does Luke 2:52 say about Jesus growing up?
answer: Jesus advanced in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and men.

question: What does Luke 4:18-19 say is the Spirit's anointing upon Jesus?
answer: To preach good tidings to the poor... proclaim release to the captives... recovering of sight to the blind... set at liberty them that are bruised... proclaim the acceptable year of the Lord.

question: What did Jesus say after reading Isaiah in the Nazareth synagogue, per Luke 4:21?
answer: Today hath this scripture been fulfilled in your ears.

question: What was the reaction in Nazareth when Jesus implied Gentiles would receive God's grace?
answer: They were all filled with wrath in the synagogue; they rose up and led him to the brow of the hill to throw him down, but he passed through their midst.

question: What is the parable of the good Samaritan in Luke 10 about?
answer: A man beaten by robbers was ignored by a priest and Levite but helped by a Samaritan, illustrating that neighbor means anyone in need.

question: What did Mary choose that Jesus said would not be taken from her, per Luke 10:42?
answer: The good part — sitting at Jesus' feet and listening to his words.

question: What did Martha complain about to Jesus, per Luke 10?
answer: That her sister Mary left her to serve alone; she asked Jesus to tell Mary to help her.

question: What is the parable of the lost coin about in Luke 15?
answer: A woman who lost one of ten silver coins, searched diligently, and rejoiced with her neighbors when she found it.

question: What is the parable of the lost son's older brother's reaction?
answer: He was angry and refused to go in, complaining that he had served his father for years without reward.

question: What parable does Jesus tell about a rich man and Lazarus in Luke 16?
answer: A rich man ignored poor Lazarus at his gate; both died; the rich man was in torment while Lazarus was in Abraham's bosom.

question: What does the rich man ask of Abraham in Luke 16:24?
answer: Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus, that he may dip the tip of his finger in water, and cool my tongue.

question: What was Abraham's answer about someone rising from the dead to warn the rich man's brothers?
answer: If they hear not Moses and the prophets, neither will they be persuaded, if one rise from the dead.

question: What is the parable of the Pharisee and the tax collector in Luke 18 about?
answer: The Pharisee boasted in prayer; the tax collector beat his breast saying, God, be merciful to me a sinner — and went home justified.

question: What does Luke 18:27 say about what is impossible with men?
answer: The things which are impossible with men are possible with God.

question: What did Jesus say to Zacchaeus, per Luke 19:5?
answer: Zacchaeus, make haste, and come down; for today I must abide at thy house.

question: What did Zacchaeus declare after Jesus came to his house?
answer: Behold, Lord, the half of my goods I give to the poor; and if I have wrongfully exacted aught of any man, I restore fourfold.

question: What parable in Luke 19 describes servants given minas (money) to do business?
answer: The parable of the minas (pounds), where the faithful servants were rewarded and the unfaithful servant was rebuked.

question: What women ministered to Jesus from their possessions, per Luke 8?
answer: Mary Magdalene, Joanna wife of Chuza, Susanna, and many others.

question: From whom were the seven demons cast out, per Luke 8:2?
answer: Mary Magdalene.

question: What does John 1:3 say about creation through the Word?
answer: All things were made through him; and without him was not anything made that hath been made.

question: What does John 1:4-5 say about the Word and light?
answer: In him was life; and the life was the light of men. And the light shineth in the darkness; and the darkness apprehended it not.

question: What was John the Baptist's testimony about himself, per John 1:23?
answer: I am the voice of one crying in the wilderness, Make straight the way of the Lord, as said Isaiah the prophet.

question: What did Jesus do to the six stone waterpots at the wedding in Cana?
answer: He had servants fill them with water; the water became wine — his first sign.

question: How many waterpots were at the Cana wedding, and their capacity?
answer: Six stone waterpots, each holding two or three firkins (about 20-30 gallons each).

question: What did Jesus mean when he said the temple would be raised in three days, per John 2?
answer: He spake of the temple of his body (the resurrection).

question: What does John 3:8 compare the Spirit to?
answer: The wind bloweth where it will, and thou hearest the voice thereof, but knowest not whence it cometh and whither it goeth: so is every one that is born of the Spirit.

question: What did Jesus say the Son of Man must be lifted up as, per John 3:14?
answer: As Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, even so must the Son of man be lifted up.

question: Who was the woman at the well in John 4, and how many husbands had she had?
answer: A Samaritan woman at Jacob's well; she had had five husbands.

question: What did the Samaritans say after hearing Jesus for themselves, per John 4:42?
answer: We know that this is indeed the Saviour of the world.

question: What official's son did Jesus heal from a distance in John 4?
answer: A nobleman's son who was at the point of death in Capernaum; Jesus healed him from Cana.

question: What pool did Jesus heal a man at in John 5?
answer: The pool of Bethesda (Bethzatha) in Jerusalem, by the sheep gate.

question: How long had the man at Bethesda been ill, per John 5:5?
answer: Thirty-eight years.

question: What does Jesus say about the works he does in John 5:17?
answer: My Father worketh even until now, and I work.

question: What does Jesus say in John 5:39 about the scriptures?
answer: Ye search the scriptures, because ye think that in them ye have eternal life; and these are they which bear witness of me.

question: What does Jesus say in John 5:46 about Moses?
answer: For Moses wrote of me.

question: What does John 6:44 say about coming to Jesus?
answer: No man can come to me, except the Father that sent me draw him.

question: What does John 6:68 say about the words of eternal life?
answer: Peter said, Lord, to whom shall we go? thou hast the words of eternal life.

question: What question did Jesus ask the woman accused of adultery's accusers?
answer: He that is without sin among you, let him first cast a stone at her.

question: What did Jesus say to the Pharisees about knowing the truth in John 8:32?
answer: Ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free.

question: What does John 8:58 say Jesus said about before Abraham?
answer: Before Abraham was born, I am.

question: Why did Jesus say the man in John 9 was born blind?
answer: That the works of God should be made manifest in him — not because of sin.

question: What does Jesus say in John 10:27-28 about his sheep?
answer: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me: and I give unto them eternal life; and they shall never perish.

question: What did the Jews pick up stones for when Jesus said 'I and the Father are one'?
answer: To stone him for blasphemy, because he, being a man, made himself God.

question: What does John 11:4 say was the purpose of Lazarus's sickness?
answer: This sickness is not unto death, but for the glory of God, that the Son of God may be glorified thereby.

question: What did Jesus say before calling Lazarus from the tomb, per John 11:41-42?
answer: Father, I thank thee that thou heardest me. And I knew that thou hearest me always: but because of the multitude that standeth around I said it, that they may believe.

question: What were Lazarus's burial wrappings, per John 11:44?
answer: He came forth bound hand and foot with grave-clothes; and his face was bound about with a napkin.

question: What was Caiaphas's cynical prophecy, per John 11:50-51?
answer: It is expedient for you that one man die for the people, and that the whole nation perish not — which was a prophecy that Jesus would die for the nation.

question: What did the crowds cry as Jesus entered Jerusalem, per John 12:13?
answer: Hosanna: Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord, even the King of Israel.

question: What does John 12:24 say about a grain of wheat?
answer: Except a grain of wheat fall into the earth and die, it abideth by itself alone; but if it die, it beareth much fruit.

question: Who were the Greeks who wanted to see Jesus in John 12?
answer: Greeks (God-fearing Gentiles) who came up to worship at the feast; they approached Philip.

question: What does John 12:32 say about Jesus being lifted up?
answer: And I, if I be lifted up from the earth, will draw all men unto myself.

question: What is the new command Jesus gives in John 13:35?
answer: By this shall all men know that ye are my disciples, if ye have love one to another.

question: What does Jesus say in John 14:1?
answer: Let not your heart be troubled: believe in God, believe also in me.

question: What does Jesus say in John 14:2-3 about his Father's house?
answer: In my Father's house are many mansions; if it were not so, I would have told you; for I go to prepare a place for you.

question: What does Jesus say in John 14:18?
answer: I will not leave you desolate: I come unto you.

question: What does Jesus say in John 14:27 about his peace?
answer: Peace I leave with you; my peace I give unto you: not as the world giveth, give I unto you. Let not your heart be troubled.

question: What does Jesus say in John 15:7 about abiding and asking?
answer: If ye abide in me, and my words abide in you, ask whatsoever ye will, and it shall be done unto you.

question: What does John 15:16 say about being chosen?
answer: Ye did not choose me, but I chose you, and appointed you, that ye should go and bear fruit.

question: What does Jesus promise in John 15:26 about the Comforter?
answer: When the Comforter is come, whom I will send unto you from the Father, even the Spirit of truth, he will bear witness of me.

question: What does Jesus say in John 16:8 the Holy Spirit will do when he comes?
answer: He will convict the world in respect of sin, and of righteousness, and of judgment.

question: What does Jesus say in John 16:13 about the Spirit of truth?
answer: He shall guide you into all the truth: for he shall not speak from himself; but what things soever he shall hear, these shall he speak.

question: What did Jesus pray for himself in John 17:1?
answer: Father, the hour is come; glorify thy Son, that the Son may glorify thee.

question: What does John 17:3 define as eternal life?
answer: This is life eternal, that they should know thee the only true God, and him whom thou didst send, even Jesus Christ.

question: What does Jesus pray for believers of all time in John 17:20-21?
answer: That they may all be one; even as thou, Father, art in me, and I in thee, that they also may be in us.

question: Who was the other disciple who outran Peter to the tomb, per John 20:4?
answer: The other disciple (the beloved disciple, John) outran Peter and came first to the tomb.

question: What did the other disciple see and believe, per John 20:8?
answer: He saw the linen cloths lying and the napkin folded, and believed.

question: How many times did Jesus ask Peter 'Lovest thou me?' per John 21?
answer: Three times.

question: What did Jesus say to Peter three times in restoration, per John 21?
answer: Feed my lambs / Tend my sheep / Feed my sheep.

question: What does John 21:25 say about all that Jesus did?
answer: If they should be written every one, I suppose that even the world itself would not contain the books that should be written.

question: What does Acts 1:8 say about receiving power?
answer: Ye shall receive power, when the Holy Spirit is come upon you: and ye shall be my witnesses both in Jerusalem, and in all Judaea and Samaria, and unto the uttermost part of the earth.

question: How many disciples were gathered in the upper room before Pentecost, per Acts 1?
answer: About a hundred and twenty.

question: What were the qualifications for replacing Judas, per Acts 1:21-22?
answer: One of the men who accompanied them during all the time the Lord Jesus went in and out among them, from John's baptism until the ascension.

question: How was Matthias chosen to replace Judas?
answer: They prayed and cast lots between Matthias and Barsabbas; the lot fell on Matthias.

question: What prophecy did Peter quote to explain the Pentecost event?
answer: The prophecy of Joel — I will pour out my Spirit upon all flesh.

question: What does Acts 2:38 say Peter commanded?
answer: Repent ye, and be baptized every one of you in the name of Jesus Christ unto the remission of your sins; and ye shall receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.

question: What happened when Peter and John healed the lame man at the temple gate Beautiful?
answer: The man leaped and walked and entered the temple walking, leaping, and praising God; all the people saw him.

question: What was the name of the gate where the lame man was healed, per Acts 3?
answer: The gate of the temple which was called Beautiful.

question: What did Ananias and Sapphira do that caused their deaths in Acts 5?
answer: They sold property but secretly kept part of the price while claiming to give all; they lied to the Holy Spirit.

question: What was Peter's shadow associated with in Acts 5:15?
answer: People brought the sick into the street so that Peter's shadow might fall on some of them and they would be healed.

question: What did the angel tell the apostles who were imprisoned in Acts 5?
answer: Go ye, and stand and speak in the temple to the people all the words of this Life.

question: What does Gamaliel's counsel in Acts 5 say about works of men?
