question: What did Moses say of the laws written on the heart, per Deuteronomy 30:14?
answer: The word is very nigh unto thee, in thy mouth, and in thy heart, that thou mayest do it.

question: How were the Israelites to honor parents, per Leviticus 19:3?
answer: Ye shall fear every man his mother, and his father; and ye shall keep my sabbaths.

question: What was Moses' instruction about firstfruits, per Deuteronomy 26:10?
answer: Now, behold, I have brought the first of the fruit of the ground, which thou, O Jehovah, hast given me.

question: What did the Lord say of vengeance, per Deuteronomy 32:35?
answer: Vengeance is mine, and recompense, at the time when their foot shall slide.

question: Whom did Jehovah commission to lead Israel after Moses, per Joshua 1:2?
answer: Moses my servant is dead; now therefore arise, go over this Jordan, thou, and all this people, unto the land which I do give to them, even to the children of Israel.

question: What did Jehovah promise Joshua about his presence, per Joshua 1:5?
answer: As I was with Moses, so I will be with thee; I will not fail thee, nor forsake thee.

question: What did Jehovah command Joshua about the book of the law, per Joshua 1:8?
answer: This book of the law shall not depart out of thy mouth, but thou shalt meditate thereon day and night.

question: What did Jehovah tell Joshua to be, per Joshua 1:9?
answer: Be strong and of good courage; be not affrighted, neither be thou dismayed: for Jehovah thy God is with thee whithersoever thou goest.

question: Who was the harlot who hid the spies in Jericho, per Joshua 2:1?
answer: Rahab the harlot.

question: What did Rahab tie in her window, per Joshua 2:18?
answer: This line of scarlet thread in the window which thou didst let us down by.

question: How did Israel cross the Jordan, per Joshua 3:17?
answer: The priests that bare the ark of the covenant of Jehovah stood firm on dry ground in the midst of the Jordan, and all Israel passed over on dry ground.

question: How many stones did each tribe take from the Jordan, per Joshua 4:3?
answer: Twelve stones, one for each tribe.

question: What did Joshua see by Jericho, per Joshua 5:13?
answer: A man stood over against him with his sword drawn in his hand.

question: How was Jericho conquered, per Joshua 6:20?
answer: The people shouted with a great shout, and the wall fell down flat, so that the people went up into the city, every man straight before him.

question: How many days did Israel march around Jericho, per Joshua 6:3?
answer: Six days, and on the seventh day they compassed the city seven times.

question: Who took the accursed thing at Ai, per Joshua 7:1?
answer: Achan, the son of Carmi, the son of Zabdi, the son of Zerah, of the tribe of Judah.

question: What did Achan take, per Joshua 7:21?
answer: A goodly Babylonish mantle, and two hundred shekels of silver, and a wedge of gold of fifty shekels weight.

question: What was Achan's punishment, per Joshua 7:25?
answer: All Israel stoned him with stones, and they burned them with fire, and stoned them with stones.

question: How did the Gibeonites deceive Joshua, per Joshua 9:4?
answer: They went and made as if they had been ambassadors, and took old sacks upon their asses, and wine-skins, old and rent and bound up.

question: What miracle happened at the battle of Gibeon, per Joshua 10:13?
answer: The sun stood still, and the moon stayed, until the nation had avenged themselves of their enemies.

question: How long did the sun stay, per Joshua 10:13?
answer: The sun stayed in the midst of heaven, and hasted not to go down about a whole day.

question: What was the inheritance of the tribe of Levi, per Joshua 13:33?
answer: Jehovah, the God of Israel, was their inheritance, as he spake unto them.

question: How old was Caleb when he asked for Hebron, per Joshua 14:7?
answer: Forty years old was I when Moses the servant of Jehovah sent me from Kadesh-barnea to spy out the land.

question: How old was Caleb when he came to claim his inheritance, per Joshua 14:10?
answer: Eighty and five years old.

question: What was Joshua's farewell challenge to Israel, per Joshua 24:15?
answer: Choose you this day whom ye will serve; but as for me and my house, we will serve Jehovah.

question: How old was Joshua when he died, per Joshua 24:29?
answer: A hundred and ten years.

question: What did the children of Israel do after Joshua died, per Judges 2:11?
answer: The children of Israel did that which was evil in the sight of Jehovah, and served the Baalim.

question: Who was the first judge of Israel, per Judges 3:9?
answer: Othniel the son of Kenaz, Caleb's younger brother.

question: How did Ehud kill Eglon, per Judges 3:21-22?
answer: Ehud put forth his left hand, and took the sword from his right thigh, and thrust it into his belly: and the haft also went in after the blade.

question: Who was the woman judge in Israel, per Judges 4:4?
answer: Deborah, a prophetess, the wife of Lappidoth; she judged Israel at that time.

question: Who killed Sisera, per Judges 4:21?
answer: Jael the wife of Heber took a tent-pin, and took a hammer in her hand, and went softly unto him, and smote the pin into his temples.

question: How did Gideon test the call of God, per Judges 6:37?
answer: Behold, I will put a fleece of wool on the threshing-floor; if there be dew on the fleece only, and it be dry upon all the ground, then shall I know.

question: How many men did Gideon take to fight Midian, per Judges 7:7?
answer: By the three hundred men that lapped will I save you, and deliver the Midianites into thy hand.

question: What weapons did Gideon's men carry, per Judges 7:16?
answer: Trumpets in their hands, and empty pitchers, with torches within the pitchers.

question: What did Gideon's men cry, per Judges 7:20?
answer: The sword of Jehovah and of Gideon.

question: How many sons did Gideon have, per Judges 8:30?
answer: Threescore and ten sons (70) of his body begotten; for he had many wives.

question: Who was Abimelech's father, per Judges 9:1?
answer: Jerubbaal (Gideon).

question: How did Abimelech die, per Judges 9:53-54?
answer: A certain woman cast an upper millstone upon Abimelech's head, and brake his skull; then he called hastily unto the young man his armor-bearer, who thrust him through, and he died.

question: Who was Jephthah's mother, per Judges 11:1?
answer: He was the son of a harlot.

question: What rash vow did Jephthah make, per Judges 11:31?
answer: Whatsoever cometh forth from the doors of my house to meet me, when I return in peace from the children of Ammon, it shall be Jehovah's, and I will offer it up for a burnt-offering.

question: Who came to meet Jephthah after his vow, per Judges 11:34?
answer: Behold, his daughter came out to meet him with timbrels and with dances; and she was his only child.

question: What was the password of the Ephraimites, per Judges 12:6?
answer: Shibboleth; and he said, Sibboleth, for he could not frame to pronounce it right.

question: Who was Samson's father, per Judges 13:24?
answer: Manoah of Zorah, of the family of the Danites.

question: What was Samson's vow from birth, per Judges 13:5?
answer: He shall be a Nazirite unto God from the womb; and he shall begin to save Israel out of the hand of the Philistines.

question: What did Samson tear apart with his bare hands, per Judges 14:6?
answer: A young lion, as one would have rent a kid.

question: What was Samson's riddle, per Judges 14:14?
answer: Out of the eater came forth food, and out of the strong came forth sweetness.

question: How many men did Samson kill with the jawbone of an ass, per Judges 15:15?
answer: A thousand men.

question: What was the source of Samson's strength, per Judges 16:17?
answer: There hath not come a razor upon my head; for I have been a Nazirite unto God from my mother's womb.

question: How did Delilah betray Samson, per Judges 16:19?
answer: She made him sleep upon her knees; and she called for a man, and shaved off the seven locks of his head.

question: How did Samson die, per Judges 16:30?
answer: He bowed himself with all his might; and the house fell upon the lords, and upon all the people that were therein.

question: How many Philistines did Samson kill at his death, per Judges 16:30?
answer: So the dead that he slew at his death were more than they that he slew in his life.

question: What was the recurring statement in Judges, per Judges 21:25?
answer: In those days there was no king in Israel: every man did that which was right in his own eyes.

question: Who was Ruth's mother-in-law, per Ruth 1:4?
answer: Naomi, whose two sons Mahlon and Chilion married Ruth and Orpah.

question: What did Ruth say to Naomi, per Ruth 1:16?
answer: Whither thou goest, I will go; and where thou lodgest, I will lodge: thy people shall be my people, and thy God my God.

question: Who was Boaz, per Ruth 2:1?
answer: A mighty man of wealth, of the family of Elimelech, whose name was Boaz.

question: What did Boaz tell his men about Ruth, per Ruth 2:9?
answer: Have I not charged the young men that they shall not touch thee?

question: Who was Boaz's father, per Ruth 4:21?
answer: Salmon (and Salmon begat Boaz).

question: What was the genealogy from Boaz to David, per Ruth 4:21-22?
answer: Salmon begat Boaz, and Boaz begat Obed, and Obed begat Jesse, and Jesse begat David.

question: Who was Hannah's husband, per 1 Samuel 1:1-2?
answer: Elkanah; and she was one of his two wives, the other being Peninnah.

question: What did Hannah vow if Jehovah gave her a son, per 1 Samuel 1:11?
answer: I will give him unto Jehovah all the days of his life, and there shall no razor come upon his head.

question: What did Hannah name her son, per 1 Samuel 1:20?
answer: Samuel, saying, Because I have asked him of Jehovah.

question: What did Eli think when Hannah was praying, per 1 Samuel 1:13?
answer: Eli thought she had been drunken.

question: What did Jehovah call to Samuel as a child, per 1 Samuel 3:10?
answer: Samuel, Samuel. Then Samuel said, Speak; for thy servant heareth.

question: What happened to the ark of God in battle, per 1 Samuel 4:10-11?
answer: Israel was smitten, and they fled every man to his tent; and the ark of God was taken.

question: How did Eli die, per 1 Samuel 4:18?
answer: He fell from off his seat backward by the side of the gate, and his neck brake, and he died: for he was an old man, and heavy.

question: How old was Eli when he died, per 1 Samuel 4:15?
answer: Ninety and eight years old.

question: What happened to Dagon when the ark was placed before him, per 1 Samuel 5:4?
answer: Dagon was fallen upon his face to the ground before the ark of Jehovah; and the head of Dagon and both the palms of his hands lay cut off upon the threshold.

question: How long was the ark in Philistine territory, per 1 Samuel 6:1?
answer: Seven months.

question: Who was the first king of Israel, per 1 Samuel 9:17?
answer: Saul, the son of Kish, of the tribe of Benjamin.

question: How was Saul described physically, per 1 Samuel 9:2?
answer: From his shoulders and upward he was higher than any of the people.

question: What did Samuel pour on Saul's head, per 1 Samuel 10:1?
answer: Samuel took the vial of oil, and poured it upon his head, and kissed him.

question: What did Saul disobey concerning Amalek, per 1 Samuel 15:9?
answer: Saul and the people spared Agag, and the best of the sheep, and of the oxen, and of the fatlings, and the lambs, and all that was good.

question: What did Samuel say about obedience and sacrifice, per 1 Samuel 15:22?
answer: Behold, to obey is better than sacrifice, and to hearken than the fat of rams.

question: Whom did Samuel anoint as the next king, per 1 Samuel 16:13?
answer: David, the son of Jesse the Beth-lehemite.

question: How did Jehovah choose David, per 1 Samuel 16:7?
answer: Jehovah looketh not on the outward appearance, but Jehovah looketh on the heart.

question: How tall was Goliath, per 1 Samuel 17:4?
answer: Six cubits and a span.

question: What weapons did David take to fight Goliath, per 1 Samuel 17:40?
answer: His staff in his hand, and chose him five smooth stones out of the brook, and put them in the shepherd's bag, and his sling was in his hand.

question: What did David say to Goliath, per 1 Samuel 17:45?
answer: Thou comest to me with a sword, and with a spear, and with a javelin: but I come to thee in the name of Jehovah of hosts, the God of the armies of Israel, whom thou hast defied.

question: How did David kill Goliath, per 1 Samuel 17:49?
answer: David put his hand in his bag, and took thence a stone, and slang it, and smote the Philistine in his forehead.

question: Who was David's closest friend, per 1 Samuel 18:1?
answer: Jonathan, the son of Saul; and the soul of Jonathan was knit with the soul of David.

question: What did the women sing of Saul and David, per 1 Samuel 18:7?
answer: Saul hath slain his thousands, and David his ten thousands.

question: How did Michal help David escape, per 1 Samuel 19:13?
answer: Michal took the teraphim, and laid it in the bed, and put a pillow of goats' hair at the head thereof, and covered it with the clothes.

question: How did David spare Saul's life in the cave, per 1 Samuel 24:4?
answer: David arose, and cut off the skirt of Saul's robe privily.

question: What did Saul say when he saw the skirt, per 1 Samuel 24:17?
answer: Thou art more righteous than I; for thou hast rendered unto me good, whereas I have rendered unto thee evil.

question: Who was Nabal's wife who appeased David, per 1 Samuel 25:3?
answer: Abigail, a woman of good understanding, and of a beautiful countenance.

question: What did the witch of Endor see, per 1 Samuel 28:13?
answer: I see a god coming up out of the earth.

question: How did Saul die, per 1 Samuel 31:4?
answer: Saul took his sword, and fell upon it; so Saul died, and his three sons, and his armor-bearer.

question: How did David lament for Saul and Jonathan, per 2 Samuel 1:23?
answer: Saul and Jonathan were lovely and pleasant in their lives, and in their death they were not divided.

question: How long did David reign in Hebron, per 2 Samuel 5:5?
answer: Seven years and six months over Judah.

question: How long did David reign over all Israel, per 2 Samuel 5:5?
answer: Thirty and three years over all Israel and Judah.

question: What city did David capture and make his capital, per 2 Samuel 5:7?
answer: David took the stronghold of Zion; the same is the city of David.

question: Who died for touching the ark, per 2 Samuel 6:7?
answer: Uzzah; and there he died by the ark of God.

question: How did David dance before the ark, per 2 Samuel 6:14?
answer: David danced before Jehovah with all his might; and David was girded with a linen ephod.

question: What did Michal say to David about his dancing, per 2 Samuel 6:20?
answer: How glorious was the king of Israel to-day, who uncovered himself to-day in the eyes of the handmaids of his servants.

question: What promise did Jehovah make to David through Nathan, per 2 Samuel 7:16?
answer: Thy house and thy kingdom shall be made sure for ever before thee: thy throne shall be established for ever.

question: With whom did David commit adultery, per 2 Samuel 11:3?
answer: Bath-sheba the daughter of Eliam, the wife of Uriah the Hittite.

question: Who was the prophet who confronted David, per 2 Samuel 12:7?
answer: Nathan said to David, Thou art the man.

question: What was the parable Nathan told David, per 2 Samuel 12:1-4?
answer: There were two men in one city; the one rich, and the other poor; and the rich man took the poor man's only ewe lamb.

question: What was the punishment for David's sin, per 2 Samuel 12:11?
answer: Behold, I will raise up evil against thee out of thine own house.

question: What son did Bath-sheba bear David, per 2 Samuel 12:24?
answer: Solomon, and Jehovah loved him.

question: Who was Absalom's beautiful sister, per 2 Samuel 13:1?
answer: Tamar.

question: How did Amnon shame Tamar, per 2 Samuel 13:14?
answer: He forced her, and lay with her.

question: How did Absalom kill Amnon, per 2 Samuel 13:29?
answer: The servants of Absalom did unto Amnon as Absalom had commanded.

question: How was Absalom known for his hair, per 2 Samuel 14:26?
answer: He weighed the hair of his head at two hundred shekels, after the king's weight.

question: How did Absalom die, per 2 Samuel 18:9?
answer: His head caught hold of the oak, and he was taken up between the heaven and the earth; and the mule that was under him went on.

question: What did David cry when he heard of Absalom's death, per 2 Samuel 18:33?
answer: O my son Absalom, my son, my son Absalom! would I had died for thee, O Absalom, my son, my son!

question: How was the famine ended in Israel, per 2 Samuel 21:14?
answer: They buried the bones of Saul and Jonathan his son in the country of Benjamin in Zela; and after that God was entreated for the land.

question: What was the cost of David's census, per 2 Samuel 24:15?
answer: Jehovah sent a pestilence upon Israel; and there died of the people from Dan even to Beer-sheba seventy thousand men.

question: Who succeeded David as king, per 1 Kings 1:39?
answer: Solomon; and Zadok the priest took the horn of oil out of the Tent, and anointed Solomon.

question: What did Solomon ask Jehovah for in a dream, per 1 Kings 3:9?
answer: Give thy servant therefore an understanding heart to judge thy people, that I may discern between good and evil.

question: What did God give Solomon besides wisdom, per 1 Kings 3:13?
answer: I have also given thee that which thou hast not asked, both riches and honor, so that there shall not be any among the kings like unto thee, all thy days.

question: How did Solomon judge between the two harlots, per 1 Kings 3:25?
answer: The king said, Divide the living child in two, and give half to the one, and half to the other.

question: How long did Solomon take to build the temple, per 1 Kings 6:38?
answer: Seven years.

question: How many wives and concubines did Solomon have, per 1 Kings 11:3?
answer: Seven hundred wives, princesses, and three hundred concubines.

question: Who was Jeroboam, per 1 Kings 11:26?
answer: An Ephrathite of Zeredah, Solomon's servant; he lifted up his hand against the king.

question: What was Rehoboam's foolish answer to Israel, per 1 Kings 12:11?
answer: Whereas my father did lade you with a heavy yoke, I will add to your yoke: my father chastised you with whips, but I will chastise you with scorpions.

question: Where did Jeroboam set up golden calves, per 1 Kings 12:29?
answer: He set the one in Beth-el, and the other put he in Dan.

question: Who was the prophet who confronted Ahab, per 1 Kings 17:1?
answer: Elijah the Tishbite, who was of the sojourners of Gilead.

question: What did Elijah declare about the rain, per 1 Kings 17:1?
answer: There shall not be dew nor rain these years, but according to my word.

question: How was Elijah fed by the brook Cherith, per 1 Kings 17:6?
answer: The ravens brought him bread and flesh in the morning, and bread and flesh in the evening.

question: What miracle did Elijah perform for the widow at Zarephath, per 1 Kings 17:14?
answer: The jar of meal shall not waste, neither shall the cruse of oil fail, until the day that Jehovah sendeth rain upon the earth.

question: What did Elijah challenge the prophets of Baal to do, per 1 Kings 18:24?
answer: The God that answereth by fire, let him be God.

question: How many prophets of Baal did Elijah challenge at Carmel, per 1 Kings 18:19?
answer: Four hundred and fifty prophets of Baal, and four hundred prophets of the Asherah.

question: How did Elijah hear God after the wind, earthquake, and fire, per 1 Kings 19:12?
answer: After the fire a still small voice.

question: Who was Elijah's successor, per 1 Kings 19:19?
answer: Elisha the son of Shaphat, who was plowing with twelve yoke of oxen.

question: How did Naboth die at Jezebel's hand, per 1 Kings 21:13?
answer: Two men, base fellows, sat before him: and the base fellows bare witness against him, even against Naboth, before the people, saying, Naboth did curse God and the king.

question: How was Elijah taken to heaven, per 2 Kings 2:11?
answer: There appeared a chariot of fire, and horses of fire, which parted them both asunder; and Elijah went up by a whirlwind into heaven.

question: What did Elisha ask of Elijah before he was taken, per 2 Kings 2:9?
answer: I pray thee, let a double portion of thy spirit be upon me.

question: What did Elisha throw into the bitter waters at Jericho, per 2 Kings 2:21?
answer: Salt; and he said, Thus saith Jehovah, I have healed these waters.

question: What did the bears do to the youths who mocked Elisha, per 2 Kings 2:24?
answer: There came forth two she-bears out of the wood, and tare forty and two lads of them.

question: Who was the leper Elisha healed, per 2 Kings 5:1?
answer: Naaman, captain of the host of the king of Syria; he was a great man with his master.

question: How was Naaman healed, per 2 Kings 5:14?
answer: He went down, and dipped himself seven times in the Jordan, according to the saying of the man of God.

question: Who was Elisha's servant who took bribes, per 2 Kings 5:25?
answer: Gehazi, who was struck with leprosy.

question: How did Elisha's bones revive a dead man, per 2 Kings 13:21?
answer: As soon as the man touched the bones of Elisha, he revived, and stood up on his feet.

question: Who was Hezekiah, per 2 Kings 18:1?
answer: Hezekiah the son of Ahaz king of Judah, who began to reign in the third year of Hoshea son of Elah king of Israel.

question: What miracle was given for Hezekiah's life, per 2 Kings 20:11?
answer: He brought the shadow ten steps backward, by which it had gone down on the dial of Ahaz.

question: How many years did Jehovah add to Hezekiah's life, per 2 Kings 20:6?
answer: I will add unto thy days fifteen years.

question: What did the prophet Isaiah say to Hezekiah, per 2 Kings 20:1?
answer: Set thy house in order; for thou shalt die, and not live.

question: Who was the youngest king of Judah, per 2 Kings 22:1?
answer: Josiah, eight years old when he began to reign.

question: What was found in the temple in Josiah's reign, per 2 Kings 22:8?
answer: I have found the book of the law in the house of Jehovah.

question: How did Josiah die, per 2 Kings 23:29?
answer: Pharaoh-necoh king of Egypt went up against the king of Assyria; and king Josiah went against him; and Pharaoh-necoh slew him at Megiddo.

question: Who carried Judah away to Babylon, per 2 Kings 24:14?
answer: Nebuchadnezzar carried away all Jerusalem, and all the princes, and all the mighty men of valor, even ten thousand captives.

question: How was Zedekiah punished, per 2 Kings 25:7?
answer: They slew the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes, and put out the eyes of Zedekiah, and bound him in fetters of brass, and carried him to Babylon.

question: What was Jabez's prayer, per 1 Chronicles 4:10?
answer: Oh that thou wouldest bless me indeed, and enlarge my border, and that thy hand might be with me, and that thou wouldest keep me from evil, that it be not to my sorrow!

question: What did the high priest say of God's keeping faith, per 1 Chronicles 16:34?
answer: O give thanks unto Jehovah; for he is good; for his lovingkindness endureth for ever.

question: What did David teach about a willing offering, per 1 Chronicles 29:14?
answer: All things come of thee, and of thine own have we given thee.

question: What did Solomon's prayer of dedication open with, per 2 Chronicles 6:14?
answer: O Jehovah, the God of Israel, there is no God like thee, in heaven, or on earth.

question: What did Jehovah say of healing the land, per 2 Chronicles 7:14?
answer: If my people, who are called by my name, shall humble themselves, and pray, and seek my face, and turn from their wicked ways; then will I hear from heaven, and will forgive their sin, and will heal their land.

question: What did Jehoshaphat do before the battle, per 2 Chronicles 20:12?
answer: O our God, wilt thou not judge them? for we have no might against this great company that cometh against us; neither know we what to do: but our eyes are upon thee.

question: What did Jehoshaphat appoint to lead the army, per 2 Chronicles 20:21?
answer: He appointed them that should sing unto Jehovah, and that should praise the beauty of holiness.

question: Who was the queen who killed her grandchildren, per 2 Chronicles 22:10?
answer: Athaliah, the mother of Ahaziah; when she saw that her son was dead, she arose and destroyed all the seed royal of the house of Judah.

question: How was Joash hidden, per 2 Chronicles 22:11?
answer: Jehoshabeath the daughter of the king took Joash the son of Ahaziah, and stole him away from among the king's sons that were slain.

question: How long was Joash hidden, per 2 Chronicles 22:12?
answer: He was with them hid in the house of God six years.

question: What was the charge to Cyrus to do, per 2 Chronicles 36:23?
answer: All the kingdoms of the earth hath Jehovah, the God of heaven, given me; and he hath charged me to build him a house in Jerusalem.

question: What did Cyrus proclaim regarding the temple, per Ezra 1:2?
answer: Jehovah, the God of heaven, hath given me all the kingdoms of the earth; and he hath charged me to build him a house in Jerusalem.

question: Who was the leader of the first return from exile, per Ezra 2:2?
answer: Zerubbabel, Jeshua, Nehemiah, Seraiah, Reelaiah, Mordecai, Bilshan, Mispar, Bigvai, Rehum, and Baanah.

question: How long was the rebuilding of the temple delayed, per Ezra 4:24?
answer: It ceased unto the second year of the reign of Darius king of Persia.

question: Who was Ezra, per Ezra 7:6?
answer: This Ezra went up from Babylon: and he was a ready scribe in the law of Moses.

question: What did Ezra prepare his heart to do, per Ezra 7:10?
answer: To seek the law of Jehovah, and to do it, and to teach in Israel statutes and ordinances.

question: What was Nehemiah's role to the king, per Nehemiah 1:11?
answer: I was cup-bearer to the king.

question: How long did it take to rebuild the wall, per Nehemiah 6:15?
answer: Fifty and two days; the wall was finished.

question: What did the people stand to listen to, per Nehemiah 8:5?
answer: Ezra opened the book in the sight of all the people; and when he opened it, all the people stood up.

question: What did Nehemiah say of the joy of Jehovah, per Nehemiah 8:10?
answer: The joy of Jehovah is your strength.

question: Who was the king Esther married, per Esther 2:17?
answer: King Ahasuerus.

question: Who was Esther's cousin who raised her, per Esther 2:7?
answer: Mordecai; and she had neither father nor mother, and the maiden was fair and beautiful.

question: Who was the chief enemy of the Jews in Esther's day, per Esther 3:1?
answer: Haman the son of Hammedatha the Agagite.

question: What did Esther proclaim before approaching the king, per Esther 4:16?
answer: Fast ye for me, and neither eat nor drink three days, night or day; I also and my maidens will fast in like manner; and so will I go in unto the king, which is not according to the law: and if I perish, I perish.

question: What was Mordecai's reward, per Esther 6:11?
answer: Then took Haman the apparel and the horse, and arrayed Mordecai, and caused him to ride through the street of the city.

question: How was Haman executed, per Esther 7:10?
answer: They hanged Haman on the gallows that he had prepared for Mordecai.

question: What feast commemorates the deliverance of the Jews, per Esther 9:26?
answer: Wherefore they called these days Purim, after the name of Pur.

question: How was the river Jabbok crossed in another battle, per Numbers 21:24?
answer: Israel smote him with the edge of the sword, and possessed his land from the Arnon unto the Jabbok.

question: What did the Lord say to Solomon at Gibeon, per 1 Kings 3:5?
answer: In Gibeon Jehovah appeared to Solomon in a dream by night: and God said, Ask what I shall give thee.

question: What did Adonijah do that displeased David, per 1 Kings 1:5?
answer: Adonijah the son of Haggith exalted himself, saying, I will be king.

question: What did Solomon command his men to do with Joab, per 1 Kings 2:29?
answer: Solomon sent Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, saying, Go, fall upon him.

question: What did Hiram of Tyre supply for the temple, per 1 Kings 5:8?
answer: Cedar timber and fir timber for the building.

question: What was the height of the cherubim in Solomon's temple, per 1 Kings 6:23?
answer: In the oracle he made two cherubim of olive-wood, each ten cubits high.

question: What was the queen of Sheba's reaction to Solomon's wisdom, per 1 Kings 10:5?
answer: There was no more spirit in her.

question: What was the cost of weaponry that Solomon collected, per 1 Kings 10:14?
answer: The weight of gold that came to Solomon in one year was six hundred threescore and six talents of gold.

question: What sin caused Solomon's downfall, per 1 Kings 11:4?
answer: His wives turned away his heart after other gods.

question: What promise did Jehovah keep about Jeroboam's lineage, per 1 Kings 14:10?
answer: I will bring evil upon the house of Jeroboam, and will cut off from Jeroboam every man-child.

question: What was the worst king of Israel, per 1 Kings 16:30?
answer: Ahab the son of Omri did that which was evil in the sight of Jehovah above all that were before him.

question: How did Elisha multiply oil for the widow, per 2 Kings 4:6?
answer: When the vessels were full, she said unto her son, Bring me yet a vessel. And he said unto her, There is not a vessel more. And the oil stayed.

question: How did Elisha raise the Shunammite's son, per 2 Kings 4:34?
answer: He went up, and lay upon the child, and put his mouth upon his mouth, and his eyes upon his eyes, and his hands upon his hands.

question: How did the iron axe head float, per 2 Kings 6:6?
answer: He cut down a stick, and cast it in thither, and made the iron to swim.

question: What did Elisha pray for the king of Syria's army, per 2 Kings 6:18?
answer: Smite this people, I pray thee, with blindness. And he smote them with blindness according to the word of Elisha.

question: What did the four lepers find at the Syrian camp, per 2 Kings 7:8?
answer: They went into one tent, and did eat and drink, and carried thence silver, and gold, and raiment, and went and hid it.

question: What did Hezekiah do with Sennacherib's letter, per 2 Kings 19:14?
answer: Hezekiah received the letter from the hand of the messengers, and read it; and Hezekiah went up unto the house of Jehovah, and spread it before Jehovah.

question: How many were slain in the camp of the Assyrians, per 2 Kings 19:35?
answer: The angel of Jehovah went forth, and smote in the camp of the Assyrians a hundred fourscore and five thousand (185,000).

question: How did Sennacherib die, per 2 Kings 19:37?
answer: Adrammelech and Sharezer his sons smote him with the sword: and they escaped into the land of Ararat.

question: What was the longest reign of any king of Judah, per 2 Kings 21:1?
answer: Manasseh, fifty and five years.

question: How many years did the temple wall stand before destruction, per 2 Kings 25:1?
answer: In the ninth year of his reign, in the tenth month, in the tenth day of the month, the city was besieged.

question: What did Mordecai say to Esther in her time of decision, per Esther 4:14?
answer: Who knoweth whether thou art not come to the kingdom for such a time as this?

question: What did Nehemiah pray after hearing of Jerusalem's plight, per Nehemiah 1:5?
answer: I beseech thee, O Jehovah, the God of heaven, the great and terrible God.

question: What were the gates Nehemiah examined, per Nehemiah 2:13?
answer: I went out by night by the valley gate, even toward the dragon's well, and to the dung gate.

question: What did the people answer Nehemiah's call to rebuild, per Nehemiah 2:18?
answer: Let us rise up and build. So they strengthened their hands for the good work.

question: Who mocked Nehemiah's work, per Nehemiah 4:1-3?
answer: Sanballat the Horonite, and Tobiah the Ammonite, and Geshem the Arabian.

question: How did the people work on the wall, per Nehemiah 4:17?
answer: Every one with one of his hands wrought in the work, and with the other held his weapon.

question: What was Ezra's confession, per Ezra 9:6?
answer: O my God, I am ashamed and blush to lift up my face to thee, my God; for our iniquities are increased over our head.

question: How was Job described in Job 1:1?
answer: He was perfect and upright, and one that feared God, and turned away from evil.

question: How many sons and daughters did Job have, per Job 1:2?
answer: Seven sons and three daughters.

question: What was Job's wealth, per Job 1:3?
answer: Seven thousand sheep, and three thousand camels, and five hundred yoke of oxen, and five hundred she-asses, and a very great household.

question: What did Satan say of Job to Jehovah, per Job 1:9?
answer: Doth Job fear God for nought?

question: What did Job say when he heard of his loss, per Job 1:21?
answer: Naked came I out of my mother's womb, and naked shall I return thither: Jehovah gave, and Jehovah hath taken away; blessed be the name of Jehovah.

question: What disease did Satan smite Job with, per Job 2:7?
answer: Sore boils from the sole of his foot unto his crown.

question: What did Job's wife say to him, per Job 2:9?
answer: Dost thou still hold fast thine integrity? renounce God, and die.

question: Who were Job's three friends, per Job 2:11?
answer: Eliphaz the Temanite, Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite.

question: How long did Job's friends sit silently with him, per Job 2:13?
answer: Seven days and seven nights, and none spake a word unto him.

question: What did Job declare about his Redeemer, per Job 19:25?
answer: I know that my Redeemer liveth, and at last he will stand up upon the earth.

question: How did Job describe wisdom's source, per Job 28:28?
answer: Behold, the fear of the Lord, that is wisdom; and to depart from evil is understanding.

question: What did Jehovah ask Job out of the whirlwind, per Job 38:4?
answer: Where wast thou when I laid the foundations of the earth? declare, if thou hast understanding.

question: What creature did Jehovah describe in Job 40:15?
answer: Behold now, behemoth, which I made as well as thee.

question: What sea creature did Jehovah describe in Job 41:1?
answer: Canst thou draw out leviathan with a fishhook? or press down his tongue with a cord?

question: What did Job confess to Jehovah, per Job 42:5-6?
answer: I had heard of thee by the hearing of the ear; but now mine eye seeth thee: wherefore I abhor myself, and repent in dust and ashes.

question: How was Job restored, per Job 42:10?
answer: Jehovah turned the captivity of Job, when he prayed for his friends: and Jehovah gave Job twice as much as he had before.

question: How long did Job live after his trial, per Job 42:16?
answer: After this Job lived a hundred and forty years, and saw his sons, and his sons' sons, even four generations.

question: How does Psalm 1 begin?
answer: Blessed is the man that walketh not in the counsel of the wicked, nor standeth in the way of sinners, nor sitteth in the seat of scoffers.

question: What is the man's delight in Psalm 1:2?
answer: His delight is in the law of Jehovah; and on his law doth he meditate day and night.

question: What is the tree planted by streams in Psalm 1:3?
answer: He shall be like a tree planted by the streams of water, that bringeth forth its fruit in its season.

question: What did Jehovah say to His Anointed, per Psalm 2:7?
answer: Thou art my son; this day have I begotten thee.

question: How does the famous shepherd psalm begin, per Psalm 23:1?
answer: Jehovah is my shepherd; I shall not want.

question: What does the shepherd lead beside, per Psalm 23:2?
answer: He maketh me to lie down in green pastures; he leadeth me beside still waters.

question: What does the shepherd restore, per Psalm 23:3?
answer: He restoreth my soul: he guideth me in the paths of righteousness for his name's sake.

question: What is in the valley of the shadow, per Psalm 23:4?
answer: Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil; for thou art with me.

question: What anoints the head in Psalm 23:5?
answer: Thou anointest my head with oil; my cup runneth over.

question: What follows all the days, per Psalm 23:6?
answer: Surely goodness and lovingkindness shall follow me all the days of my life; and I shall dwell in the house of Jehovah for ever.

question: What is the earth's, per Psalm 24:1?
answer: The earth is Jehovah's, and the fulness thereof; the world, and they that dwell therein.

question: Who shall ascend the hill of Jehovah, per Psalm 24:3-4?
answer: He that hath clean hands, and a pure heart; who hath not lifted up his soul unto falsehood, and hath not sworn deceitfully.

question: What did David say of waiting upon Jehovah, per Psalm 27:14?
answer: Wait for Jehovah: be strong, and let thy heart take courage; yea, wait thou for Jehovah.

question: What is forgiven in Psalm 32:1?
answer: Blessed is he whose transgression is forgiven, whose sin is covered.

question: What is the great call to taste, per Psalm 34:8?
answer: Oh taste and see that Jehovah is good: blessed is the man that taketh refuge in him.

question: What does Jehovah deliver from, per Psalm 34:7?
answer: The angel of Jehovah encampeth round about them that fear him, and delivereth them.

question: What is the heart's longing in Psalm 42:1?
answer: As the hart panteth after the water brooks, so panteth my soul after thee, O God.

question: What is the refuge in Psalm 46:1?
answer: God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.

question: What did the psalmist say of being still, per Psalm 46:10?
answer: Be still, and know that I am God.

question: What does David ask God to create, per Psalm 51:10?
answer: Create in me a clean heart, O God; and renew a right spirit within me.

question: What did David ask not to be cast away, per Psalm 51:11?
answer: Cast me not away from thy presence; and take not thy holy Spirit from me.

question: What is the broken sacrifice God will not despise, per Psalm 51:17?
answer: The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit: a broken and contrite heart, O God, thou wilt not despise.

question: Whom does the psalmist say is the fool, per Psalm 53:1?
answer: The fool hath said in his heart, There is no God.

question: What did David say of casting his burden, per Psalm 55:22?
answer: Cast thy burden upon Jehovah, and he will sustain thee.

question: What is the beautiful place, per Psalm 84:1?
answer: How amiable are thy tabernacles, O Jehovah of hosts!

question: What did the psalmist say of one day in His courts, per Psalm 84:10?
answer: For a day in thy courts is better than a thousand.

question: What is the eternal dwelling place, per Psalm 90:1?
answer: Lord, thou hast been our dwelling-place in all generations.

question: How long is a thousand years to God, per Psalm 90:4?
answer: For a thousand years in thy sight are but as yesterday when it is past, and as a watch in the night.

question: How many are our days, per Psalm 90:10?
answer: The days of our years are threescore years and ten, or even by reason of strength fourscore years.

question: What is the secret place, per Psalm 91:1?
answer: He that dwelleth in the secret place of the Most High shall abide under the shadow of the Almighty.

question: What does Psalm 91:11 say about angels?
answer: He will give his angels charge over thee, to keep thee in all thy ways.

question: What is the call to enter, per Psalm 100:4?
answer: Enter into his gates with thanksgiving, and into his courts with praise.

question: How does Psalm 103 begin?
answer: Bless Jehovah, O my soul; and all that is within me, bless his holy name.

question: What does the Lord remove our sins, per Psalm 103:12?
answer: As far as the east is from the west, so far hath he removed our transgressions from us.

question: What is the great refuge in Psalm 121:1-2?
answer: I will lift up mine eyes unto the mountains: From whence shall my help come? My help cometh from Jehovah, who made heaven and earth.

question: What does the Lord do for the slumbering, per Psalm 121:4?
answer: Behold, he that keepeth Israel will neither slumber nor sleep.

question: What is the call to unity, per Psalm 133:1?
answer: Behold, how good and how pleasant it is for brethren to dwell together in unity!

question: What is the praise of God's wonderful made-ness, per Psalm 139:14?
answer: I will give thanks unto thee; for I am fearfully and wonderfully made.

question: What does the psalmist ask of searching, per Psalm 139:23?
answer: Search me, O God, and know my heart: try me, and know my thoughts.

question: What is the lamp to my feet, per Psalm 119:105?
answer: Thy word is a lamp unto my feet, and light unto my path.

question: How does Psalm 150 close the Psalter, per Psalm 150:6?
answer: Let everything that hath breath praise Jehovah. Praise ye Jehovah.

question: What is the beginning of knowledge, per Proverbs 1:7?
answer: The fear of Jehovah is the beginning of knowledge; but the foolish despise wisdom and instruction.

question: What did Solomon say of trusting in Jehovah, per Proverbs 3:5?
answer: Trust in Jehovah with all thy heart, and lean not upon thine own understanding.

question: What is the way to direct paths, per Proverbs 3:6?
answer: In all thy ways acknowledge him, and he will direct thy paths.

question: What did Solomon say about firstfruits, per Proverbs 3:9?
answer: Honor Jehovah with thy substance, and with the first-fruits of all thine increase.

question: What does Jehovah love to chasten, per Proverbs 3:12?
answer: Whom Jehovah loveth he reproveth; even as a father the son in whom he delighteth.

question: What is the principal thing, per Proverbs 4:7?
answer: Wisdom is the principal thing; therefore get wisdom; yea, with all thy getting get understanding.

question: What is the issue of life, per Proverbs 4:23?
answer: Keep thy heart with all diligence; for out of it are the issues of life.

question: What seven things does the Lord hate, per Proverbs 6:16-19?
answer: Haughty eyes, a lying tongue, hands that shed innocent blood, a heart that deviseth wicked purposes, feet swift in running to mischief, a false witness that uttereth lies, and he that soweth discord among brethren.

question: How does a soft answer affect wrath, per Proverbs 15:1?
answer: A soft answer turneth away wrath; but a grievous word stirreth up anger.

question: What goes before destruction, per Proverbs 16:18?
answer: Pride goeth before destruction, and a haughty spirit before a fall.

question: What is better than gold, per Proverbs 16:16?
answer: How much better is it to get wisdom than gold! yea, to get understanding is rather to be chosen than silver.

question: What is the friend that loveth, per Proverbs 17:17?
answer: A friend loveth at all times; and a brother is born for adversity.

question: What is the strong tower, per Proverbs 18:10?
answer: The name of Jehovah is a strong tower; the righteous runneth into it, and is safe.

question: What is the heart of man, per Proverbs 19:21?
answer: There are many devices in a man's heart; but the counsel of Jehovah, that shall stand.

question: What does training a child accomplish, per Proverbs 22:6?

question: What does the Lord weigh, per Proverbs 21:2?

question: What is iron sharpening iron, per Proverbs 27:17?
answer: Iron sharpeneth iron; so a man sharpeneth the countenance of his friend.

question: What are precious in Proverbs 25:11?
answer: A word fitly spoken is like apples of gold in network of silver.

question: What did Agur ask in Proverbs 30:8?
answer: Remove far from me falsehood and lies; give me neither poverty nor riches; feed me with the food that is needful for me.

question: What is the worthy woman in Proverbs 31:10?
answer: A worthy woman who can find? for her price is far above rubies.

question: What is the woman who shall be praised, per Proverbs 31:30?
answer: Grace is deceitful, and beauty is vain; but a woman that feareth Jehovah, she shall be praised.

question: What is the conclusion of the Preacher, per Ecclesiastes 12:13?
answer: Fear God, and keep his commandments; for this is the whole duty of man.

question: What is to everything a season, per Ecclesiastes 3:1?
answer: For everything there is a season, and a time for every purpose under heaven.

question: What is vanity according to the Preacher, per Ecclesiastes 1:2?
answer: Vanity of vanities, saith the Preacher; vanity of vanities, all is vanity.

question: What is wiser than the strong, per Ecclesiastes 9:11?
answer: The race is not to the swift, nor the battle to the strong.

question: What is the blessing of two together, per Ecclesiastes 4:9?
answer: Two are better than one; because they have a good reward for their labor.

question: What is the threefold cord, per Ecclesiastes 4:12?
answer: A threefold cord is not quickly broken.

question: What is the loving call of the bride, per Song of Solomon 2:4?
answer: He brought me to the banqueting-house, and his banner over me was love.

question: What is the seal upon the heart, per Song of Solomon 8:6?
answer: Set me as a seal upon thy heart, as a seal upon thine arm: for love is strong as death.

question: How does Psalm 19 describe creation, per Psalm 19:1?
answer: The heavens declare the glory of God; and the firmament showeth his handiwork.

question: What is sweeter than honey, per Psalm 19:10?
answer: More to be desired are they than gold, yea, than much fine gold; sweeter also than honey and the droppings of the honeycomb.

question: What does the psalmist say of God's law, per Psalm 19:7?
answer: The law of Jehovah is perfect, restoring the soul: the testimony of Jehovah is sure, making wise the simple.

question: What is the prayer of acceptance, per Psalm 19:14?

question: What does the heart speak in Psalm 14:1?
answer: The fool hath said in his heart, There is no God.

question: What does Psalm 22:1 cry?
answer: My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?

question: What is divided in Psalm 22:18?
answer: They part my garments among them, and upon my vesture do they cast lots.

question: What is the throne of Jehovah, per Psalm 11:4?
answer: Jehovah is in his holy temple; Jehovah, his throne is in heaven.

question: What does the psalmist describe of God's hand, per Psalm 8:3?
answer: When I consider thy heavens, the work of thy fingers, the moon and the stars, which thou hast ordained.

question: How does the psalmist describe man, per Psalm 8:4-5?
answer: What is man, that thou art mindful of him? and the son of man, that thou visitest him? For thou hast made him but little lower than God.

question: What does the psalmist say of fear and worship, per Psalm 9:10?
answer: They that know thy name will put their trust in thee; for thou, Jehovah, hast not forsaken them that seek thee.

question: What does the psalmist say of trust, per Psalm 20:7?
answer: Some trust in chariots, and some in horses; but we will make mention of the name of Jehovah our God.

question: How does Psalm 37:4 describe delight?
answer: Delight thyself also in Jehovah; and he will give thee the desires of thy heart.

question: What does Psalm 37:5 say of committing the way?
answer: Commit thy way unto Jehovah; trust also in him, and he will bring it to pass.

question: What does Psalm 37:7 say of resting?
answer: Rest in Jehovah, and wait patiently for him.

question: What is the prayer of Psalm 40:1?
answer: I waited patiently for Jehovah; and he inclined unto me, and heard my cry.

question: What did the Psalmist say of the new song, per Psalm 40:3?
answer: He hath put a new song in my mouth, even praise unto our God.

question: What does Psalm 56:3 say about fear?
answer: What time I am afraid, I will put my trust in thee.

question: What is the path of life, per Psalm 16:11?
answer: Thou wilt show me the path of life: in thy presence is fulness of joy; in thy right hand there are pleasures for evermore.

question: What is God my exceeding joy, per Psalm 43:4?
answer: Then will I go unto the altar of God, unto God my exceeding joy.

question: What is the command of Psalm 37:1?
answer: Fret not thyself because of evil-doers, neither be thou envious against them that work unrighteousness.

question: What is the inheritance of the meek, per Psalm 37:11?
answer: The meek shall inherit the land, and shall delight themselves in the abundance of peace.

question: What does Psalm 30:5 say of weeping and joy?
answer: Weeping may tarry for the night, but joy cometh in the morning.

question: What did the Psalmist make in Psalm 51:3?
answer: For I know my transgressions; and my sin is ever before me.

question: What is forgiven and not, per Psalm 32:5?
answer: I acknowledged my sin unto thee, and mine iniquity did I not hide; thou forgavest the iniquity of my sin.

question: What is the gospel of Psalm 32:8?
answer: I will instruct thee and teach thee in the way which thou shalt go: I will counsel thee with mine eye upon thee.

question: What is the joy of forgiveness, per Psalm 32:11?
answer: Be glad in Jehovah, and rejoice, ye righteous; and shout for joy, all ye that are upright in heart.

question: What is in the Lord's hand, per Psalm 31:5?
answer: Into thy hand I commend my spirit: thou hast redeemed me, O Jehovah, thou God of truth.

question: What is the nation God blesses, per Psalm 33:12?
answer: Blessed is the nation whose God is Jehovah.

question: What does Psalm 27:1 say about light?
answer: Jehovah is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear?

question: What is the one thing the psalmist desires, per Psalm 27:4?
answer: One thing have I asked of Jehovah, that will I seek after: that I may dwell in the house of Jehovah all the days of my life.

question: What is the rejoicing in Psalm 118:24?
answer: This is the day which Jehovah hath made; we will rejoice and be glad in it.

question: What is the stone the builders rejected, per Psalm 118:22?
answer: The stone which the builders rejected is become the head of the corner.

question: What is the great call of Psalm 117:1?
answer: O praise Jehovah, all ye nations; laud him, all ye peoples.

question: What does the psalmist hide in his heart, per Psalm 119:11?
answer: Thy word have I laid up in my heart, that I might not sin against thee.

question: What does Psalm 119:9 say about cleansing?
answer: Wherewithal shall a young man cleanse his way? By taking heed thereto according to thy word.

question: What is in Psalm 23 about goodness, per Psalm 23:6?
answer: Surely goodness and lovingkindness shall follow me all the days of my life.

question: What does Solomon say of slothfulness, per Proverbs 6:6?
answer: Go to the ant, thou sluggard; consider her ways, and be wise.

question: What is the warning of the strange woman, per Proverbs 7:21?
answer: With her much fair speech she causeth him to yield; with the flattering of her lips she forceth him along.

question: What is wisdom calling at the gates, per Proverbs 8:1?
answer: Doth not wisdom cry, and understanding put forth her voice?

question: What did wisdom build, per Proverbs 9:1?
answer: Wisdom hath builded her house; she hath hewn out her seven pillars.

question: What is the beginning of wisdom, per Proverbs 9:10?
answer: The fear of Jehovah is the beginning of wisdom; and the knowledge of the Holy One is understanding.

question: What does a wise son make, per Proverbs 10:1?
answer: A wise son maketh a glad father; but a foolish son is the heaviness of his mother.

question: What does the slack hand do, per Proverbs 10:4?
answer: He becometh poor that worketh with a slack hand; but the hand of the diligent maketh rich.

question: What does righteousness deliver, per Proverbs 11:4?
answer: Riches profit not in the day of wrath; but righteousness delivereth from death.

question: What does Proverbs 11:14 say of counsel?
answer: Where no wise guidance is, the people falleth; but in the multitude of counsellors there is safety.

question: What does Proverbs 12:18 say of the tongue?
answer: There is that speaketh rashly like the piercings of a sword; but the tongue of the wise is health.

question: What is Proverbs 13:24 about discipline?
answer: He that spareth his rod hateth his son; but he that loveth him chasteneth him betimes.

question: What does Proverbs 14:12 warn about?
answer: There is a way which seemeth right unto a man; but the end thereof are the ways of death.

question: What is in Proverbs 15:13 about the heart?
answer: A glad heart maketh a cheerful countenance; but by sorrow of heart the spirit is broken.

question: What does Proverbs 15:33 say of fear?
answer: The fear of Jehovah is the instruction of wisdom; and before honor goeth humility.

question: What is in Proverbs 16:9 about the heart?
answer: A man's heart deviseth his way; but Jehovah directeth his steps.

question: What does Proverbs 17:22 say of merry hearts?
answer: A cheerful heart is a good medicine; but a broken spirit drieth up the bones.

question: What does Proverbs 18:21 say of the tongue?
answer: Death and life are in the power of the tongue; and they that love it shall eat the fruit thereof.

question: What does Proverbs 18:22 say of finding a wife?
answer: Whoso findeth a wife findeth a good thing, and obtaineth favor of Jehovah.

question: What is in Proverbs 19:17 about the poor?
answer: He that hath pity upon the poor lendeth unto Jehovah, and his good deed will he pay him again.

question: What is in Proverbs 22:1 about a name?
answer: A good name is rather to be chosen than great riches, and loving favor rather than silver and gold.

question: What does Proverbs 23:7 say about the heart?
answer: For as he thinketh within himself, so is he.

question: What is in Proverbs 25:21 about enemies?
answer: If thine enemy be hungry, give him bread to eat; and if he be thirsty, give him water to drink.

question: What is the answer to a fool, per Proverbs 26:4-5?
answer: Answer not a fool according to his folly, lest thou also be like unto him. Answer a fool according to his folly, lest he be wise in his own conceit.

question: What does Proverbs 27:1 say of tomorrow?
answer: Boast not thyself of tomorrow; for thou knowest not what a day may bring forth.

question: What is in Proverbs 27:6 about wounds?
answer: Faithful are the wounds of a friend; but the kisses of an enemy are profuse.

question: What does Proverbs 28:1 say of the wicked?
answer: The wicked flee when no man pursueth; but the righteous are bold as a lion.

question: What is in Proverbs 29:11 about a fool?
answer: A fool uttereth all his anger; but a wise man keepeth it back and stilleth it.

question: What is in Proverbs 29:18 about vision?
answer: Where there is no vision, the people cast off restraint.

question: What does Proverbs 31:8-9 say about defending?
answer: Open thy mouth for the dumb, in the cause of all such as are left desolate. Open thy mouth, judge righteously, and minister justice to the poor and needy.

question: What is in Proverbs 31:25 about strength?
answer: Strength and dignity are her clothing; and she laugheth at the time to come.

question: What is the great sound of joy, per Ecclesiastes 7:1?
answer: A good name is better than precious oil; and the day of death, than the day of one's birth.

question: What is the better path, per Ecclesiastes 7:8?
answer: Better is the end of a thing than the beginning thereof; and the patient in spirit is better than the proud in spirit.

question: What is in Ecclesiastes 11:1 about bread on waters?
answer: Cast thy bread upon the waters; for thou shalt find it after many days.

question: What is the youth's joy, per Ecclesiastes 11:9?
answer: Rejoice, O young man, in thy youth, and let thy heart cheer thee in the days of thy youth.

question: What is in Ecclesiastes 12:1 about youth?
answer: Remember also thy Creator in the days of thy youth, before the evil days come.

question: What is the longing of Song of Solomon 2:1?
answer: I am a rose of Sharon, a lily of the valleys.

question: What is the love stronger than death, per Song of Solomon 8:6?
answer: Love is strong as death; jealousy is cruel as Sheol.

question: What did Job say of the Almighty's protection, per Job 1:10?
answer: Hast not thou made a hedge about him, and about his house, and about all that he hath, on every side?

question: What was the wager Satan proposed about Job, per Job 1:11?
answer: Put forth thy hand now, and touch all that he hath, and he will renounce thee to thy face.

question: What was Eliphaz's accusation in Job 4:7?
answer: Remember, I pray thee, who ever perished, being innocent? or where were the upright cut off?

question: What did Bildad say of the hypocrite, per Job 8:13?
answer: So are the paths of all that forget God; and the hope of the godless man shall perish.

question: What did Job say of his birthday, per Job 3:1?
answer: After this opened Job his mouth, and cursed his day.

question: What did Job say of God's wisdom, per Job 12:13?
answer: With God is wisdom and might; he hath counsel and understanding.

question: What was Elihu's response in Job 32:8?
answer: There is a spirit in man, and the breath of the Almighty giveth them understanding.

question: What did the LORD say to Job's friends, per Job 42:7?
answer: My wrath is kindled against thee, and against thy two friends; for ye have not spoken of me the thing that is right, as my servant Job hath.

question: How many sheep did Job have after his trial, per Job 42:12?
answer: Fourteen thousand sheep, and six thousand camels, and a thousand yoke of oxen, and a thousand she-asses.

question: What did Job's daughters' beauty represent, per Job 42:15?
answer: In all the land were no women found so fair as the daughters of Job: and their father gave them inheritance among their brethren.

question: What is the great refuge under wings, per Psalm 91:4?
answer: He will cover thee with his pinions, and under his wings shalt thou take refuge.

question: What does Jehovah say in Psalm 91:14-15?
answer: Because he hath set his love upon me, therefore will I deliver him: I will set him on high, because he hath known my name.

question: What is the shouting of joy in Psalm 100:1?
answer: Make a joyful noise unto Jehovah, all ye lands.

question: What is the call to know in Psalm 100:3?
answer: Know ye that Jehovah, he is God: it is he that hath made us, and we are his.

question: What is in Psalm 103:13 about a father?
answer: Like as a father pitieth his children, so Jehovah pitieth them that fear him.

question: What is the comparison in Psalm 103:15?
answer: As for man, his days are as grass; as a flower of the field, so he flourisheth.

question: What is in Psalm 100:5 about Jehovah?
answer: For Jehovah is good; his lovingkindness endureth for ever.

question: What did the Psalmist describe in Psalm 139:7?
answer: Whither shall I go from thy Spirit? Or whither shall I flee from thy presence?

question: What is the morning prayer of Psalm 5:3?
answer: O Jehovah, in the morning shalt thou hear my voice; in the morning will I order my prayer unto thee.

question: What is Psalm 25:5 about teaching?
answer: Guide me in thy truth, and teach me; for thou art the God of my salvation; for thee do I wait all the day.

question: What does Psalm 27:14 advise?
answer: Wait for Jehovah: be strong, and let thy heart take courage; yea, wait thou for Jehovah.

question: What is in Psalm 33:18 about God's eye?
answer: Behold, the eye of Jehovah is upon them that fear him, upon them that hope in his lovingkindness.

question: What does Psalm 37:23 say of steps?
answer: A man's goings are established of Jehovah; and he delighteth in his way.

question: What does Psalm 46:10 declare?
answer: Be still, and know that I am God: I will be exalted among the nations, I will be exalted in the earth.

question: What does Psalm 50:15 promise?
answer: Call upon me in the day of trouble; I will deliver thee, and thou shalt glorify me.

question: What does Psalm 73:26 say of the heart?
answer: My flesh and my heart faileth; but God is the strength of my heart and my portion for ever.

question: What does Psalm 86:11 ask?
answer: Teach me thy way, O Jehovah; I will walk in thy truth: unite my heart to fear thy name.

question: What does Psalm 145:8 declare?
answer: Jehovah is gracious, and merciful; slow to anger, and of great lovingkindness.

question: What is in Psalm 145:18 about prayer?
answer: Jehovah is nigh unto all them that call upon him, to all that call upon him in truth.

question: What is in Psalm 147:3 about the brokenhearted?
answer: He healeth the broken in heart, and bindeth up their wounds.

question: What is in Psalm 147:4 about stars?
answer: He counteth the number of the stars; he calleth them all by their names.

question: What is the praise of Psalm 148:7-8?
answer: Praise Jehovah from the earth, ye sea-monsters, and all deeps; fire and hail, snow and vapor; stormy wind, fulfilling his word.

question: How does Psalm 1:6 close?
answer: For Jehovah knoweth the way of the righteous; but the way of the wicked shall perish.

question: What did Psalm 4:4 advise?
answer: Stand in awe, and sin not: commune with your own heart upon your bed, and be still.

question: What is the Psalm 6 plea?
answer: O Jehovah, rebuke me not in thine anger, neither chasten me in thy hot displeasure.

question: What is the request of Psalm 13:1?
answer: How long, O Jehovah? wilt thou forget me for ever? how long wilt thou hide thy face from me?

question: What does Psalm 16:8 declare?
answer: I have set Jehovah always before me: because he is at my right hand, I shall not be moved.

question: What does Psalm 18:2 declare?
answer: Jehovah is my rock, and my fortress, and my deliverer.

question: What is the end of Psalm 22:24?
answer: For he hath not despised nor abhorred the affliction of the afflicted; neither hath he hid his face from him.

question: What does Psalm 25:8 declare?
answer: Good and upright is Jehovah: therefore will he instruct sinners in the way.

question: What does Psalm 34:14 say of evil?
answer: Depart from evil, and do good; seek peace, and pursue it.

question: What does Psalm 34:18 promise?
answer: Jehovah is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart, and saveth such as are of a contrite spirit.

question: How does Isaiah's vision begin, per Isaiah 1:1?
answer: The vision of Isaiah the son of Amoz, which he saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem.

question: What did Isaiah hear in his temple vision, per Isaiah 6:3?
answer: Holy, holy, holy, is Jehovah of hosts: the whole earth is full of his glory.

question: How was Isaiah's mouth purified, per Isaiah 6:7?
answer: Lo, this hath touched thy lips; and thine iniquity is taken away, and thy sin forgiven.

question: What did Isaiah say when called, per Isaiah 6:8?
answer: Here am I; send me.

question: What sign did Isaiah give of Immanuel, per Isaiah 7:14?
answer: Behold, a virgin shall conceive, and bear a son, and shall call his name Immanuel.

question: What is the prophecy of the child in Isaiah 9:6?
answer: For unto us a child is born, unto us a son is given; and the government shall be upon his shoulder.

question: What names are given to the child, per Isaiah 9:6?
answer: His name shall be called Wonderful, Counsellor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace.

question: What rod from Jesse is foretold in Isaiah 11:1?
answer: There shall come forth a shoot out of the stock of Jesse, and a branch out of his roots shall bear fruit.

question: What is the peaceable kingdom in Isaiah 11:6?
answer: The wolf shall dwell with the lamb, and the leopard shall lie down with the kid.

question: What is the wells of salvation, per Isaiah 12:3?
answer: Therefore with joy shall ye draw water out of the wells of salvation.

question: What does Isaiah 26:3 say about peace?
answer: Thou wilt keep him in perfect peace, whose mind is stayed on thee.

question: What is the everlasting rock in Isaiah 26:4?
answer: Trust ye in Jehovah for ever; for in Jehovah, even Jehovah, is an everlasting rock.

question: What is the renewal of strength in Isaiah 40:31?
answer: They that wait for Jehovah shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings as eagles.

question: What is the voice crying in Isaiah 40:3?
answer: The voice of one that crieth, Prepare ye in the wilderness the way of Jehovah.

question: What does grass do in Isaiah 40:8?
answer: The grass withereth, the flower fadeth; but the word of our God shall stand forever.

question: What is the comfort of Isaiah 41:10?
answer: Fear thou not, for I am with thee; be not dismayed, for I am thy God.

question: What is the Servant in Isaiah 42:1?
answer: Behold, my servant, whom I uphold; my chosen, in whom my soul delighteth.

question: What does Isaiah 43:1 declare?
answer: Fear not, for I have redeemed thee; I have called thee by thy name, thou art mine.

question: What does Isaiah 43:2 promise?
answer: When thou passest through the waters, I will be with thee; and through the rivers, they shall not overflow thee.

question: What is the new thing in Isaiah 43:19?
answer: Behold, I will do a new thing; now shall it spring forth; shall ye not know it?

question: What does Isaiah 53:5 declare about the Servant?
answer: He was wounded for our transgressions, he was bruised for our iniquities; the chastisement of our peace was upon him; and with his stripes we are healed.

question: What is the lamb in Isaiah 53:7?
answer: He was oppressed, yet when he was afflicted he opened not his mouth; as a lamb that is led to the slaughter.

question: What is the call of Isaiah 55:1?
answer: Ho, every one that thirsteth, come ye to the waters, and he that hath no money; come ye, buy, and eat.

question: What does Isaiah 55:6 say of seeking?
answer: Seek ye Jehovah while he may be found; call ye upon him while he is near.

question: What does Isaiah 55:8 say of God's thoughts?
answer: For my thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways my ways, saith Jehovah.

question: What does Isaiah 55:11 say of the word?
answer: My word shall not return unto me void, but it shall accomplish that which I please.

question: What is the new heavens and earth in Isaiah 65:17?
answer: Behold, I create new heavens and a new earth; and the former things shall not be remembered.

question: What did Jehovah say of Jeremiah's calling, per Jeremiah 1:5?
answer: Before I formed thee in the belly I knew thee, and before thou camest forth out of the womb I sanctified thee.

question: What did Jeremiah say in his calling, per Jeremiah 1:6?
answer: Ah, Lord Jehovah! behold, I know not how to speak; for I am a child.

question: What did Jeremiah see as a vision, per Jeremiah 1:11?
answer: I see a rod of an almond-tree.

question: What is the broken cisterns image, per Jeremiah 2:13?
answer: They have forsaken me, the fountain of living waters, and hewed them out cisterns, broken cisterns, that can hold no water.

question: What does Jeremiah 9:23-24 say of glorying?
answer: Let not the wise man glory in his wisdom, neither let the mighty man glory in his might; but let him that glorieth glory in this, that he hath understanding, and knoweth me.

question: What is Jeremiah 17:9 about the heart?
answer: The heart is deceitful above all things, and it is exceedingly corrupt: who can know it?

question: What is Jeremiah 29:11 about plans?
answer: For I know the thoughts that I think toward you, saith Jehovah, thoughts of peace, and not of evil, to give you hope in your latter end.

question: What is Jeremiah 29:13 about seeking?
answer: Ye shall seek me, and find me, when ye shall search for me with all your heart.

question: What is the new covenant in Jeremiah 31:33?
answer: I will put my law in their inward parts, and in their heart will I write it.

question: What does Jeremiah 33:3 declare?
answer: Call unto me, and I will answer thee, and will show thee great things, and difficult, which thou knowest not.

question: How long did Jeremiah prophesy concerning Babylonian captivity, per Jeremiah 25:11?
answer: This whole land shall be a desolation, and an astonishment; and these nations shall serve the king of Babylon seventy years.

question: What does Lamentations 3:22 declare?
answer: It is of Jehovah's lovingkindnesses that we are not consumed, because his compassions fail not.

question: What is Lamentations 3:23 about mercies?
answer: They are new every morning; great is thy faithfulness.

question: What did Ezekiel see in his vision, per Ezekiel 1:4?
answer: A stormy wind came out of the north, a great cloud, with a fire infolding itself, and a brightness round about it.

question: What were the four faces of the living creatures, per Ezekiel 1:10?
answer: They four had the face of a man; and the face of a lion on the right side; and the face of an ox on the left side; they four had also the face of an eagle.

question: What did Ezekiel eat as a sign, per Ezekiel 3:1?
answer: Son of man, eat that which thou findest; eat this roll, and go, speak unto the house of Israel.

question: What did Ezekiel say of the watchman, per Ezekiel 3:17?
answer: Son of man, I have made thee a watchman unto the house of Israel.

question: What is the new heart promise, per Ezekiel 36:26?
answer: A new heart also will I give you, and a new spirit will I put within you.

question: What was the valley Ezekiel saw, per Ezekiel 37:1?
answer: He brought me forth in the Spirit of Jehovah, and set me down in the midst of the valley; and it was full of bones.

question: What did Jehovah ask Ezekiel about the bones, per Ezekiel 37:3?
answer: Son of man, can these bones live?

question: What did Ezekiel prophesy to the bones, per Ezekiel 37:7?
answer: There was a noise, and behold, an earthquake; and the bones came together, bone to its bone.

question: How was Daniel taken to Babylon, per Daniel 1:3-4?
answer: The king spake unto Ashpenaz the master of his eunuchs, that he should bring in certain of the children of Israel.

question: What were Daniel's three friends' Babylonian names, per Daniel 1:7?
answer: Belteshazzar, Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed-nego.

question: What did Daniel refuse to do, per Daniel 1:8?
answer: Daniel purposed in his heart that he would not defile himself with the king's dainties, nor with the wine which he drank.

question: How long did Daniel test the diet of pulse, per Daniel 1:12?
answer: Prove thy servants, I beseech thee, ten days.

question: What did Nebuchadnezzar dream of in Daniel 2:31?
answer: Thou, O king, sawest, and, behold, a great image. This image, which was mighty, and whose brightness was excellent, stood before thee.

question: What was the head of the image, per Daniel 2:32?
answer: As for this image, his head was of fine gold, his breast and his arms of silver, his belly and his thighs of brass.

question: What was the stone in the dream, per Daniel 2:34?
answer: A stone was cut out without hands, which smote the image upon its feet that were of iron and clay, and brake them in pieces.

question: What did Daniel's three friends refuse to do, per Daniel 3:18?
answer: Be it known unto thee, O king, that we will not serve thy gods, nor worship the golden image which thou hast set up.

question: What did Nebuchadnezzar see in the fiery furnace, per Daniel 3:25?
answer: I see four men loose, walking in the midst of the fire, and they have no hurt; and the aspect of the fourth is like a son of the gods.

question: What did Belshazzar see written on the wall, per Daniel 5:25?
answer: Mene, Mene, Tekel, Upharsin.

question: What did the writing mean, per Daniel 5:26-28?
answer: Mene; God hath numbered thy kingdom, and brought it to an end. Tekel; thou art weighed in the balances, and art found wanting. Peres; thy kingdom is divided.

question: How was Daniel cast into the lions' den, per Daniel 6:16?
answer: They brought Daniel, and cast him into the den of lions.

question: What did Daniel say to the king, per Daniel 6:22?
answer: My God hath sent his angel, and hath shut the lions' mouths, and they have not hurt me.

question: What was the seventy weeks prophecy, per Daniel 9:24?
answer: Seventy weeks are decreed upon thy people and upon thy holy city, to finish transgression, and to make an end of sins.

question: What did Daniel see of the Ancient of Days, per Daniel 7:9?
answer: His raiment was white as snow, and the hair of his head like pure wool; his throne was fiery flames, and the wheels thereof burning fire.

question: What did Daniel see of the Son of man, per Daniel 7:13?
answer: There came with the clouds of heaven one like unto a son of man, and he came even to the ancient of days.

question: How does Hosea's prophecy begin, per Hosea 1:2?
answer: Go, take unto thee a wife of whoredom and children of whoredom; for the land doth commit great whoredom.

question: What did Hosea name his children, per Hosea 1:4-9?
answer: Jezreel, Lo-ruhamah (not pitied), and Lo-ammi (not my people).

question: What does Hosea 6:6 say of mercy?
answer: For I desire goodness, and not sacrifice; and the knowledge of God more than burnt-offerings.

question: What is the famous Hosea 4:6 verse?
answer: My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge.

question: What does Hosea 11:1 say of Israel?
answer: When Israel was a child, then I loved him, and called my son out of Egypt.

question: What did Joel call for, per Joel 2:12?
answer: Turn ye unto me with all your heart, and with fasting, and with weeping, and with mourning.

question: What did Joel prophesy about the Spirit, per Joel 2:28?
answer: It shall come to pass afterward, that I will pour out my Spirit upon all flesh; and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy.

question: What did Joel say about the day of Jehovah, per Joel 2:31?
answer: The sun shall be turned into darkness, and the moon into blood, before the great and terrible day of Jehovah cometh.

question: What was Amos by occupation, per Amos 1:1?
answer: Amos, who was among the herdsmen of Tekoa.

question: What did Amos say of God's word, per Amos 3:7?
answer: Surely the Lord Jehovah will do nothing, except he reveal his secret unto his servants the prophets.

question: What did Amos call for, per Amos 5:24?
answer: But let justice roll down as waters, and righteousness as a mighty stream.

question: What was the source of Amos's vision, per Amos 7:14-15?
answer: I was no prophet, neither was I a prophet's son; but I was a herdsman, and a dresser of sycomore-trees: and Jehovah took me from following the flock.

question: What was the message of Obadiah, per Obadiah 1:4?
answer: Though thou mount on high as the eagle, and though thy nest be set among the stars, I will bring thee down from thence, saith Jehovah.

question: How did Jonah flee from God, per Jonah 1:3?
answer: He went down to Joppa, and found a ship going to Tarshish: so he paid the fare thereof, and went down into it.

question: What was thrown to calm the storm, per Jonah 1:15?
answer: They took up Jonah, and cast him forth into the sea: and the sea ceased from its raging.

question: How long was Jonah in the fish, per Jonah 1:17?
answer: Jonah was in the belly of the fish three days and three nights.

question: What was Jonah's prayer in the fish, per Jonah 2:2?
answer: I called by reason of mine affliction unto Jehovah, and he answered me.

question: How big was Nineveh, per Jonah 3:3?
answer: An exceeding great city, of three days' journey.

question: What did the king of Nineveh do, per Jonah 3:6?
answer: He arose from his throne, and laid his robe from him, and covered him with sackcloth, and sat in ashes.

question: What did God say to Jonah about the gourd, per Jonah 4:11?
answer: Should not I have regard for Nineveh, that great city, wherein are more than sixscore thousand persons that cannot discern between their right hand and their left hand?

question: What does Micah 6:8 require?
answer: What doth Jehovah require of thee, but to do justly, and to love kindness, and to walk humbly with thy God?

question: What did Micah prophesy about Bethlehem, per Micah 5:2?
answer: But thou, Beth-lehem Ephrathah, which art little to be among the thousands of Judah, out of thee shall one come forth unto me that is to be ruler in Israel.

question: What does Nahum 1:7 say of Jehovah?
answer: Jehovah is good, a stronghold in the day of trouble; and he knoweth them that take refuge in him.

question: What did Habakkuk 2:4 declare?
answer: The righteous shall live by his faith.

question: What is Habakkuk's vision in 2:14?
answer: For the earth shall be filled with the knowledge of the glory of Jehovah, as the waters cover the sea.

question: What does Habakkuk 3:17-18 declare about rejoicing?
answer: Though the fig-tree shall not flourish, neither shall fruit be in the vines; the labor of the olive shall fail, and the fields shall yield no food; yet I will rejoice in Jehovah, I will joy in the God of my salvation.

question: What is Zephaniah 3:17 about?
answer: Jehovah thy God is in the midst of thee, a mighty one who will save; he will rejoice over thee with joy; he will rest in his love.

question: What did Haggai call the people to do, per Haggai 1:7?
answer: Consider your ways.

question: What was Zechariah's vision of horses, per Zechariah 1:8?
answer: I saw in the night, and, behold, a man riding upon a red horse, and he stood among the myrtle-trees that were in the bottom; and behind him there were horses, red, sorrel, and white.

question: What did Zechariah prophesy of the king, per Zechariah 9:9?
answer: Behold, thy king cometh unto thee; he is just, and having salvation; lowly, and riding upon an ass.

question: What does Zechariah 13:6 prophesy?
answer: What are these wounds between thine arms? Then he shall answer, Those with which I was wounded in the house of my friends.

question: What did Malachi prophesy about tithes, per Malachi 3:10?
answer: Bring ye the whole tithe into the store-house, that there may be food in my house, and prove me now herewith, saith Jehovah of hosts.

question: What did Malachi prophesy about the messenger, per Malachi 3:1?
answer: Behold, I send my messenger, and he shall prepare the way before me.

question: What is Malachi's prophecy about Elijah, per Malachi 4:5?
answer: Behold, I will send you Elijah the prophet before the great and terrible day of Jehovah come.

question: What does Malachi 4:2 prophesy?
answer: Unto you that fear my name shall the sun of righteousness arise with healing in its wings.

question: What was Isaiah's prophecy of swords to plowshares, per Isaiah 2:4?
answer: They shall beat their swords into plowshares, and their spears into pruning-hooks.

question: What does Isaiah 5:20 warn?
answer: Woe unto them that call evil good, and good evil; that put darkness for light, and light for darkness.

question: What is Isaiah 30:21 about the way?
answer: Thine ears shall hear a word behind thee, saying, This is the way, walk ye in it.

question: What does Isaiah 32:17 declare about righteousness?
answer: The work of righteousness shall be peace; and the effect of righteousness, quietness and confidence for ever.

question: What does Isaiah 35:1 prophesy?
answer: The wilderness and the dry land shall be glad; and the desert shall rejoice, and blossom as the rose.

question: What does Isaiah 35:5 prophesy?
answer: Then the eyes of the blind shall be opened, and the ears of the deaf shall be unstopped.

question: What does Isaiah 38:5 promise Hezekiah?
answer: Behold, I will add unto thy days fifteen years.

question: What does Isaiah 40:11 say of the shepherd?
answer: He will feed his flock like a shepherd, he will gather the lambs in his arm.
