answer: Let us hold fast the confession of our hope that it waver not; for he is faithful that promised.

question: What does Hebrews 11:3 declare?
answer: By faith we understand that the worlds have been framed by the word of God, so that what is seen hath not been made out of things which appear.

question: What does Hebrews 12:5-6 declare?
answer: My son, regard not lightly the chastening of the Lord, nor faint when thou art reproved of him; for whom the Lord loveth he chasteneth.

question: What does Hebrews 12:28-29 declare?
answer: Wherefore, receiving a kingdom that cannot be shaken, let us have grace, whereby we may offer service well-pleasing to God with reverence and awe: for our God is a consuming fire.

question: What does Hebrews 13:6 declare?
answer: The Lord is my helper; I will not fear: What shall man do unto me?

question: What does Hebrews 13:20-21 declare?
answer: Now the God of peace, who brought again from the dead the great shepherd of the sheep with the blood of an eternal covenant, even our Lord Jesus.

question: What does James 1:12 declare?
answer: Blessed is the man that endureth temptation; for when he hath been approved, he shall receive the crown of life.

question: What does James 3:17 declare?
answer: The wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, easy to be entreated, full of mercy and good fruits, without variance, without hypocrisy.

question: What does James 5:11 declare?
answer: Behold, we call them blessed that endured: ye have heard of the patience of Job, and have seen the end of the Lord.

question: What does 1 Peter 1:8 declare?
answer: Whom not having seen ye love; on whom, though now ye see him not, yet believing, ye rejoice greatly with joy unspeakable and full of glory.

question: What does 1 Peter 2:2 declare?
answer: As newborn babes, long for the spiritual milk which is without guile, that ye may grow thereby unto salvation.

question: What does 1 Peter 4:12-13 declare?
answer: Beloved, think it not strange concerning the fiery trial among you, which cometh upon you to prove you, as though a strange thing happened unto you: but insomuch as ye are partakers of Christ's sufferings, rejoice.

question: What does 2 Peter 1:10 declare?
answer: Wherefore, brethren, give the more diligence to make your calling and election sure.

question: What does 1 John 2:3 declare?
answer: Hereby we know that we know him, if we keep his commandments.

question: What does 1 John 5:7 declare?
answer: It is the Spirit that beareth witness, because the Spirit is the truth.

question: What does Revelation 1:3 declare?
answer: Blessed is he that readeth, and they that hear the words of the prophecy, and keep the things that are written therein.

question: What does Revelation 1:7 declare?
answer: Behold, he cometh with the clouds; and every eye shall see him, and they that pierced him; and all the tribes of the earth shall mourn over him.

question: What does Revelation 21:8 declare?
answer: For the fearful, and unbelieving, and abominable, and murderers, and fornicators, and sorcerers, and idolaters, and all liars, their part shall be in the lake that burneth with fire and brimstone.

question: How many foundations did the city have, per Revelation 21:14?
answer: The wall of the city had twelve foundations, and on them twelve names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.

question: What was the measurement of the city, per Revelation 21:16?
answer: The city lieth foursquare, and the length thereof is as great as the breadth: and he measured the city with the reed, twelve thousand furlongs: the length and the breadth and the height thereof are equal.

question: What is the absence in New Jerusalem, per Revelation 21:22?
answer: I saw no temple therein: for the Lord God the Almighty, and the Lamb, are the temple thereof.

question: What does Revelation 22:3-4 declare?
answer: There shall be no curse any more: and the throne of God and of the Lamb shall be therein: and his servants shall serve him; and they shall see his face.

question: Who is God?
answer: God is the eternal, almighty Creator of heaven and earth; one in essence, existing in three Persons: the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit.

question: What does it mean that God is a Trinity?
answer: It means there is one God who exists eternally in three distinct Persons — Father, Son, and Holy Spirit — coequal in power and glory, sharing one divine essence.

question: Who is Jesus Christ?
answer: Jesus is the eternal Son of God who became man, was born of the virgin Mary, lived a sinless life, died on the cross for our sins, rose again the third day, and now sits at the right hand of the Father.

question: Who is the Holy Spirit?
answer: The Holy Spirit is the third Person of the Trinity, fully God, who convicts of sin, regenerates believers, indwells them, and empowers them for godly living and service.

question: What is the Bible?
answer: The Bible is the inspired, written Word of God, comprising 66 books in two Testaments, given by the Holy Spirit through human authors as the rule of faith and practice.

question: How many books are in the Bible?
answer: The Protestant Bible contains 66 books: 39 in the Old Testament and 27 in the New Testament.

question: What is the Old Testament?
answer: The Old Testament is the first part of the Bible, containing 39 books that record creation, God's covenant with Israel, the Law, the prophets, and Israel's history before the coming of Christ.

question: What is the New Testament?
answer: The New Testament is the second part of the Bible, containing 27 books that record the life and teachings of Jesus Christ, the founding of the church, the apostles' letters, and the prophecies of the end.

question: What is the Gospel?
answer: The Gospel is the good news that Jesus Christ died for our sins, was buried, and rose again on the third day, offering forgiveness and eternal life to all who believe in him.

question: What is sin?
answer: Sin is any thought, word, or action that breaks God's law, whether by doing what He forbids or failing to do what He commands.

question: What is salvation?
answer: Salvation is God's deliverance of sinners from the guilt, power, and ultimately the presence of sin, given freely by grace through faith in Jesus Christ.

question: How is a person saved?
answer: A person is saved by God's grace through faith in Jesus Christ, who died for our sins and rose again, not by works of righteousness.

question: What does it mean to be born again?
answer: To be born again is to be spiritually renewed by the Holy Spirit, receiving a new heart and new life through faith in Jesus Christ, as Jesus told Nicodemus in John 3.

question: What is the difference between grace and mercy?
answer: Mercy is God not giving us the punishment we deserve; grace is God giving us the blessing and favor we do not deserve.

question: What is heaven?
answer: Heaven is the eternal dwelling place of God where believers will live in His presence forever in joy, holiness, and freedom from sin and sorrow.

question: What is hell?
answer: Hell is the place of eternal punishment for those who reject God; a state of separation from His blessings and conscious torment.

question: What is the Lord's Prayer?
answer: The Lord's Prayer is the model prayer Jesus taught his disciples in Matthew 6:9-13 and Luke 11:2-4, beginning, 'Our Father who art in heaven, Hallowed be thy name.'

question: What are the Ten Commandments?
answer: The Ten Commandments are God's moral law given to Moses on Mount Sinai, recorded in Exodus 20 and Deuteronomy 5, summarizing duties to God and to neighbor.

question: What is the greatest commandment?
answer: Jesus said the greatest commandment is to love the Lord your God with all your heart, soul, mind, and strength, and the second is to love your neighbor as yourself.

question: What is the Church?
answer: The Church is the body of Christ, made up of all true believers in Jesus from every nation, called out of the world to worship God and proclaim the gospel.

question: What is the Lord's Supper?
answer: The Lord's Supper, or Communion, is the ordinance Jesus instituted on the night before his crucifixion, in which believers eat bread and drink the cup in remembrance of his body and blood given for them.

question: What does it mean that Jesus is Lord?
answer: To call Jesus 'Lord' means to acknowledge that he is God, the rightful sovereign over all creation, and to submit to him as Master and King.

question: What does it mean that Jesus is Savior?
answer: To call Jesus 'Savior' means to trust him as the only one who can rescue us from sin, death, and God's judgment, having paid the price by his death and resurrection.

question: What is the meaning of Christ?
answer: 'Christ' (Greek Christos) means 'Anointed One' — the equivalent of the Hebrew 'Messiah' — referring to Jesus as the promised, anointed King and Savior.

question: What does Messiah mean?
answer: Messiah means 'Anointed One' in Hebrew, the title for the promised deliverer of Israel, fulfilled in Jesus of Nazareth.

question: What is the incarnation?
answer: The incarnation is the eternal Son of God taking on human nature in the womb of the virgin Mary, becoming fully God and fully man in the person of Jesus Christ.

question: What is the virgin birth?
answer: The virgin birth is the doctrine that Jesus was conceived in Mary's womb by the Holy Spirit without a human father, fulfilling Isaiah 7:14.

question: What is the ascension?
answer: The ascension is Jesus' bodily return to heaven forty days after his resurrection, where he was enthroned at the right hand of the Father.

question: What is the second coming of Christ?
answer: The second coming is Jesus' future, visible, bodily return to earth in glory to judge the living and the dead and to consummate his kingdom.

question: What does it mean Jesus died for our sins?
answer: It means Jesus took the place of sinners, bearing on the cross the punishment our sins deserved, so that all who trust in him might be forgiven and reconciled to God.

question: What is the atonement?
answer: The atonement is the work of Christ on the cross by which he reconciled sinners to God, satisfying divine justice and removing the guilt of sin through his sacrifice.

question: What is forgiveness?
answer: Forgiveness is God's removal of the guilt and penalty of sin from the believer through the atoning death of Jesus, and the believer's release of others from offense in turn.

question: How can my sins be forgiven?
answer: Sins are forgiven through faith in Jesus Christ, whose blood was shed for the remission of sins; whoever confesses sin and trusts in him is cleansed.

question: What is the difference between soul and spirit?
answer: Scripture sometimes distinguishes the soul (the seat of mind, will, and emotions) from the spirit (the inmost part that relates to God), though the two are often used interchangeably.

question: What happens when a Christian dies?
answer: At death, a Christian's body returns to the dust, but the soul goes to be with the Lord, awaiting the resurrection of the body at Christ's return.

question: What is eternal life?
answer: Eternal life is the gift of God in Jesus Christ — a quality of life that begins now in fellowship with God and continues unbroken in His presence forever.

question: How should we pray?
answer: We should pray sincerely, humbly, and persistently, in the name of Jesus, in accordance with God's will, with thanksgiving and faith, expecting God to hear and answer.

question: Does God answer prayer?
answer: Yes; God hears and answers the prayers of His people, sometimes with yes, sometimes with no, sometimes with wait, always according to His perfect will and timing.

question: What is worship?
answer: Worship is honoring and exalting God for who He is and what He has done, expressed through prayer, praise, song, obedience, and the offering of our whole lives to Him.

question: What is the fear of the Lord?
answer: The fear of the Lord is a reverent awe and respect for God's holiness, power, and majesty that produces obedience, humility, and worship.

question: What is fellowship?
answer: Fellowship is the sharing of life together among believers, rooted in their common union with the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit.

question: What does the word Hallelujah mean?
answer: Hallelujah is a Hebrew word meaning 'Praise ye Jehovah' — a call to praise the Lord.

question: What does Amen mean?
answer: Amen means 'so be it' or 'truly,' affirming the truthfulness of a statement or the sincerity of a prayer.

question: What does Hosanna mean?
answer: Hosanna is a cry meaning 'Save now!' or 'Save, we pray!' used to acclaim Jesus as he entered Jerusalem.

question: Why is the Bible called Holy Scripture?
answer: Because it is set apart and inspired by the Holy Spirit, given by God for instruction in righteousness.

question: What is inspiration of Scripture?
answer: Inspiration is the work of the Holy Spirit moving the human authors of Scripture to write the very words of God, so that the Bible is fully truthful and authoritative.

question: What is the Word of God?
answer: The Word of God is God's self-revelation, supremely the person of Jesus Christ, and recorded for us in the Holy Scriptures.

question: Who wrote the Bible?
answer: About forty different human authors, moved by the Holy Spirit, wrote the Bible over a span of roughly 1,500 years; ultimately, God Himself is its divine Author.

question: In what languages was the Bible originally written?
answer: The Old Testament was written mostly in Hebrew (with some passages in Aramaic), and the New Testament was written in Greek.

question: What does Genesis mean?
answer: Genesis means 'beginning' or 'origin' — fitting for the first book of the Bible, which records the beginning of creation, humanity, sin, and God's plan of redemption.

question: What does Exodus mean?
answer: Exodus means 'going out' or 'departure,' referring to Israel's deliverance from slavery in Egypt.

question: What does Deuteronomy mean?
answer: Deuteronomy means 'second law,' a restatement of God's law to a new generation of Israelites before they entered the Promised Land.

question: What does Gospel mean?
answer: 'Gospel' translates the Greek 'euangelion,' meaning 'good news' — specifically the good news of Jesus Christ.

question: What does apostle mean?
answer: Apostle means 'one sent forth' — a messenger commissioned by Jesus, especially the twelve, plus Paul, who were sent to lay the foundation of the church.

question: What is the difference between an apostle and a disciple?
answer: A disciple is a learner or follower of Jesus; an apostle is a specially appointed messenger sent with authority to proclaim the gospel and lead the early church.

question: What is the kingdom of heaven?
answer: The kingdom of heaven is the same reality as the kingdom of God; Matthew often uses this term to honor the Jewish reverence for the divine name.

question: Why is Jesus called the Son of God?
answer: Jesus is called the Son of God because he is eternally begotten of the Father, sharing the same divine essence and nature.

question: Why is Jesus called the Son of Man?
answer: Son of Man was Jesus' favorite self-designation, drawn from Daniel 7:13, identifying him with humanity and as the heavenly figure given an everlasting kingdom.

question: What does Lamb of God mean?
answer: Lamb of God is a title for Jesus highlighting that he is God's appointed sacrifice, who takes away the sin of the world.

question: What does the cross mean to Christians?
answer: The cross is the central symbol of the Christian faith, representing Jesus' atoning death by which sinners are reconciled to God and the power of God for salvation.

question: What is the law of Christ?
answer: The law of Christ is the call to love God and love one another as Christ has loved us, summarizing the moral will of God for believers.

question: What is the fruit of the Spirit?
answer: The fruit of the Spirit, per Galatians 5:22-23, is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, meekness, and self-control.

question: What are spiritual gifts?
answer: Spiritual gifts are abilities given by the Holy Spirit to believers for the building up of the church and the service of others.

question: Who is the head of the church?
answer: Jesus Christ is the head of the church; he is the chief shepherd, husband, and Lord over his people.

question: What is a Christian?
answer: A Christian is a follower of Jesus Christ — one who has been forgiven of sin through faith in him and now seeks to live in obedience to him as Lord.

question: How does someone become a Christian?
answer: By repenting of sin and believing in Jesus Christ as Lord and Savior, trusting in his death and resurrection for forgiveness and eternal life.

question: What is a believer?
answer: A believer is anyone who places saving faith in Jesus Christ — receiving forgiveness, the Holy Spirit, and adoption as a child of God.

question: What is the role of the Holy Spirit in a believer's life?
answer: The Holy Spirit indwells, teaches, comforts, convicts, sanctifies, and empowers the believer for holy living and faithful witness.

question: What is conviction of sin?
answer: Conviction of sin is the work of the Holy Spirit that exposes our wrongdoing, awakens our conscience, and leads us to repentance and faith.

question: What is the new covenant?
answer: The new covenant is the gracious covenant Jesus established by his blood, in which God writes His law on hearts, forgives sin, and pours out the Holy Spirit.

question: What is the difference between the old and new covenants?
answer: The old covenant, given through Moses, was based on the law and animal sacrifices; the new covenant, established in Christ, is based on grace, fulfilled by his perfect sacrifice, and accompanied by the indwelling Spirit.

question: What is the Sabbath?
answer: The Sabbath is the seventh day of the week, set apart by God as a day of rest and worship under the old covenant; for many Christians, the Lord's Day (Sunday) is now observed in remembrance of Christ's resurrection.

question: Why do Christians worship on Sunday?
answer: Christians worship on Sunday because Jesus rose from the dead on the first day of the week, the Lord's Day, and the early church gathered then to remember and celebrate his resurrection.

question: What is tithing?
answer: Tithing is the practice of giving a tenth of one's income or produce to the Lord, taught in the Old Testament and viewed by many Christians as a guide for proportionate, generous giving today.

question: What is fasting?
answer: Fasting is the voluntary abstaining from food (or other things) for spiritual purposes — to humble oneself, seek God earnestly, and grow in dependence on Him.

question: What is meditation in Scripture?
answer: Biblical meditation is the prayerful, repeated reflection on God's Word, allowing it to fill the mind and shape the heart.

question: What is wisdom?
answer: Biblical wisdom is the skill of living rightly under God; it begins with the fear of the Lord and applies God's truth to everyday life.

question: What is the difference between wisdom and knowledge?
answer: Knowledge is information about facts or truths; wisdom is the skill to apply truth rightly to life in a way that pleases God.

question: What is hope?
answer: Christian hope is a confident expectation of the good God has promised in Christ — including resurrection, glorification, and eternal life with Him.

question: What is love?
answer: Biblical love is patient, kind, self-giving devotion — willing the good of another, supremely shown by God's love in giving His Son for sinners.

question: What is the world in the Bible?
answer: 'The world' may refer to the created order, to humanity, or, often in Scripture, to the system of values and powers in rebellion against God.

question: Who is Satan?
answer: Satan is a fallen angel and the great enemy of God and His people; he is called the devil, the deceiver, and the accuser of the brethren.

question: Are angels real?
answer: Yes; angels are real spiritual beings created by God, who serve Him, worship Him, and minister to those who will inherit salvation.

question: Are demons real?
answer: Yes; demons are fallen angels who follow Satan in opposing God and seeking to harm people, but they are limited and ultimately doomed.

question: What is spiritual warfare?
answer: Spiritual warfare is the believer's ongoing struggle against the devil, the world, and the flesh, fought with the armor of God, prayer, and the Word.

question: How do I overcome temptation?
answer: By relying on God's strength, fleeing from sin, hiding His Word in your heart, praying for help, and remembering that He provides a way of escape.

question: How do I find God's will for my life?
answer: By saturating yourself in God's Word, walking in obedience, seeking godly counsel, praying for wisdom, and trusting the Lord to direct your steps.

question: What does it mean to walk by faith?
answer: To walk by faith is to live trusting God's promises and unseen realities rather than relying merely on what is visible to the senses.

question: What is the meaning of life according to the Bible?
answer: According to Scripture, the meaning of life is to know and glorify God — to love Him with all our being and to enjoy Him forever.

question: What does it mean to fear God?
answer: To fear God is to revere Him as holy and almighty, taking sin seriously and walking humbly before Him in love and obedience.

question: How do I read the Bible?
answer: Read prayerfully, regularly, and reflectively — asking the Holy Spirit to teach you, considering each passage in its context, and applying its truth to your life.

question: Where should a new Christian start reading the Bible?
answer: Many encourage new believers to begin with the Gospel of John or the Gospel of Mark, then read through the New Testament and Psalms.

question: What is the Great Tribulation?
answer: The Great Tribulation is a period of unparalleled distress and trouble on the earth in the last days, prophesied in Daniel and Revelation.

question: What is the millennium?
answer: The millennium is the thousand-year reign of Christ described in Revelation 20, understood by Christians in differing ways but pointing to Christ's victorious rule.

question: What is the rapture?
answer: The rapture is the catching up of believers to meet the Lord in the air at His coming, described in 1 Thessalonians 4:16-17 and 1 Corinthians 15:51-52.

question: What is the final judgment?
answer: The final judgment is the future event when Christ will judge the living and the dead, rewarding His servants and condemning evildoers.

question: What is the new heaven and new earth?
answer: The new heaven and new earth are God's renewed creation, free from sin, suffering, and death, where the redeemed will dwell with Him forever.

question: Will there be marriage in heaven?
answer: Jesus said that in the resurrection people neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like the angels in heaven (Matthew 22:30).

question: Will we know each other in heaven?
answer: Scripture indicates that believers will recognize one another in glory; the disciples recognized Moses and Elijah at the Transfiguration, and Paul speaks of being known fully (1 Corinthians 13:12).

question: Are there guardian angels?
answer: Jesus speaks of children's angels who behold the Father (Matthew 18:10), and Hebrews 1:14 calls angels ministering spirits sent for those who inherit salvation.

question: What does sovereignty of God mean?
answer: God's sovereignty is His absolute right and power to rule over all things; everything in heaven and earth is under His wise and holy government.

question: What does omniscient mean?
answer: Omniscient means all-knowing; God knows everything past, present, and future, including thoughts and hearts.

question: What does omnipotent mean?
answer: Omnipotent means all-powerful; nothing is too hard for God.

question: What does omnipresent mean?
answer: Omnipresent means present everywhere; God fills heaven and earth and is never absent.

question: What does immutable mean?
answer: Immutable means unchanging; God does not change in His being, character, or purposes.

question: What does eternal mean of God?
answer: To say God is eternal means He has no beginning and no end; He existed before time and will exist beyond it.

question: What does holy mean?
answer: Holy means set apart, pure, and morally perfect; God's holiness is His absolute purity and separateness from all that is sinful.

question: What is the glory of God?
answer: The glory of God is the manifest beauty, weight, and majesty of His character — His holiness, power, love, and goodness on display.

question: What is providence?
answer: Providence is God's continual care and government of all His creation, working all things together according to His wise and holy plan.

question: Does God have a plan for my life?
answer: Yes; God has good purposes for those who love Him (Romans 8:28), and He invites us to walk in His revealed will, trusting Him with the unknown.

question: Why does God allow suffering?
answer: Scripture teaches that God allows suffering for many reasons — to discipline, refine, draw us to Himself, and ultimately to display His glory and accomplish good — even when we cannot fully understand.

question: How do I know God loves me?
answer: You know God loves you because He sent His only Son Jesus to die for your sins; His love is proven on the cross and confirmed by His Word and Spirit (Romans 5:8).

question: How can I be sure I am saved?
answer: Assurance of salvation rests on God's promises in His Word, the testimony of the Holy Spirit in the believer, and the fruit of a transformed life.

question: What if I doubt my faith?
answer: Doubt is common; bring your honest questions to God, immerse yourself in Scripture, talk with mature believers, and ask the Lord to strengthen your faith — He helps unbelief (Mark 9:24).

question: How do I share my faith?
answer: Share your faith by living a life shaped by the gospel, praying for opportunities, telling others what Christ has done for you, and explaining how Jesus saves sinners.

question: What is evangelism?
answer: Evangelism is the proclamation of the good news of Jesus Christ — calling people to repent, believe, and be saved.

question: What is discipleship?
answer: Discipleship is the lifelong process of following Jesus and helping others follow him — being shaped by his Word and his Spirit, and reproducing his life in others.

question: What is a pastor?
answer: A pastor (or shepherd) is one called and gifted by God to lead, teach, and care for a local church under the authority of Jesus, the Chief Shepherd.

question: What is a deacon?
answer: A deacon is a servant-leader in the church appointed to assist the elders by caring for practical needs, especially of the poor and weak.

question: What is an elder?
answer: An elder is a spiritually mature man called to oversee, teach, and shepherd the local church (also called bishop or overseer in the New Testament).

question: What is the priesthood of all believers?
answer: The priesthood of all believers is the New Testament truth that every Christian has direct access to God through Christ and is called to offer spiritual sacrifices and proclaim His praises.

question: Can a Christian lose their salvation?
answer: Christians differ on this question; many hold that those truly born again are kept by God's power and will not be cast out, while warnings in Scripture call all believers to persevere in faith.

question: What is the difference between a sin and a mistake?
answer: A mistake is an unintended error; a sin is any failure to conform to God's law in thought, word, or deed — sin is fundamentally against God.

question: What is original sin?
answer: Original sin is the corrupted nature inherited from Adam by all humanity, which makes us prone to sin and in need of God's redeeming grace.

question: What was the fall?
answer: The fall refers to Adam and Eve's disobedience in eating the forbidden fruit (Genesis 3), bringing sin, death, and brokenness into the world.

question: Who was Adam?
answer: Adam was the first man, created by God from the dust of the ground, placed in the Garden of Eden, and the head of the human race.

question: Who was Eve?
answer: Eve was the first woman, formed by God from Adam's side to be his helper and the mother of all living.

question: Why did God create the world?
answer: God created the world for His own glory — to display His wisdom, power, goodness, and love, and to share His joy with creatures made in His image.

question: Why did God create human beings?
answer: God created human beings in His image to know Him, love Him, glorify Him, and rule the earth as His stewards.

question: What does it mean to be made in God's image?
answer: Being made in God's image means humans uniquely reflect God in personhood, reason, morality, creativity, and capacity for relationship — and possess God-given dignity.

question: How long did God take to create the world?
answer: According to Genesis 1, God created the world in six days and rested on the seventh.

question: What is the meaning of life on earth?
answer: Our life on earth is a stewardship and a pilgrimage — to know God, love and serve others, share the gospel, and prepare for eternity with Him.

question: What does the Bible say about marriage?
answer: The Bible teaches that marriage is a covenant union between one man and one woman, instituted by God in Genesis, picturing Christ and his Church.

question: What does the Bible say about divorce?
answer: Jesus taught that God hates divorce; while there are limited grounds permitted in Scripture (such as sexual immorality and abandonment), God's design is lifelong faithfulness in marriage.

question: What does the Bible say about work?
answer: Work is God's good gift, given before the fall; we are to work heartily as for the Lord, providing for our families, serving others, and giving God glory.

question: What does the Bible say about money?
answer: The Bible warns against the love of money but encourages diligence, generosity, contentment, and using resources for God's glory and the good of others.

question: What does the Bible say about giving?
answer: Believers are to give cheerfully, generously, and willingly, recognizing that all we have is from God; God loves a cheerful giver (2 Corinthians 9:7).

question: What does the Bible say about anxiety?
answer: The Bible commands us not to be anxious but to bring everything to God in prayer with thanksgiving, trusting Him to guard our hearts with His peace (Philippians 4:6-7).

question: What does the Bible say about anger?
answer: Be angry and sin not (Ephesians 4:26); we are to put away sinful anger, refrain from outbursts, and pursue forgiveness.

question: What does the Bible say about patience?
answer: Patience is a fruit of the Spirit; God is patient with us, and we are called to be patient with one another in love (Galatians 5:22; Ephesians 4:2).

question: What does the Bible say about pride?
answer: Pride goes before destruction (Proverbs 16:18); God resists the proud but gives grace to the humble (James 4:6).

question: What does the Bible say about humility?
answer: Humility is a Christ-like virtue; we are to esteem others above ourselves and walk humbly with God (Philippians 2:3; Micah 6:8).

question: What does the Bible say about lying?
answer: God commands us not to bear false witness, hates lying lips, and calls believers to put away falsehood and speak the truth in love (Exodus 20:16; Ephesians 4:25).

question: What does the Bible say about stealing?
answer: The eighth commandment forbids stealing; instead, believers are to work and share with those in need (Exodus 20:15; Ephesians 4:28).

question: What does the Bible say about gossip?
answer: The Bible warns against gossip and slander as destructive of friendships and unity, calling us to wholesome speech that builds others up (Proverbs 16:28; Ephesians 4:29).

question: What does the Bible say about forgiveness?
answer: Believers are to forgive others as God in Christ has forgiven them — fully, freely, and from the heart (Ephesians 4:32; Matthew 6:14-15).

question: What does the Bible say about love?
answer: Love is the greatest commandment and the chief evidence of being Jesus' disciple; God's love in Christ is the source and pattern of all true love (1 John 4:7-12).

question: What does the Bible say about friendship?
answer: A friend loves at all times (Proverbs 17:17); true friendship encourages, sharpens, prays, and points one another to Christ.

question: What does the Bible say about parents?
answer: Children are to honor and obey their parents in the Lord (Exodus 20:12; Ephesians 6:1-3); parents are to raise their children in the discipline and instruction of the Lord.

question: What does the Bible say about children?
answer: Children are a gift and heritage from the Lord (Psalm 127:3) and are to be brought up in the training and admonition of the Lord.

question: What does the Bible say about old age?
answer: Gray hair is a crown of glory when found in the way of righteousness (Proverbs 16:31); the elderly are to be honored and their wisdom valued.

question: What does the Bible say about death?
answer: Death entered the world through sin, but Jesus has conquered death through his resurrection; for the believer, to be absent from the body is to be present with the Lord.

question: What does the Bible say about heaven?
answer: Heaven is the eternal presence of God, where the redeemed will see His face, dwell without sin or sorrow, and serve and worship Him forever.

question: What does the Bible say about hell?
answer: Hell is described as a place of conscious, eternal punishment for those who reject God, depicted with images of fire, darkness, and separation from God's blessing.

question: Does God love everyone?
answer: Yes; God's love is for all the world (John 3:16). At the same time, only those who repent and believe in Jesus are saved from condemnation.

question: Why did Jesus have to die?
answer: Jesus had to die because the wages of sin is death; only a perfect, divine substitute could pay that penalty and reconcile sinners to a holy God (Romans 6:23; 2 Corinthians 5:21).

question: How do I know Jesus is the only way?
answer: Jesus himself said, 'I am the way, and the truth, and the life: no one cometh unto the Father, but by me' (John 14:6); Acts 4:12 declares no other name under heaven by which we must be saved.

question: How do I know the Bible is true?
answer: The Bible's truthfulness is supported by its unity, prophetic accuracy, historical reliability, transformed lives, and supremely the testimony of the risen Jesus, who affirmed the Scriptures.

question: What is apologetics?
answer: Apologetics is the discipline of giving a thoughtful defense of the Christian faith — providing reasons for the hope that is in us (1 Peter 3:15).

question: What is theology?
answer: Theology is the study of God and of all things in relation to God, drawn from Scripture and aimed at knowing, loving, and serving Him.

question: What is communion?
answer: Communion (the Lord's Supper) is the ordinance Jesus gave the church, in which believers eat bread and drink the cup remembering his body broken and blood shed for them.

question: How often should I pray?
answer: Scripture calls believers to pray without ceasing (1 Thessalonians 5:17) — to develop a habit of communion with God throughout the day, with set times of focused prayer.

question: How often should I read the Bible?
answer: Believers are encouraged to read and meditate on God's Word daily, allowing it to renew the mind and direct life.

question: What is corporate worship?
answer: Corporate worship is the gathered worship of God's people on the Lord's Day, including prayer, praise, the reading and preaching of Scripture, and the ordinances.

question: What is the importance of the church?
answer: The church is Christ's body and bride, the gathered community where believers worship, hear the Word, receive the ordinances, fellowship, and serve God together.

question: Why should I go to church?
answer: Going to church is commanded for believers (Hebrews 10:25); it builds up your faith, helps you grow in love, and equips you to serve and witness.

question: What is the difference between religion and relationship?
answer: True biblical faith is more than ritual: it is a personal relationship with God through Jesus Christ, who has reconciled us to the Father by his death and resurrection.

question: What does it mean to abide in Christ?
answer: To abide in Christ is to remain in living union with him by faith — drawing daily life from his presence, his Word, and his Spirit, and bearing fruit through him.

question: What is a covenant?
answer: A covenant is a binding relational agreement initiated by God; the Bible records covenants such as those with Noah, Abraham, Moses, David, and the new covenant in Christ.

question: What is the covenant with Abraham?
answer: In the Abrahamic covenant, God promised Abraham land, descendants, and blessing — and that all the families of the earth would be blessed through his seed (Genesis 12; 15; 17).

question: What is the Davidic covenant?
answer: In the Davidic covenant, God promised that David's throne and kingdom would be established forever — fulfilled ultimately in Jesus Christ, the Son of David (2 Samuel 7).

question: What is the Mosaic covenant?
answer: The Mosaic covenant is the covenant God made with Israel through Moses at Sinai, including the Ten Commandments and the law, by which Israel was to live as God's holy nation.

question: What is conversion?
answer: Conversion is turning to God in repentance and faith — a person's response to the gospel by which they are united with Christ for salvation.

question: What is regeneration?
answer: Regeneration is the work of the Holy Spirit who gives new spiritual life to a sinner, enabling repentance and faith — being 'born again'.

question: What does it mean to be in Christ?
answer: To be 'in Christ' is to be united by faith to Jesus, sharing in his death, resurrection, righteousness, and life — the basis of every spiritual blessing.

question: What is a saint?
answer: In the New Testament, all believers are called saints — those set apart by God in Christ for His holy purposes.

question: What is the Day of the Lord?
answer: The Day of the Lord refers to a future time of God's decisive intervention in history — judgment for the wicked and salvation for His people, culminating in Christ's return.

question: Why is Jesus called the Lamb of God?
answer: Because, like the Passover lamb and the Old Testament sacrifices, Jesus' blood was shed to take away the sin of the world (John 1:29).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'I am the way'?
answer: Jesus meant that he is the only path of access to God the Father — that no one comes to the Father except through him (John 14:6).

question: What is the gospel in one sentence?
answer: The gospel is the good news that Jesus Christ — the Son of God — lived a perfect life, died for our sins, rose from the dead, and offers eternal life to all who repent and believe.

question: What is the Roman road to salvation?
answer: The Roman road is a presentation of the gospel from Romans: all have sinned (3:23), the wages of sin is death (6:23), Christ died for us (5:8), confess Jesus as Lord and believe in his resurrection to be saved (10:9-10).

question: What is the ABCs of becoming a Christian?
answer: A common summary: Admit you are a sinner; Believe Jesus is Lord and rose from the dead; Confess him with your mouth and surrender your life to him.

question: What are some names of God in the Bible?
answer: Jehovah, El Shaddai (God Almighty), El Elyon (God Most High), Adonai (Lord), Jehovah-Jireh (the Lord will provide), Jehovah-Shalom (the Lord is peace), Father, and many others.

question: What does Emmanuel mean?
answer: Emmanuel (or Immanuel) means 'God with us,' a name given to Jesus showing that God himself dwells among His people.

question: Why is Jesus called the Word?
answer: Jesus is called the Word (Logos) because he is the eternal expression and revelation of God; in him the unseen God is made known (John 1:1, 14).

question: What are the parables of Jesus?
answer: Parables are short, vivid stories Jesus told to illustrate truths about God's kingdom — such as the sower, the good Samaritan, the prodigal son, and the lost sheep.

question: What are the miracles of Jesus?
answer: Jesus performed many miracles — healing the sick, raising the dead, calming storms, feeding multitudes, casting out demons — testifying that he was the Son of God.

question: How many disciples did Jesus have?
answer: Jesus had many followers, but he chose twelve as his apostles, the foundational witnesses of his ministry, death, and resurrection.

question: What is meant by 'taking up your cross'?
answer: Taking up your cross means daily denying yourself, dying to sin and self-will, and following Jesus wherever he leads — even to suffering for his sake.

question: What is the kingdom of priests?
answer: In Exodus 19:6 God called Israel a kingdom of priests; in 1 Peter 2:9 the church is called a royal priesthood — God's people who mediate His glory to the world.

question: Who are the patriarchs?
answer: The patriarchs are the fathers of the Hebrew people: especially Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob (Israel), with whom God made His foundational covenants.

question: Who are the twelve tribes of Israel?
answer: The twelve tribes of Israel descend from Jacob's twelve sons: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Judah, Dan, Naphtali, Gad, Asher, Issachar, Zebulun, Joseph (Ephraim and Manasseh), and Benjamin.

question: What is the difference between the Torah and the Talmud?
answer: The Torah is the first five books of the Bible (inspired Scripture); the Talmud is a later Jewish compilation of rabbinic teachings and discussions on the law (not Scripture).

question: Who were the major prophets?
answer: The major prophets are Isaiah, Jeremiah (with Lamentations), Ezekiel, and Daniel — so called because of the length of their books.

question: Who were the minor prophets?
answer: The minor prophets are Hosea, Joel, Amos, Obadiah, Jonah, Micah, Nahum, Habakkuk, Zephaniah, Haggai, Zechariah, and Malachi — so called for the brevity of their books.

question: What is the Apocrypha?
answer: The Apocrypha is a collection of Jewish writings produced between the Old and New Testaments; some Christian traditions include them in the Bible, while Protestants do not consider them inspired Scripture.

question: What is canon of Scripture?
answer: The canon of Scripture is the recognized list of inspired books — the 66 books of the Bible — accepted as the rule of faith and practice for the Church.

question: What does inerrancy mean?
answer: Inerrancy means that the Bible, in its original manuscripts, is without error in all that it affirms — historically, doctrinally, and morally.

question: What is hermeneutics?
answer: Hermeneutics is the science and art of interpreting Scripture rightly — understanding what God communicates through the biblical authors in their original context.

question: What is the difference between law and gospel?
answer: The law commands what we must do and exposes sin; the gospel announces what God has done in Christ to save sinners — the law convicts, the gospel saves.

question: What is legalism?
answer: Legalism is the error of seeking acceptance with God by keeping rules instead of trusting in Christ alone — or imposing extra-biblical rules on others.

question: What is antinomianism?
answer: Antinomianism (literally 'against law') is the error of denying that God's moral law is a guide for the Christian life, often resulting in license to sin.

question: What is the assurance of salvation?
answer: Assurance of salvation is the believer's confident knowledge of being saved, grounded in God's promises, the work of the Spirit, and the fruit of new life.

question: What is heaven like?
answer: Heaven is described as a place of unending joy, perfect holiness, and direct communion with God — without sin, sorrow, sickness, or death (Revelation 21-22).

question: Will I see loved ones in heaven?
answer: Believers in Christ will be reunited together in the Lord's presence; Scripture gives us strong hope of joyful fellowship in the resurrection (1 Thessalonians 4:13-18).

question: How can I know God personally?
answer: You can know God personally by repenting of your sins, believing in Jesus Christ as your Lord and Savior, and entering daily fellowship with Him through His Word and prayer.

question: What is sanctification by the Spirit?
answer: It is the Holy Spirit's ongoing work in a believer to transform character, kill sin, and produce Christlike holiness.

question: What does it mean that God is sovereign?
answer: It means God reigns over all creation and history; nothing happens outside His knowledge, plan, or permission.

question: How do I trust God in suffering?
answer: By remembering His character, clinging to His promises, pouring out your heart in prayer, leaning on His Word, and resting in the truth that He works all things for good (Romans 8:28).

question: What is the gospel of grace?
answer: The gospel of grace is the good news that salvation is God's free gift in Christ — not earned by works, but received by faith, to the praise of His glorious grace.

question: What is the bride of Christ?
answer: The bride of Christ is the church — the redeemed people of God, joined to Jesus in covenant love, awaiting the marriage supper of the Lamb.

question: What is the priesthood of Christ?
answer: Christ is our great High Priest who offered himself once for all on the cross and now intercedes for us at the right hand of the Father (Hebrews 7-10).

question: What is the throne of grace?
answer: The throne of grace is the seat of God's gracious rule, where believers may come boldly through Jesus to find mercy and grace to help in time of need (Hebrews 4:16).

question: What does it mean to walk in the Spirit?
answer: To walk in the Spirit is to live each day in dependence on the Holy Spirit, yielded to His leading, so as not to fulfill the desires of the flesh (Galatians 5:16).

question: What is grieving the Holy Spirit?
answer: Grieving the Holy Spirit means wounding Him by sin and disobedience; Ephesians 4:30 commands us not to grieve the Spirit of God.

question: What is quenching the Spirit?
answer: Quenching the Spirit means stifling or resisting His work in our lives; 1 Thessalonians 5:19 commands believers not to quench the Spirit.

question: What is the unforgivable sin?
answer: Jesus called blasphemy against the Holy Spirit unforgivable (Matthew 12:31-32) — a settled, willful rejection of the Spirit's testimony to Christ.

question: What is meant by predestination?
answer: Predestination is God's eternal decree concerning the destiny of His creatures, particularly the truth that He chose His people in Christ before the foundation of the world (Ephesians 1:4-5).

question: What is meant by election?
answer: Election is God's gracious choice of a people for salvation in Christ, not based on their works but on His mercy and purpose (Romans 9:11-16).

question: What does free will mean?
answer: Free will is humanity's God-given capacity to choose; Scripture teaches that fallen sinners freely choose sin until God graciously enables them to choose Christ.

question: What is human depravity?
answer: Human depravity is the doctrine that sin has affected every part of human nature — mind, will, emotions, and body — so that no one can save themselves apart from God's grace.

question: What is the problem of evil?
answer: It is the question of why a good and powerful God allows evil; Scripture affirms God's goodness, sovereignty, and ultimate purpose to overthrow evil through Christ.

question: What is theodicy?
answer: Theodicy is the attempt to defend God's justice and goodness in light of the existence of evil and suffering.

question: What does it mean that Christ is the Mediator?
answer: As Mediator, Jesus is the one who stands between God and humanity to reconcile them — fully God and fully man, the only Mediator of salvation (1 Timothy 2:5).

question: What is the Apostles' Creed?
answer: The Apostles' Creed is an early Christian summary of the faith, affirming belief in the Father, the Son (His incarnation, death, resurrection, ascension, and return), the Holy Spirit, and the Church.

question: What is the Nicene Creed?
answer: The Nicene Creed is a 4th-century Christian creed affirming the deity of Christ and the Trinity, defending the historic faith against heresy.

question: What is the Westminster Confession?
answer: The Westminster Confession of Faith is a 17th-century Reformed statement of doctrine, summarizing the Scripture's teaching on God, salvation, the church, and Christian living.

question: What does idolatry mean?
answer: Idolatry is giving to anything other than God the worship, trust, or supreme love that belongs to Him alone.

question: What is blasphemy?
answer: Blasphemy is speaking irreverently or contemptuously against God; the third commandment forbids taking God's name in vain.

question: What does sanctified mean?
answer: Sanctified means set apart as holy unto God — believers are sanctified positionally in Christ and progressively in daily life by the Spirit.

question: What does justified mean?
answer: To be justified is to be declared righteous before God on the basis of Christ's atoning death and perfect righteousness, received through faith.

question: What does adopted mean?
answer: Believers are adopted by God as His children through faith in Christ, gaining the rights and privileges of sonship in His family (Romans 8:15).

question: What does it mean to deny yourself?
answer: To deny yourself is to refuse to live by your own sinful desires and to submit your will to the lordship of Jesus Christ.

question: What does crucified with Christ mean?
answer: It means that by union with Jesus, the believer's old self has died with him, and a new life has begun, lived by faith in the Son of God (Galatians 2:20).

question: How do I overcome sin?
answer: By relying on the Holy Spirit, putting on Christ, abiding in His Word, fleeing temptation, confessing sin quickly, and walking in fellowship with God's people.

question: What is the difference between an apostle and a prophet?
answer: An apostle was sent with authority by Christ to proclaim the gospel and lay the foundation of the church; a prophet spoke God's revealed message to His people.

question: Who is Lucifer?
answer: Lucifer is a Latin term meaning 'shining one' or 'morning star' (from Isaiah 14:12), often interpreted as a reference to Satan in his pre-fall state.

question: Was Satan once an angel?
answer: Many Christians, drawing on passages such as Isaiah 14 and Ezekiel 28, believe Satan was once a created angel who rebelled against God and was cast down.

question: What does the Bible say about end times?
answer: The Bible teaches that history is moving toward Christ's return, when he will defeat all evil, raise the dead, judge the world, and bring in a new heaven and new earth.

question: What are the signs of Christ's coming?
answer: Jesus spoke of wars, famines, false christs, persecution, the gospel preached to all nations, and great distress as signs that point toward his return (Matthew 24).

question: How should we live waiting for Christ's return?
answer: We should live in faithfulness, holiness, watchfulness, and active service — being ready, occupying until he comes, and keeping our lamps trimmed.

question: What does Maranatha mean?
answer: Maranatha is an Aramaic word meaning 'Our Lord, come!' (1 Corinthians 16:22), expressing the church's longing for Christ's return.

question: What is the meaning of Selah?
answer: Selah is a term used in the Psalms whose precise meaning is uncertain; it likely indicates a pause for reflection or a musical interlude.

question: What is a psalm?
answer: A psalm is an inspired song or prayer, especially in the Old Testament book of Psalms — used by God's people for praise, lament, thanksgiving, and worship.

question: What is a proverb?
answer: A proverb is a short, memorable saying that gives wise instruction; the book of Proverbs collects such sayings to teach skillful living under God.

question: What is wisdom literature in the Bible?
answer: Wisdom literature includes books such as Job, Proverbs, Ecclesiastes, Song of Solomon, and parts of the Psalms — addressing God's truth as it applies to human experience.

question: What is biblical poetry?
answer: Biblical poetry uses parallelism, imagery, and rhythm to communicate truth — found especially in Psalms, Proverbs, Job, Lamentations, and the prophets.

question: What is a covenant of grace?
answer: The covenant of grace is God's overarching plan to save sinners through Jesus Christ, unfolded across history from Eden to the new creation.

question: How do God's promises help me?
answer: God's promises anchor your faith, comfort your heart, guide your decisions, and remind you that He is faithful and will never fail those who trust in Him.

question: What is the believer's hope?
answer: The believer's hope is the certain expectation of being with Christ — through resurrection and glorification — and reigning with him in the new heavens and new earth.

question: What does it mean to glorify God?
answer: To glorify God is to recognize, declare, and reflect His worth — through worship, obedience, and lives that point others to Him.

question: How do I show love to God?
answer: By trusting Him, obeying His commandments, worshiping Him, communing with Him in prayer and Word, and loving the people He has made.

question: How do I love my neighbor?
answer: By treating others with kindness, justice, and compassion; meeting their needs as you would want yours met; sharing the gospel; and bearing their burdens in love.

question: What is biblical meekness?
answer: Biblical meekness is strength under God's control — humility, gentleness, and submission to God that does not retaliate when wronged.

question: What does it mean to count the cost?
answer: It means soberly considering the demands of following Jesus — denying self, taking up the cross — and committing to follow him whatever it requires (Luke 14:25-33).

question: What does it mean to be salt and light?
answer: It means living in such a way that your life preserves what is good and shines truth into the world, pointing others to glorify your Father in heaven (Matthew 5:13-16).

question: What are the offices of Christ?
answer: Christ holds three offices: Prophet (revealing God), Priest (atoning and interceding), and King (ruling over His kingdom).

question: What does the Bible say about gambling?
answer: While not directly named, biblical principles warn against the love of money, covetousness, dishonest gain, and putting trust in chance rather than God.

question: What does the Bible say about alcohol?
answer: Scripture allows the moderate use of wine but strongly warns against drunkenness and any drinking that causes others to stumble (Ephesians 5:18; Proverbs 20:1).

question: What does the Bible say about modesty?
answer: Believers are to dress and live with humility and self-control, adorning themselves with good works and the inner beauty of a gentle and quiet spirit (1 Timothy 2:9-10; 1 Peter 3:3-4).

question: What does the Bible say about the tongue?
answer: The tongue is a small but powerful member that can bless and curse; we are urged to bridle it, speak truth in love, and let our speech be gracious (James 3; Ephesians 4:29).

question: What does the Bible say about jealousy?
answer: Sinful jealousy is condemned as a work of the flesh; godly jealousy — like God's holy jealousy for His people — is a different thing entirely (Galatians 5:20; Exodus 20:5).

question: What does the Bible say about envy?
answer: Envy is named among the works of the flesh and is to be put off; love does not envy but rejoices when others are blessed (Galatians 5:21; 1 Corinthians 13:4).

question: What does the Bible say about laziness?
answer: Scripture warns repeatedly against sloth; the diligent are rewarded, while laziness leads to poverty and trouble (Proverbs 6:6-11; 13:4).

question: What does the Bible say about hard work?
answer: Scripture commends diligent labor, working as unto the Lord, providing for one's family, and serving others through honest work (Colossians 3:23; 1 Thessalonians 4:11-12).

question: What does the Bible say about contentment?
answer: Believers are called to be content in every state, finding sufficiency in Christ rather than circumstances or possessions (Philippians 4:11-13; Hebrews 13:5).

question: What does the Bible say about generosity?
answer: God loves a cheerful giver; we are to share with those in need, rich in good works, ready to give and to communicate (2 Corinthians 9:7; 1 Timothy 6:18).

question: What does the Bible say about hospitality?
answer: Believers are urged to practice hospitality, welcoming strangers and one another, knowing that some have entertained angels unawares (Romans 12:13; Hebrews 13:2).

question: What does the Bible say about discipline of children?
answer: Parents are urged to discipline their children in love and not provoke them to anger, training them in the discipline and instruction of the Lord (Proverbs 13:24; Ephesians 6:4).

question: What does the Bible say about loving enemies?
answer: Jesus commands us to love our enemies, do good to those who hate us, bless those who curse us, and pray for those who persecute us (Matthew 5:44; Romans 12:20).

question: What does the Bible say about revenge?
answer: Believers are not to take revenge but to leave room for God's wrath; vengeance belongs to the Lord (Romans 12:19).

question: What does the Bible say about justice?
answer: God loves justice and calls His people to do justly, defend the weak, and uphold the cause of the oppressed (Micah 6:8; Isaiah 1:17).

question: What does the Bible say about the poor?
answer: God commands His people to care for the poor; defending them, giving generously, and judging righteously is true religion before the Lord (James 1:27; Proverbs 19:17).

question: What does the Bible say about widows and orphans?
answer: God is the Father of the fatherless and Defender of widows; His people are to care for them and uphold their cause (Psalm 68:5; James 1:27).

question: What does the Bible say about strangers?
answer: Israel was commanded to love the stranger, remembering they were once strangers in Egypt; the New Testament urges hospitality toward all (Leviticus 19:34; Hebrews 13:2).

question: What does the Bible say about government?
answer: Christians are to honor and submit to governing authorities as God's servants, obey just laws, pray for rulers, and obey God rather than men when commands conflict (Romans 13:1-7; Acts 5:29).

question: What does the Bible say about war?
answer: While Scripture acknowledges the realities of war and lawful defense, believers are also called to be peacemakers and to long for the day when wars will cease (Isaiah 2:4; Matthew 5:9).

question: What does the Bible say about peace?
answer: True peace begins with peace with God through Christ and overflows in peace with others; God calls His people to pursue peace with all (Romans 5:1; Hebrews 12:14).

question: Who was Methuselah and how did he live longest?
answer: Methuselah was the son of Enoch and grandfather of Noah; he lived 969 years (Genesis 5:27), the longest lifespan in the Bible.

question: Who was Enoch and what was unique about him?
answer: Enoch was the father of Methuselah; he walked with God and was taken without seeing death (Genesis 5:24).

question: Who was Noah?
answer: Noah was a righteous man whom God chose to build an ark and preserve life through the great flood; he became the father of all post-flood humanity.

question: Who was Abraham?
answer: Abraham was the patriarch chosen by God, called from Ur, given covenant promises of land, descendants, and blessing for all nations through his offspring.

question: Who was Sarah?
answer: Sarah was Abraham's wife and mother of Isaac, who bore the child of promise in old age and is honored among the women of faith.

question: Who was Isaac?
answer: Isaac was the son of Abraham and Sarah, the child of promise, who married Rebekah and fathered Esau and Jacob.

question: Who was Jacob?
answer: Jacob was the younger son of Isaac and Rebekah; he wrestled with God and was renamed Israel, becoming father of the twelve tribes.

question: Who was Joseph?
answer: Joseph was the favored son of Jacob, sold into Egypt by his brothers, who rose to be governor of Egypt and saved his family from famine.

question: Who was Moses?
answer: Moses was the lawgiver and prophet who led Israel out of Egypt, received the Ten Commandments, and brought the people to the edge of the Promised Land.

question: Who was Aaron?
answer: Aaron was Moses' older brother and the first high priest of Israel, set apart with his sons to serve in the tabernacle.

question: Who was Miriam?
answer: Miriam was the older sister of Moses and Aaron, a prophetess who led the women in song after the Red Sea crossing.

question: Who was Joshua?
answer: Joshua was Moses' assistant and successor who led Israel into the Promised Land and oversaw its conquest and division among the tribes.

question: Who was Caleb?
answer: Caleb was one of the twelve spies sent into Canaan; with Joshua he gave a faithful report and was rewarded with an inheritance in the land.

question: Who was Rahab?
answer: Rahab was a harlot of Jericho who hid the Hebrew spies and was spared with her household; she is named in Christ's genealogy.

question: Who was Deborah?
answer: Deborah was a prophetess and judge of Israel who, with Barak, led the army against the Canaanite general Sisera.

question: Who was Gideon?
answer: Gideon was a judge of Israel whom God used to defeat the Midianites with only 300 men, demonstrating God's power to save.

question: Who was Samson?
answer: Samson was a Nazarite judge of Israel of great strength who fought the Philistines; betrayed by Delilah, he died bringing down their temple.

question: Who was Ruth?
answer: Ruth was a Moabite woman who clung to her Hebrew mother-in-law Naomi, married Boaz, and became the great-grandmother of King David.

question: Who was Boaz?
answer: Boaz was a wealthy and godly man of Bethlehem who acted as kinsman-redeemer for Ruth and Naomi, marrying Ruth into the line of David.

question: Who was Samuel?
answer: Samuel was the last judge and a great prophet of Israel, who anointed both Saul and David as kings.

question: Who was Saul?
answer: Saul was the first king of Israel, anointed by Samuel; he reigned over Israel but was rejected for his disobedience.

question: Who was David?
answer: David was the second king of Israel, a man after God's own heart, the slayer of Goliath, sweet psalmist of Israel, and ancestor of Jesus the Messiah.

question: Who was Jonathan?
answer: Jonathan was the son of King Saul and the loyal friend of David, sealing a covenant of friendship and loving him as his own soul.

question: Who was Solomon?
answer: Solomon was the son of David, third king of Israel, renowned for wisdom, who built the temple in Jerusalem.

question: Who was Elijah?
answer: Elijah was a great prophet who confronted Ahab and the prophets of Baal, fed by ravens, and was taken to heaven by a whirlwind.

question: Who was Elisha?
answer: Elisha was the prophet who succeeded Elijah and received a double portion of his spirit, performing many miracles in Israel.

question: Who was Isaiah the prophet?
answer: Isaiah was a major prophet of Judah who proclaimed God's holiness, judgment, and the coming Messiah, including the Servant who suffers for our sins.

question: Who was Jeremiah?
answer: Jeremiah was the 'weeping prophet' who warned Judah of coming exile and proclaimed the new covenant God would establish with His people.

question: Who was Daniel?
answer: Daniel was a young Hebrew exile in Babylon who served kings, refused to compromise his faith, and received great prophetic visions.

question: Who was Ezra?
answer: Ezra was a priest and scribe who led a group of exiles back to Jerusalem and taught the law to a renewed people.

question: Who was Nehemiah?
answer: Nehemiah was the cup-bearer of Artaxerxes who returned to Jerusalem and led the rebuilding of the city walls in fifty-two days.

question: Who was Esther?
answer: Esther was a Jewish orphan who became queen of Persia and risked her life to save her people from Haman's plot.

question: Who was Mordecai?
answer: Mordecai was Esther's older cousin who raised her, refused to bow to Haman, and was honored by the king for saving his life.

question: Who was Job?
answer: Job was a righteous man whom God allowed Satan to test through the loss of his wealth, children, and health, yet who held fast to his integrity.

question: Who was John the Baptist?
answer: John the Baptist was the forerunner of Christ who called Israel to repentance, baptized Jesus in the Jordan, and was beheaded by Herod.

question: Who was Mary the mother of Jesus?
answer: Mary was the virgin betrothed to Joseph, who, by the Holy Spirit, conceived and gave birth to Jesus the Son of God.

question: Who was Joseph the husband of Mary?
answer: Joseph was a carpenter of Nazareth, a righteous man of David's line, who took Mary as his wife and became Jesus' adoptive earthly father.

question: Who was Peter?
answer: Peter was the fisherman called Simon, named Peter (the Rock) by Jesus, an apostle and leader in the early church.

question: Who was Andrew?
answer: Andrew was the brother of Peter, a fisherman, originally a disciple of John the Baptist, who first brought Peter to Jesus.

question: Who was James the son of Zebedee?
answer: James was a fisherman and brother of John the apostle, one of the inner three with Jesus, the first apostle martyred by Herod Agrippa.

question: Who was John the apostle?
answer: John was the brother of James, a fisherman, the disciple whom Jesus loved, who wrote the Gospel of John, three epistles, and Revelation.

question: Who was Matthew?
answer: Matthew (Levi) was a tax collector called by Jesus to be an apostle and the writer of the Gospel of Matthew.

question: Who was Thomas?
answer: Thomas was an apostle, also called Didymus, who at first doubted the resurrection but confessed Jesus as 'My Lord and my God.'

question: Who was Judas Iscariot?
answer: Judas Iscariot was the apostle who betrayed Jesus to the chief priests for thirty pieces of silver and afterward hanged himself.

question: Who was Paul (Saul of Tarsus)?
answer: Paul was a Pharisee who persecuted the church until he met the risen Christ on the road to Damascus and became the great apostle to the Gentiles.

question: Who was Barnabas?
answer: Barnabas was a Levite from Cyprus, an early disciple known as 'son of encouragement,' a missionary partner of Paul.

question: Who was Silas?
answer: Silas was a leader in the Jerusalem church and a missionary companion of Paul, imprisoned with him at Philippi.

question: Who was Timothy?
answer: Timothy was Paul's young protégé and son in the faith, raised by his godly mother Eunice and grandmother Lois, to whom Paul wrote two letters.

question: Who was Titus?
answer: Titus was a Greek convert and trusted companion of Paul, sent to organize churches on Crete, to whom Paul wrote a pastoral letter.

question: Who was Luke?
answer: Luke was a physician and traveling companion of Paul, the author of the Gospel of Luke and the book of Acts.

question: Who was Mark?
answer: Mark (John Mark) was a young disciple, cousin of Barnabas, and the author of the Gospel of Mark, closely associated with Peter.

question: Who was Apollos?
answer: Apollos was a learned Jewish Christian from Alexandria, mighty in the Scriptures, instructed more fully by Aquila and Priscilla.

question: Who were Aquila and Priscilla?
answer: Aquila and Priscilla were a tent-making couple, fellow workers with Paul, who hosted churches and discipled Apollos.

question: Who was Stephen?
answer: Stephen was one of the seven chosen to serve, full of grace and power, who became the first Christian martyr after preaching to the Sanhedrin.

question: Who was Philip the evangelist?
answer: Philip was one of the seven, an evangelist who preached in Samaria and led the Ethiopian eunuch to Christ.

question: Who was Cornelius?
answer: Cornelius was a Roman centurion of the Italian Cohort, a devout God-fearer to whom Peter preached, marking the gospel's spread to the Gentiles.

question: Who was Lazarus of Bethany?
answer: Lazarus was the brother of Mary and Martha whom Jesus raised from the dead after he had been four days in the tomb.

question: Who was Mary Magdalene?
answer: Mary Magdalene was a follower of Jesus from whom he had cast out seven demons; she was the first to see the risen Christ.

question: Who was Pontius Pilate?
answer: Pontius Pilate was the Roman governor of Judaea who tried Jesus, declared him innocent, but yielded to the crowd and ordered his crucifixion.

question: Who was Herod the Great?
answer: Herod the Great was the king of Judaea at Jesus' birth who, fearing the new king, ordered the slaughter of the male children of Bethlehem.

question: Who was Caiaphas?
answer: Caiaphas was the high priest who presided over Jesus' Jewish trial and prophesied that one man should die for the people.

question: Who was Nicodemus?
answer: Nicodemus was a Pharisee and ruler of the Jews who came to Jesus by night, heard 'You must be born again,' and later helped bury him.

question: Who was Joseph of Arimathea?
answer: Joseph of Arimathea was a rich, righteous member of the council and secret disciple who placed Jesus' body in his own new tomb.

question: Who was Lydia?
answer: Lydia was a seller of purple in Philippi, the first European convert under Paul's ministry, who hosted the new church in her home.

question: Who was Onesimus?
answer: Onesimus was a runaway slave converted under Paul in Rome and sent back to his master Philemon as a beloved brother.

question: Who was the prodigal son?
answer: The prodigal son is the figure in Jesus' parable (Luke 15) who squandered his inheritance, repented, and was received back by his loving father.

question: Who was the good Samaritan?
answer: The good Samaritan is the figure in Jesus' parable (Luke 10) who showed mercy to a wounded stranger when others passed by.

question: Who was the rich young ruler?
answer: The rich young ruler is the man who came to Jesus seeking eternal life but went away sorrowful, unwilling to part with his great possessions (Matthew 19).

question: Who was Zacchaeus?
answer: Zacchaeus was a wealthy chief tax collector in Jericho who climbed a sycamore tree to see Jesus and joyfully welcomed him into his home.

question: Who was Bartimaeus?
answer: Bartimaeus was a blind beggar near Jericho whose faith Jesus rewarded by giving him sight.

question: Who was the woman at the well?
answer: The woman at the well was the Samaritan whom Jesus met at Jacob's well, to whom he revealed living water and his identity as the Messiah.

question: Who was Simon of Cyrene?
answer: Simon of Cyrene was the man compelled by the Romans to carry Jesus' cross on the way to Golgotha (Matthew 27:32).

question: Who was Martha?
answer: Martha was the sister of Mary and Lazarus of Bethany, known for her hospitality and her great confession of faith in Jesus as the Messiah.

question: Who was Mary of Bethany?
answer: Mary of Bethany was the sister of Martha and Lazarus, who sat at Jesus' feet and anointed him with costly perfume before his death.

question: Who was Salome?
answer: Salome was a follower of Jesus, mother of James and John, who came to anoint Jesus' body and witnessed the empty tomb.

question: Who was Dorcas?
answer: Dorcas (Tabitha) was a disciple at Joppa full of good works, raised to life by Peter (Acts 9:36-42).

question: Who was Eutychus?
answer: Eutychus was the young man who fell from a third-story window during Paul's long sermon in Troas and was raised to life (Acts 20:9-12).

question: Who was Agabus?
answer: Agabus was a New Testament prophet who foretold a famine and the binding of Paul in Jerusalem (Acts 11:28; 21:10-11).

question: Who was Felix?
answer: Felix was the Roman governor of Judaea who heard Paul's defense at Caesarea but kept him bound for two years hoping for a bribe.

question: Who was Festus?
answer: Festus was the Roman governor who succeeded Felix, before whom Paul stood and appealed to Caesar.

question: Who was King Agrippa?
answer: King Agrippa II was the Jewish king before whom Paul gave his defense, almost persuaded to be a Christian (Acts 26:28).

question: Who was Demas?
answer: Demas was a former companion of Paul who, having loved this present world, forsook him and departed to Thessalonica (2 Timothy 4:10).

question: Who was Diotrephes?
answer: Diotrephes was a man rebuked in 3 John for his pride and refusal to receive the brethren.

question: Who was the woman caught in adultery?
answer: She was the woman brought to Jesus to be stoned, whom he forgave and told to go and sin no more (John 8).

question: Who was the centurion at the cross?
answer: He was the Roman centurion who, witnessing Jesus' death, declared, 'Truly this was the Son of God' (Matthew 27:54).

question: Who was the thief on the cross who repented?
answer: He was one of the criminals crucified with Jesus, who confessed his guilt and asked Jesus to remember him; Jesus promised him paradise (Luke 23:42-43).

question: Who was Hannah?
answer: Hannah was the wife of Elkanah and mother of Samuel; she prayed earnestly for a son and dedicated him to the Lord.

question: Who was Eli?
answer: Eli was the high priest at Shiloh under whose care young Samuel served; he failed to restrain his wicked sons and the ark was lost in his time.

question: Who were Eli's wicked sons?
answer: Eli's sons Hophni and Phinehas were corrupt priests who despised the Lord's offering and were slain in battle (1 Samuel 2; 4).

question: Who was Goliath?
answer: Goliath was the Philistine giant of Gath, defying Israel, whom David slew with a sling and a stone in the name of the Lord.

question: Who was Bathsheba?
answer: Bathsheba was the wife of Uriah whom David took in adultery; she later became David's wife and the mother of Solomon.

question: Who was Uriah the Hittite?
answer: Uriah the Hittite was a faithful soldier of David and the husband of Bathsheba, whom David sent to die in battle.

question: Who was Nathan the prophet?
answer: Nathan was a prophet during the reigns of David and Solomon; he confronted David's sin with the parable of the ewe lamb.

question: Who was Absalom?
answer: Absalom was David's beautiful but rebellious son who led a coup against his father and died caught by his hair in an oak.

question: Who was Joab?
answer: Joab was David's nephew and commander of his army; a fierce warrior who often acted ruthlessly, eventually slain by Solomon's order.

question: Who was Jezebel?
answer: Jezebel was the wicked Phoenician wife of King Ahab who promoted Baal worship and persecuted the prophets of the Lord.

question: Who was Ahab?
answer: Ahab was the wicked king of Israel, husband of Jezebel, who led Israel into Baal worship and was confronted by Elijah.

question: Who was Naaman?
answer: Naaman was a Syrian commander cured of leprosy when he obeyed Elisha's command to wash seven times in the Jordan.

question: Who was Hezekiah?
answer: Hezekiah was a righteous king of Judah who trusted the Lord, removed idolatry, and saw the Assyrian army defeated by the angel of the Lord.

question: Who was Josiah?
answer: Josiah was a righteous king of Judah who, beginning at age eight, led great reforms after the Book of the Law was found in the temple.

question: Who was Manasseh, son of Hezekiah?
answer: Manasseh was a king of Judah who led the people into idolatry; later, in captivity, he humbled himself before God and was restored.

question: Who was Nebuchadnezzar?
answer: Nebuchadnezzar was the Babylonian king who destroyed Jerusalem and exiled Judah; God humbled him until he confessed the Most High.

question: Who was Cyrus?
answer: Cyrus was the Persian king whom Isaiah named in advance and whom God used to issue the decree allowing the Jews to return and rebuild the temple.

question: Who was Belshazzar?
answer: Belshazzar was the last king of Babylon, who saw the writing on the wall and was slain that very night when Babylon fell.

question: Who was Darius?
answer: Darius the Mede became king after Babylon's fall; he reluctantly cast Daniel into the lions' den, then rejoiced at God's deliverance.

question: Who was Haman?
answer: Haman was the proud Agagite who plotted the destruction of the Jews under King Ahasuerus and was hanged on the gallows he made for Mordecai.

question: Who were the wise men?
answer: The wise men (Magi) were Gentiles from the East who came following a star to worship the newborn King and presented gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.

question: Who were the shepherds at Jesus' birth?
answer: The shepherds were keepers of flocks near Bethlehem to whom angels announced the Saviour's birth, and they came to see the babe in the manger.

question: Who was Simeon at the temple?
answer: Simeon was a righteous man in Jerusalem who, by the Holy Spirit, recognized the infant Jesus as the Lord's salvation (Luke 2).

question: Who was Anna the prophetess?
answer: Anna was an aged widow and prophetess who served God in the temple and praised God when she saw the infant Jesus.

question: Who was Mary the mother of James and Joses?
answer: She was one of the women who followed Jesus from Galilee, witnessed the crucifixion, and went to the tomb on resurrection morning.

question: Who was Cleopas?
answer: Cleopas was one of the two disciples to whom the risen Jesus appeared and explained the Scriptures on the road to Emmaus.

question: Who was Tabitha?
answer: Tabitha (Dorcas) was a disciple in Joppa devoted to good works and almsgiving, raised from the dead by Peter.

question: Who was Lydia of Thyatira?
answer: Lydia was a successful businesswoman whose heart the Lord opened to receive Paul's message at Philippi.

question: Who was the Ethiopian eunuch?
answer: He was a high official of Candace, queen of the Ethiopians, who came to Jerusalem to worship and was led to Christ by Philip on the road to Gaza.

question: Who was Sergius Paulus?
answer: Sergius Paulus was the Roman proconsul of Cyprus who believed the gospel through Paul's ministry (Acts 13:7-12).

question: Who was Gallio?
answer: Gallio was the Roman proconsul of Achaia who refused to judge the Jewish charges against Paul in Corinth (Acts 18).

question: Who was Phoebe?
answer: Phoebe was a servant of the church at Cenchreae commended by Paul to the Roman believers (Romans 16:1).

question: Who was Eunice?
answer: Eunice was the godly mother of Timothy who, with grandmother Lois, raised him in unfeigned faith and the Holy Scriptures.

question: Who was Lois?
answer: Lois was Timothy's grandmother, commended for her sincere faith that lived also in Eunice and Timothy (2 Timothy 1:5).

question: Who was Tychicus?
