question: What did Jesus say about the centurion's faith?
answer: I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel (Luke 7:9).

question: What did Jesus say about the woman who anointed his feet?
answer: Her sins, which are many, are forgiven; for she loved much: but to whom little is forgiven, the same loveth little (Luke 7:47).

question: What did Jesus say about hearing and obeying?
answer: My mother and my brethren are these that hear the word of God, and do it (Luke 8:21).

question: What did Jesus say about denying him?
answer: Whosoever shall be ashamed of me and of my words, of him shall the Son of man be ashamed (Luke 9:26).

question: What did Jesus say about the cost of following?
answer: The Son of man hath not where to lay his head (Luke 9:58).

question: What did Jesus say about not looking back?
answer: No man, having put his hand to the plow, and looking back, is fit for the kingdom of God (Luke 9:62).

question: What did Jesus say about the harvest?
answer: The harvest is plenteous, but the laborers are few: pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he send forth laborers (Luke 10:2).

question: What did Jesus say about rejoicing?
answer: Rejoice not that the spirits are subject unto you; but rejoice that your names are written in heaven (Luke 10:20).

question: What did Jesus say about the lawyer's question?
answer: Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart... and thy neighbor as thyself (Luke 10:27).

question: What did Jesus say about the good Samaritan?
answer: Go, and do thou likewise (Luke 10:37).

question: What did Jesus say about Mary and Martha?
answer: Mary hath chosen the good part, which shall not be taken away from her (Luke 10:42).

question: What did Jesus say about persistence in prayer?
answer: Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you (Luke 11:9).

question: What did Jesus say about the Father giving good gifts?
answer: How much more shall your heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to them that ask him? (Luke 11:13).

question: What did Jesus say about clean inside?
answer: Now do ye Pharisees cleanse the outside of the cup and of the platter; but your inward part is full of extortion and wickedness (Luke 11:39).

question: What did Jesus say about the rich fool?
answer: Thou foolish one, this night is thy soul required of thee; and the things which thou hast prepared, whose shall they be? (Luke 12:20).

question: What did Jesus say about being rich toward God?
answer: So is he that layeth up treasure for himself, and is not rich toward God (Luke 12:21).

question: What did Jesus say about ravens and lilies?
answer: Consider the ravens... God feedeth them: of how much more value are ye than the birds! Consider the lilies, how they grow (Luke 12:24, 27).

question: What did Jesus say about the watchful servants?
answer: Blessed are those servants, whom the lord when he cometh shall find watching (Luke 12:37).

question: What did Jesus say about being given much?
answer: Unto whomsoever much is given, of him shall much be required (Luke 12:48).

question: What did Jesus say about reading the times?
answer: Ye know how to interpret the face of the earth and the heaven; but how is it that ye know not how to interpret this time? (Luke 12:56).

question: What did Jesus say about repenting or perishing?
answer: Except ye repent, ye shall all in like manner perish (Luke 13:3, 5).

question: What did Jesus say about the narrow door?
answer: Strive to enter in by the narrow door: for many, I say unto you, shall seek to enter in, and shall not be able (Luke 13:24).

question: What did Jesus say about Jerusalem's rejection?
answer: O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, that killeth the prophets... how often would I have gathered thy children together (Luke 13:34).

question: What did Jesus say about taking the lowest seat?
answer: When thou art bidden of any man to a marriage feast, sit not down in the chief seat... but go and sit down in the lowest place (Luke 14:8, 10).

question: What did Jesus say about counting the cost?
answer: For which of you, desiring to build a tower, doth not first sit down and count the cost? (Luke 14:28).

question: What did Jesus say about the lost sheep?
answer: There shall be joy in heaven over one sinner that repenteth, more than over ninety and nine righteous persons (Luke 15:7).

question: What did Jesus say about wealth and faithfulness?
answer: He that is faithful in a very little is faithful also in much (Luke 16:10).

question: What did Jesus say about serving God or money?
answer: No servant can serve two masters... ye cannot serve God and mammon (Luke 16:13).

question: What did Jesus say about offending people?
answer: It is impossible but that occasions of stumbling should come; but woe unto him, through whom they come! (Luke 17:1).

question: What did Jesus say about forgiving seven times?
answer: If thy brother sin, rebuke him; and if he repent, forgive him. And if he sin against thee seven times in the day, and seven times turn again to thee, saying, I repent; thou shalt forgive him (Luke 17:3-4).

question: What did Jesus say about faith as a mustard seed?
answer: If ye had faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye would say unto this sycamine tree, Be thou rooted up, and be thou planted in the sea; and it would have obeyed you (Luke 17:6).

question: What did Jesus say about being unprofitable servants?
answer: When ye shall have done all the things that are commanded you, say, We are unprofitable servants; we have done that which it was our duty to do (Luke 17:10).

question: What did Jesus say about the kingdom within?
answer: The kingdom of God is within you (Luke 17:21).

question: What did Jesus say about losing one's life?
answer: Whosoever shall seek to gain his life shall lose it: but whosoever shall lose his life shall preserve it (Luke 17:33).

question: What did Jesus say about persistent prayer?
answer: He spake a parable unto them to the end that they ought always to pray, and not to faint (Luke 18:1).

question: What did Jesus say about the Pharisee and publican?
answer: This man went down to his house justified rather than the other: for every one that exalteth himself shall be humbled (Luke 18:14).

question: What did Jesus say about receiving the kingdom as a child?
answer: Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom of God as a little child, he shall in no wise enter therein (Luke 18:17).

question: What did Jesus say about all things being possible?
answer: The things which are impossible with men are possible with God (Luke 18:27).

question: What did Jesus say about lifting up the Son of Man?
answer: As Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, even so must the Son of man be lifted up (John 3:14).

question: What did Jesus say about coming to him?
answer: Him that cometh to me I will in no wise cast out (John 6:37).

question: What did Jesus say about the Father drawing?
answer: No man can come to me, except the Father that sent me draw him (John 6:44).

question: What did Jesus say about the Son of Man's flesh and blood?
answer: Except ye eat the flesh of the Son of man and drink his blood, ye have not life in yourselves (John 6:53).

question: What did Jesus say about the words being spirit and life?
answer: The words that I have spoken unto you are spirit, and are life (John 6:63).

question: What did Jesus say about the Spirit's gift?
answer: He that believeth on me, as the scripture hath said, from within him shall flow rivers of living water (John 7:38).

question: What did Jesus say about hating the world's hate?
answer: If the world hateth you, ye know that it hath hated me before it hated you (John 15:18).

question: What did Jesus say about being chosen?
answer: Ye did not choose me, but I chose you, and appointed you, that ye should go and bear fruit (John 15:16).

question: What did Jesus say about asking in his name?
answer: Whatsoever ye shall ask the Father in my name, he may give it you (John 15:16).

question: What did Jesus say about overcoming the world?
answer: Be of good cheer; I have overcome the world (John 16:33).

question: What did Jesus say about the disciples' sorrow turning to joy?
answer: Your sorrow shall be turned into joy (John 16:20).

question: What did Jesus pray for the Father to glorify him?
answer: Father, the hour is come; glorify thy Son, that the Son may glorify thee (John 17:1).

question: What did Jesus pray about eternal life?
answer: This is life eternal, that they should know thee the only true God, and him whom thou didst send, even Jesus Christ (John 17:3).

question: What did Jesus pray about his disciples?
answer: I pray not for the world, but for those whom thou hast given me; for they are thine (John 17:9).

question: What did Jesus pray about the Word?
answer: Sanctify them in the truth: thy word is truth (John 17:17).

question: What did Jesus pray about future believers?
answer: Neither for these only do I pray, but for them also that believe on me through their word (John 17:20).

question: What did Jesus pray about being one?
answer: That they may all be one; even as thou, Father, art in me, and I in thee (John 17:21).

question: What did Jesus say about glory before the world?
answer: Now, Father, glorify thou me with thine own self with the glory which I had with thee before the world was (John 17:5).

question: What did Jesus say to those who arrested him?
answer: Whom seek ye? They answered him, Jesus of Nazareth. Jesus saith unto them, I am he (John 18:5).

question: What did Jesus say to Peter about putting up his sword?
answer: Put up the sword into the sheath: the cup which the Father hath given me, shall I not drink it? (John 18:11).

question: What did Jesus say about his kingdom not of this world?
answer: My kingdom is not of this world: if my kingdom were of this world, then would my servants fight (John 18:36).

question: What did Jesus say about hearing his voice?
answer: Every one that is of the truth heareth my voice (John 18:37).

question: What did Jesus say about Peter's denial?
answer: Verily I say unto thee, that this night, before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice (Matthew 26:34).

question: What did Jesus say about Judas's betrayal?
answer: It were good for that man if he had not been born (Matthew 26:24).

question: What did Jesus say to the women on the way to the cross?
answer: Daughters of Jerusalem, weep not for me, but weep for yourselves, and for your children (Luke 23:28).

question: What did Jesus say to the thief on the cross?
answer: Verily I say unto thee, To-day shalt thou be with me in Paradise (Luke 23:43).

question: What did Jesus say to his mother from the cross?
answer: Woman, behold thy son! Then saith he to the disciple, Behold thy mother! (John 19:26-27).

question: What were Jesus' final words on the cross?
answer: Father, into thy hands I commend my spirit (Luke 23:46); and 'It is finished' (John 19:30).

question: What did Jesus say to Mary at the tomb?
answer: Woman, why weepest thou? Whom seekest thou? (John 20:15) — and then 'Mary' when she recognized him.

question: What did Jesus say to Thomas about doubt?
answer: Be not faithless, but believing... blessed are they that have not seen, and yet have believed (John 20:27, 29).

question: What did Jesus say to Peter about Peter's death?
answer: When thou shalt be old, thou shalt stretch forth thy hands, and another shall gird thee, and carry thee whither thou wouldest not (John 21:18).

question: What did Jesus say about loving more than these?
answer: Simon, son of John, lovest thou me more than these? (John 21:15).

question: What did Jesus say about feeding his sheep?
answer: Feed my lambs... Tend my sheep... Feed my sheep (John 21:15-17).

question: What did Jesus say about following him?
answer: What is that to thee? Follow thou me (John 21:22).

question: What did Jesus say about not knowing the day of his return?
answer: It is not for you to know times or seasons, which the Father hath set within his own authority (Acts 1:7).

question: What did Jesus say about being witnesses?
answer: Ye shall be my witnesses both in Jerusalem, and in all Judaea and Samaria, and unto the uttermost part of the earth (Acts 1:8).

question: What did Jesus say in the Great Commission?
answer: Go ye therefore, and make disciples of all the nations, baptizing them into the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit, teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I commanded you (Matthew 28:19-20).

question: What did Jesus say about being with us always?
answer: Lo, I am with you always, even unto the end of the world (Matthew 28:20).

question: What did Jesus say about a sign of his coming?
answer: As the lightning cometh forth from the east, and is seen even unto the west; so shall be the coming of the Son of man (Matthew 24:27).

question: What did Jesus say about heaven and earth passing?
answer: Heaven and earth shall pass away, but my words shall not pass away (Matthew 24:35).

question: What did Jesus say about being faithful?
answer: Be thou faithful unto death, and I will give thee the crown of life (Revelation 2:10).

question: What did Jesus say about overcoming?
answer: He that overcometh, I will give to him to sit down with me in my throne, as I also overcame (Revelation 3:21).

question: What did Jesus say about the Lord's Day?
answer: I am the first and the last, and the Living one; and I was dead, and behold, I am alive for evermore (Revelation 1:17-18).

question: What did Jesus say about coming quickly?
answer: Behold, I come quickly: hold fast that which thou hast (Revelation 3:11; 22:7, 12, 20).

question: What did Jesus say about the Spirit and the bride?
answer: The Spirit and the bride say, Come. And he that heareth, let him say, Come (Revelation 22:17).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'Verily, verily'?
answer: It is a solemn affirmation, doubled in John ('truly, truly'), introducing weighty teachings of Jesus.

question: What did Jesus mean by 'I am' in John 8:58?
answer: He invoked the divine name (Exodus 3:14) — 'Before Abraham was born, I am' — declaring his eternal deity.

question: What did Jesus mean by 'follow me'?
answer: Jesus called people to give him supreme allegiance, leave all to be his disciples, walk in his way, and trust him with everything.

question: What did Jesus mean by 'come to me'?
answer: Jesus invited the weary and the burdened to find rest, salvation, and life in him by faith.

question: What did Jesus mean by 'go and sin no more'?
answer: He called those he forgave to leave their sin and walk in newness of life, recognizing the seriousness of God's mercy.

question: What did Jesus mean by 'born again'?
answer: He meant a new spiritual birth by the Holy Spirit — a fresh life given to those who trust in him (John 3:3-8).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'born of water and the Spirit'?
answer: He meant the Spirit's cleansing, life-giving work — required for entrance into the kingdom of God (John 3:5).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'eat my flesh, drink my blood'?
answer: He meant the spiritual feeding by faith on his death and life — receiving him personally for eternal life (John 6:53-58).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'the truth shall make you free'?
answer: He meant that knowing him, the truth, releases us from the slavery of sin into the freedom of God's children (John 8:31-36).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'love one another as I have loved you'?
answer: He commanded a sacrificial, self-giving love patterned after his own — the mark of his disciples (John 13:34-35).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'the way, the truth, and the life'?
answer: He is the only path to God, the embodiment of God's truth, and the source of true life — no one comes to the Father but by him (John 14:6).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'abide in me'?
answer: He called us to remain in living union with him by faith — drawing daily life and fruitfulness from him (John 15:1-8).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'the Comforter'?
answer: He meant the Holy Spirit, the Paraclete, who comes alongside to teach, remind, comfort, and empower his disciples (John 14:16-17, 26).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'in my Father's house are many mansions'?
answer: He spoke of the Father's house as the Christian's eternal home, where Jesus prepares a place for those who trust in him (John 14:2).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'greater works than these'?
answer: He referred to the church's worldwide gospel work after his ascension — the Spirit's power extending beyond Galilee through his disciples (John 14:12).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'I will not leave you desolate'?
answer: He promised the Comforter, the Holy Spirit, who would come to dwell with his disciples after his departure (John 14:18).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'whatsoever ye shall ask in my name'?
answer: He invited believers to pray in alignment with his character and mission, with the assurance that the Father hears and answers (John 14:13-14; 16:23).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'he that hath my commandments and keepeth them'?
answer: He defined love for him as obedience to his commands, with the promise of being loved by the Father and manifested by the Son (John 14:21).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'in my name they shall cast out demons'?
answer: He declared that signs would accompany those who believe — a manifestation of his ongoing authority through his servants (Mark 16:17-18).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'a new commandment'?
answer: He gave the supreme law of love patterned after his own — to love one another as he has loved us (John 13:34).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'except a grain of wheat fall into the earth'?
answer: He spoke of his death — like a grain that dies to bear fruit — and of disciples who likewise die to self to bear lasting fruit (John 12:24).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'lifted up from the earth'?
answer: He spoke of his crucifixion as drawing all peoples to himself (John 12:32-33).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'give to him that asketh'?
answer: He called for radical generosity and willingness to lend, expecting nothing in return (Matthew 5:42).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'the meek shall inherit the earth'?
answer: He blessed those who are humble and gentle under God's care, who will share in the renewed earth (Matthew 5:5; Psalm 37:11).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'the pure in heart shall see God'?
answer: He blessed those whose hearts are cleansed by faith and aim at God alone — they will behold him (Matthew 5:8).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'blessed are the persecuted'?
answer: He blessed those who suffer for righteousness' sake, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven (Matthew 5:10).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'enter by the narrow gate'?
answer: He warned that the way to life is hard and few find it; the way to destruction is broad and many travel it (Matthew 7:13-14).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'wolves in sheep's clothing'?
answer: He warned of false prophets who appear safe but devour souls; their fruit reveals their true nature (Matthew 7:15-20).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'I never knew you'?
answer: To those who claimed his name without true faith and obedience, he will say, 'Depart from me' — a sober warning for the self-deceived (Matthew 7:21-23).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'where two or three are gathered'?
answer: He promised his special presence among believers gathered in his name, especially in matters of accountability and prayer (Matthew 18:20).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'be of good cheer'?
answer: He encouraged trust and courage in the midst of trials, sins forgiven, and the world's hostility — for he has overcome (Matthew 9:2; 14:27; John 16:33).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'fear not, only believe'?
answer: He calmed Jairus' grief, calling him to trust him beyond death itself (Mark 5:36).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'all things are possible to him that believeth'?
answer: He encouraged the father of the demonized boy to trust him for the impossible (Mark 9:23).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'except ye be converted'?
answer: He called for childlike turning to God in faith and humility, the true mark of kingdom citizens (Matthew 18:3).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'be ye therefore wise as serpents'?
answer: He instructed his disciples to combine wise discernment with harmless innocence as they minister in a hostile world (Matthew 10:16).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'fear not them that kill the body'?
answer: He told his followers not to fear those who can only end physical life, but to fear God who has authority over body and soul (Matthew 10:28).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'I came not to send peace, but a sword'?
answer: He warned that allegiance to him would divide families and societies; loyalty to Christ takes priority (Matthew 10:34-37).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'he that loseth his life for my sake shall find it'?
answer: He taught that life surrendered to him is truly gained, while life clutched in self is finally lost (Matthew 10:39).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'a cup of cold water'?
answer: Even the smallest service done in his name to one of his disciples will not lose its reward (Matthew 10:42).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'the Sabbath was made for man'?
answer: He affirmed that the Sabbath was given for human good, and as Lord of the Sabbath, his presence transcends rigid traditions (Mark 2:27-28).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'they that are whole have no need of a physician'?
answer: He explained that, like a doctor, he came to call sinners — those who recognize their need for him (Matthew 9:12-13).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'I came not to call the righteous'?
answer: He pointed out that those who think themselves righteous resist him; he came to save sinners who recognize their need.

question: What did Jesus mean by 'tax collectors and prostitutes go before you'?
answer: He said that humble repenters enter the kingdom ahead of religious leaders who reject God's call (Matthew 21:31-32).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'the stone which the builders rejected'?
answer: He applied Psalm 118 to himself: rejected by leaders, he became the chief cornerstone of God's building (Matthew 21:42).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'render unto Caesar'?
answer: Believers owe civil obedience and taxes to government, while reserving ultimate worship and allegiance for God (Matthew 22:21).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'God of the living'?
answer: He affirmed the resurrection by saying God is the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob — they live with him (Matthew 22:32).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'on these two depend the law and prophets'?
answer: All God's commandments are summed up in loving God supremely and loving neighbor as oneself (Matthew 22:40).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'whosoever exalteth himself shall be humbled'?
answer: God resists the proud but exalts the humble; reverse the world's order in his kingdom (Matthew 23:12; Luke 18:14).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'cleansing the inside of the cup'?
answer: He called the religious leaders to address inward sin first, knowing that true cleanness comes from a transformed heart (Matthew 23:25-26).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'whitewashed tombs'?
answer: He warned that outwardly clean people may be inwardly full of sin; God sees the heart (Matthew 23:27).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'how often would I have gathered you'?
answer: He lamented Jerusalem's persistent rejection of God's prophets, longing to gather them as a hen gathers her chicks (Matthew 23:37).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'where the body is, there the eagles will be gathered'?
answer: It is a proverb signaling that the events of the end will be unmistakable when they come (Matthew 24:28).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'as in the days of Noah'?
answer: He warned that his coming would be sudden and most would be unprepared, like the people in Noah's day (Matthew 24:37-39).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'watch therefore, for ye know not'?
answer: He called for sober readiness because no one knows the day or hour of his coming (Matthew 24:42).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'as ye did it unto the least'?
answer: He identified himself with his needy brethren; how we treat them reflects our heart toward him (Matthew 25:40, 45).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'this is my body'?
answer: At the Last Supper, he gave the bread as a sign and seal of his body broken for sinners (Matthew 26:26).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'this is my blood of the covenant'?
answer: He offered the cup as a sign of his blood, by which the new covenant is established for the forgiveness of sins (Matthew 26:28).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'flesh is weak'?
answer: He acknowledged the disciples' good intentions while warning that human weakness needs prayer for strength (Matthew 26:41).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'rise, let us be going'?
answer: He called the disciples to face his betrayer and the events ordained by the Father, willingly fulfilling his mission (Matthew 26:46).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'thou hast said'?
answer: It is an idiom meaning 'Yes, you have said it correctly,' affirming the question as true (Matthew 26:25, 64).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?'
answer: From Psalm 22, he expressed the depths of bearing our sin and judgment, abandoned by the Father in our place — yet ending the Psalm in vindication.

question: What did Jesus mean by 'into thy hands I commend my spirit'?
answer: From Psalm 31, he entrusted his soul to the Father in death, demonstrating perfect trust to the end (Luke 23:46).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'It is finished'?
answer: He declared the work of redemption complete — the price for sin paid in full at the cross (John 19:30).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'Peace be unto you'?
answer: To his disciples after the resurrection, he announced peace — the gospel peace and forgiveness procured by his death and victory.

question: What did Jesus mean by 'Receive ye the Holy Spirit'?
answer: It was a foretaste and promise of the fuller outpouring of the Spirit at Pentecost, equipping his disciples for mission.

question: What did Jesus mean by 'Behold the Lamb of God'?
answer: It was John the Baptist's testimony that Jesus is the appointed sacrifice who takes away the sin of the world (John 1:29).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'before Abraham was, I am'?
answer: He declared his eternal preexistence and divine identity by using the divine name (John 8:58).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'I and the Father are one'?
answer: He affirmed unity of essence and purpose with the Father, claiming deity (John 10:30).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'this generation shall not pass away'?
answer: He referred to the generation witnessing the signs he described — emphasizing the certainty of fulfillment within or near their time, including the destruction of Jerusalem (Matthew 24:34).

question: What is the Sermon on the Mount?
answer: It is the longest collection of Jesus' teaching, recorded in Matthew 5-7, including the Beatitudes, the Lord's Prayer, and the Golden Rule.

question: Who is the Word in John 1?
answer: The eternal Son of God, Jesus Christ, who was with God and was God in the beginning, by whom all things were made (John 1:1-3, 14).

question: How did God make Eve?
answer: From a rib taken from Adam's side while he slept, then closed up the flesh; God brought her to Adam (Genesis 2:21-22).

question: Why did God create marriage?
answer: For companionship, fruitfulness, and as a picture of Christ and his church (Genesis 2:18-24; Ephesians 5:31-32).

question: What did Jesus say is the greatest commandment?
answer: Love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, soul, and mind (Matthew 22:37-38; Deuteronomy 6:5).

question: Why is the church called the body of Christ?
answer: Because believers are organically joined to Christ as members of his body, with him as head (1 Corinthians 12:12-27; Ephesians 4:15-16).

question: What does the Bible say about anger and forgiveness?
answer: Be angry and sin not; let not the sun go down upon your wrath (Ephesians 4:26); forgive as God in Christ has forgiven you.

question: What does it mean Jesus is our peace?
answer: He has reconciled us to God and broken the dividing wall, making peace through his blood (Ephesians 2:14-16).

question: What does the Bible say about wisdom from above?
answer: It is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, easy to be entreated, full of mercy and good fruits, without partiality, without hypocrisy (James 3:17).

question: How many days passed between Jesus' resurrection and ascension?
answer: Forty days, during which he gave many infallible proofs of his resurrection and taught about the kingdom (Acts 1:3).

question: Where did Jesus ascend from?
answer: From the Mount of Olives, near Bethany (Acts 1:9-12; Luke 24:50-51).

question: How many people were in the upper room at Pentecost?
answer: About 120 disciples were gathered before Pentecost (Acts 1:15).

question: What languages were heard at Pentecost?
answer: Various known languages of those who had come to Jerusalem from many nations heard the wonderful works of God in their own tongues (Acts 2:5-11).

question: What did Peter preach at Pentecost?
answer: He proclaimed Jesus crucified and risen, called for repentance and baptism, and proclaimed the gift of the Holy Spirit (Acts 2:14-40).

question: What was the response to Peter's Pentecost sermon?
answer: They were pricked in their heart; about 3,000 souls received the word and were baptized (Acts 2:37-41).

question: Who was the second Christian martyr (besides Stephen)?
answer: James the brother of John was put to death by Herod Agrippa I (Acts 12:1-2).

question: What was Saul's role in Stephen's death?
answer: Saul (later Paul) was consenting to his death; the witnesses laid their garments at his feet (Acts 7:58; 8:1).

question: Where was the gospel first preached to Gentiles in earnest?
answer: In Antioch of Syria, by believers scattered after Stephen's martyrdom (Acts 11:19-21).

question: Who first brought the gospel to Cornelius?
answer: Peter, after a vision and the leading of the Spirit, preached to Cornelius and his household (Acts 10).

question: What did the Holy Spirit do at Cornelius's house?
answer: While Peter spoke, the Holy Spirit fell on all who heard the word, and they spoke with tongues and praised God (Acts 10:44-46).

question: Who first called Paul Paul instead of Saul?
answer: Acts 13:9 marks the transition: 'But Saul, who is also called Paul,' from his first missionary journey onward.

question: What miracle did Paul do on Cyprus?
answer: He pronounced blindness on the sorcerer Elymas, who opposed the gospel (Acts 13:11).

question: Who was converted under Paul on Cyprus?
answer: Sergius Paulus, the Roman proconsul, believed (Acts 13:12).

question: What happened at Lystra after Paul healed the lame man?
answer: The crowd called Barnabas Zeus and Paul Hermes, attempting to offer sacrifice; Paul refused and preached the living God (Acts 14:8-18).

question: What happened to Paul at Lystra after the riot?
answer: They stoned Paul, dragged him out of the city as if dead, but he rose up and went on (Acts 14:19-20).

question: What was the Council of Jerusalem's decision?
answer: Gentile believers were not required to be circumcised; they were to abstain from idol food, blood, what is strangled, and from sexual immorality (Acts 15:19-20).

question: Why did Paul and Barnabas separate?
answer: They sharply disagreed over taking John Mark on the second journey; Paul took Silas, and Barnabas took Mark (Acts 15:36-40).

question: How did Paul gain Timothy as a coworker?
answer: He took Timothy from Lystra, where Timothy was well spoken of by the believers (Acts 16:1-3).

question: What did the slave girl in Philippi say?
answer: These men are servants of the Most High God, who proclaim unto you the way of salvation (Acts 16:17).

question: Who was the first European convert?
answer: Lydia, a worshiper of God and seller of purple from Thyatira, was baptized with her household at Philippi (Acts 16:14-15).

question: What happened to the Philippian jailer?
answer: After the earthquake, he asked, 'What must I do to be saved?' and believed with his household, and was baptized (Acts 16:30-33).

question: What did the Bereans do that was admirable?
answer: They received the word with all readiness, examining the Scriptures daily to see whether these things were so (Acts 17:11).

question: To what altar did Paul refer at Athens?
answer: To an altar inscribed 'TO AN UNKNOWN GOD,' using it to introduce the true Creator (Acts 17:23).

question: Who believed at Athens?
answer: Among others, Dionysius the Areopagite, a woman named Damaris, and others (Acts 17:34).

question: Whom did Paul stay with in Corinth?
answer: He worked and lived with Aquila and Priscilla, fellow tentmakers (Acts 18:1-3).

question: What did the Lord say to Paul in a night vision in Corinth?
answer: Be not afraid, but speak and hold not thy peace: for I am with thee... for I have much people in this city (Acts 18:9-10).

question: Who became a believer in Ephesus through Aquila and Priscilla?
answer: Apollos, an eloquent Alexandrian Jew, was instructed more accurately by them (Acts 18:24-26).

question: What happened with the seven sons of Sceva?
answer: They tried to use Jesus' name without faith; the evil spirit overpowered them, and they fled (Acts 19:13-16).

question: What was the value of magic books burned at Ephesus?
answer: Fifty thousand pieces of silver (Acts 19:19) — a remarkable testimony of repentance.

question: What was Paul's farewell to the Ephesian elders?
answer: He told them they would see his face no more, warned of false teachers, commended them to God, and quoted Jesus: 'It is more blessed to give than to receive' (Acts 20:17-38).

question: What did Agabus prophesy about Paul?
answer: That the Jews at Jerusalem would bind the man whose girdle this is, and deliver him into the hands of the Gentiles (Acts 21:11).

question: How did Paul defend himself in Jerusalem?
answer: He addressed the people in Hebrew, recounting his Jewish heritage, his persecution of the church, and his conversion (Acts 22:1-21).

question: How did Paul appeal to Caesar?
answer: As a Roman citizen, when on trial before Festus, Paul said, 'I appeal unto Caesar' (Acts 25:11).

question: Whom did Paul stand before with his testimony at Caesarea?
answer: Festus and King Agrippa II, with Bernice (Acts 25:13-26:32).

question: How did Paul's voyage to Rome end up?
answer: His ship was wrecked on Malta after a great storm, but all 276 souls aboard were saved (Acts 27:39-44).

question: Where did Paul live in Rome at first?
answer: In his own hired house under guard, receiving all who came to him for two years (Acts 28:30).

question: What is the climactic statement of Acts?
answer: Preaching the kingdom of God, and teaching the things concerning the Lord Jesus Christ with all boldness, none forbidding him (Acts 28:31).

question: How does Romans begin in greeting?
answer: Paul, a servant of Jesus Christ, called to be an apostle, separated unto the gospel of God (Romans 1:1).

question: What does Romans 1:16 declare?
answer: For I am not ashamed of the gospel: for it is the power of God unto salvation to every one that believeth.

question: What is Romans 1:17 about?
answer: For therein is revealed a righteousness of God from faith unto faith: as it is written, But the righteous shall live by faith.

question: How does Romans describe sin's universality?
answer: There is none righteous, no, not one... for all have sinned, and fall short of the glory of God (Romans 3:10, 23).

question: What does Romans 4 say about Abraham?
answer: Abraham believed God, and it was reckoned unto him for righteousness (Romans 4:3).

question: What does Romans 5 say about Christ's love?
answer: God commendeth his own love toward us, in that, while we were yet sinners, Christ died for us (Romans 5:8).

question: What does Romans 6 teach about baptism?
answer: Baptized into Christ Jesus, we were baptized into his death; we are buried with him through baptism that we might walk in newness of life (Romans 6:3-4).

question: What does Romans 7 portray?
answer: The struggle of life under the law and the inability of fallen flesh to obey, ending with deliverance through Jesus Christ (Romans 7:24-25).

question: What does Romans 8 declare about the Spirit?
answer: As many as are led by the Spirit of God, these are sons of God; the Spirit himself bears witness with our spirit that we are God's children (Romans 8:14, 16).

question: What does Romans 9-11 address?
answer: God's sovereign election, the present unbelief of Israel, the inclusion of the Gentiles, and a future great hope for Israel.

question: What does Romans 12 begin with?
answer: I beseech you... that ye present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable to God, which is your spiritual service (Romans 12:1).

question: What does Romans 13 teach about authorities?
answer: Let every soul be in subjection to the higher powers: for there is no power but of God; the powers that be are ordained of God (Romans 13:1).

question: What does 1 Corinthians address most centrally?
answer: Disorder in the church — divisions, immorality, lawsuits, marriage, idol meat, worship, gifts, and the resurrection — all addressed by the cross.

question: What does 2 Corinthians address?
answer: Paul's defense of his apostleship, the nature of gospel ministry, comfort in suffering, generosity, and the new creation in Christ.

question: What is the central message of Galatians?
answer: Justification by faith in Christ alone, freedom from the law, life by the Spirit, and walking in love.

question: What is the central theme of Ephesians?
answer: The riches of God's grace in Christ, the unity and beauty of the church, and the call to walk worthy in love and stand against evil.

question: What is the central theme of Philippians?
answer: Joy in Christ in every circumstance, gospel partnership, and pressing on toward the goal in him.

question: What is the central theme of Colossians?
answer: The supremacy and sufficiency of Christ over every power and tradition; in him are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.

question: What is the central theme of 1 Thessalonians?
answer: Encouragement of new believers in faith, love, holiness, and hope in the coming of the Lord Jesus.

question: What is the central theme of 2 Thessalonians?
answer: Steadfastness in trials, correction about the day of the Lord, and continued faithful work and service.

question: What is the central theme of 1-2 Timothy?
answer: Pastoral guidance for sound doctrine, godly leadership, faithful witness, and persevering ministry.

question: What is the central theme of Titus?
answer: Sound doctrine and good works flowing from the gospel, with leaders qualified to lead in grace and truth.

question: What is the central theme of Philemon?
answer: Gospel transformation in relationships — Paul interceding for the runaway slave Onesimus to be received as a beloved brother.

question: What is the central theme of Hebrews?
answer: The supremacy of Christ over angels, Moses, the priesthood, and the old covenant; he is the better Mediator who saves to the uttermost.

question: What is the central theme of James?
answer: Living, working faith — endurance in trials, taming the tongue, doing the word, prayer, and patience until the Lord's coming.

question: What is the central theme of 1 Peter?
answer: Suffering as elect exiles in hope, holiness, and following Christ's example, anchored in his resurrection.

question: What is the central theme of 2 Peter?
answer: Growing in grace and knowledge, opposing false teachers, and watching for the day of the Lord.

question: What is the central theme of 1 John?
answer: Tests of true faith — walking in light, loving the brethren, confessing sin, and believing Jesus is the Christ — assurance of life.

question: What is the central theme of 2 John?
answer: Walk in love and truth, refusing those who deny Christ's coming in the flesh.

question: What is the central theme of 3 John?
answer: Hospitality and support for faithful gospel workers; warning against domineering Diotrephes.

question: What is the central theme of Jude?
answer: Contend earnestly for the faith once delivered, beware of false teachers, and keep yourselves in the love of God.

question: What is the central message of Revelation?
answer: Jesus Christ is the conquering King; he will judge evil, deliver his people, and consummate all things in the new heavens and new earth.

question: How is Christ presented in Revelation 1?
answer: As the glorified Son of Man, with eyes like flame, hair white as snow, voice like many waters, and holding the keys of death and Hades.

question: How is Christ presented in Revelation 5?
answer: As the Lion of Judah and the Lamb who was slain, alone worthy to open the seven-sealed scroll.

question: How is Christ presented in Revelation 19?
answer: As Faithful and True, the Word of God, riding on a white horse with eyes of fire and a sharp sword from his mouth, the King of kings and Lord of lords.

question: How is Christ presented in Revelation 22?
answer: As the Alpha and Omega, the bright morning star, who declares, 'Behold, I come quickly.'

question: What is meant by the soul's salvation?
answer: The receiving of forgiveness, eternal life, and inheritance in Christ — the end of saving faith (1 Peter 1:9).

question: What does it mean to grow in grace?
answer: It means to deepen our trust, knowledge, and obedience as we walk with Christ, increasing in Christlike character (2 Peter 3:18).

question: What does it mean that Christ ever lives to intercede?
answer: At the Father's right hand, Christ continually pleads our case as our great High Priest, ensuring our salvation (Hebrews 7:25; Romans 8:34).

question: How does the resurrection of Christ affect believers' bodies?
answer: Believers will be raised with imperishable, glorious, powerful, spiritual bodies like his (1 Corinthians 15:42-49; Philippians 3:20-21).

question: How does the resurrection of Christ affect believers' present lives?
answer: It gives new life now — power to walk in newness, hope amid trials, and confidence in future glory (Romans 6:4; 1 Peter 1:3-5).

question: How does the gospel address the past?
answer: It promises forgiveness for all our sins through the blood of Christ — old things passed away, all things are become new (1 John 1:9; 2 Corinthians 5:17).

question: How does the gospel address the present?
answer: It gives the indwelling Spirit, daily grace, and peace with God for ongoing transformation and joy (Romans 8:1-11).

question: How does the gospel address the future?
answer: It gives sure hope of resurrection, glorification, and eternal life with God in the new creation (1 Thessalonians 4:16-18; Revelation 21:1-7).

question: What is biblical hope?
answer: Confident expectation grounded in God's promises and proven character — not wishful thinking but anchor of the soul (Hebrews 6:19).

question: What does it mean Christ is Mediator of the new covenant?
answer: As Mediator, he stands between God and humanity, securing eternal salvation by his death and continuing intercession (Hebrews 9:15; 1 Timothy 2:5).

question: What does it mean Christ is the Lamb without blemish?
answer: He is the perfect, sinless sacrifice — fulfilling the Old Testament types and providing complete atonement (1 Peter 1:19).

question: What does it mean Christ is firstborn from the dead?
answer: He is the first to rise to immortal, glorified life, the leader and pioneer of the resurrection (Colossians 1:18; Revelation 1:5).

question: What is the importance of Christ's blood?
answer: It is the ransom price of redemption, the means of cleansing, the seal of the new covenant, and the basis of our reconciliation with God.

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's compassion?
answer: He was moved with compassion toward the multitudes, the sick, the bereaved, and his disciples — a compassionate High Priest (Matthew 9:36; Hebrews 4:15).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's tears?
answer: He wept at Lazarus' tomb (John 11:35) and over Jerusalem (Luke 19:41), feeling deeply the pain of sin and unbelief.

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's prayer life?
answer: He withdrew often to pray, sometimes all night; he interceded for his disciples and now intercedes for us at the Father's right hand.

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's meekness?
answer: He is meek and lowly of heart (Matthew 11:29); did not strive nor cry out (Matthew 12:19); is the perfect example of meekness.

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's authority?
answer: All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to him (Matthew 28:18); he speaks and commands with divine authority (Matthew 7:29).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's wisdom?
answer: In him are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge (Colossians 2:3); he confounded the Pharisees and Sadducees in their own learning.

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's holiness?
answer: He was without sin, holy, harmless, undefiled, separated from sinners — yet drew near to save them (Hebrews 7:26; 1 Peter 2:22).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's love?
answer: He loved his own who were in the world to the end (John 13:1); his love passes knowledge (Ephesians 3:19).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's joy?
answer: For the joy that was set before him, he endured the cross (Hebrews 12:2); he rejoiced in the Holy Spirit (Luke 10:21).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's grief?
answer: He was a man of sorrows and acquainted with grief (Isaiah 53:3); he sorrowed in Gethsemane and wept at the tomb.

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's anger?
answer: He was filled with righteous anger when he cleansed the temple and at the hardness of heart he saw in synagogue worshipers (Mark 3:5; John 2:13-17).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's miracles of nature?
answer: He calmed storms, walked on water, multiplied bread and fish, and turned water into wine — Lord over all creation.

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's miracles of healing?
answer: He healed all manner of sickness — lepers, the lame, the blind, the deaf, the bleeding, the demonized — by his powerful word.

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's miracles of resurrection?
answer: He raised the widow's son at Nain, Jairus' daughter, and Lazarus from the dead, prefiguring his own resurrection.

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's miracles over demons?
answer: He cast out demons by his word, freeing many; even the demons confessed his identity as the Holy One of God.

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's wisdom in disputes?
answer: He answered religious leaders with such wisdom that they could not catch him in his words (Mark 12:13-17, 28-34).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's identification with sinners?
answer: He ate with tax collectors and sinners, came to seek and save the lost, and was numbered with transgressors at the cross.

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's compassion toward outcasts?
answer: He embraced lepers, healed the bleeding woman, welcomed the Samaritan, ate with the despised, and showed mercy to the lowest.

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's care for women?
answer: He honored women with respect, taught them theology, ministered to them, and entrusted the news of his resurrection to them first.

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's love for children?
answer: He took them in his arms, blessed them, and said the kingdom of God belonged to such as these (Mark 10:13-16).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's care for the poor?
answer: He preached good news to the poor, fed multitudes, taught alms-giving, and identified himself with the needy.

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's prayer at Gethsemane?
answer: He prayed in agony, sweating drops of blood, surrendered to the Father's will, and was strengthened by an angel (Luke 22:39-44).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's silence at trial?
answer: He answered nothing to the accusations of the chief priests, fulfilling Isaiah 53 — like a lamb led to the slaughter.

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's mockery and beating?
answer: He was scourged, crowned with thorns, mocked as 'King of the Jews,' spat upon, and beaten — bearing the reproach for our sake.

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's words on the cross?
answer: Seven sayings: 'Father, forgive them'; 'Today shalt thou be with me in Paradise'; 'Woman, behold thy son'; 'My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?'; 'I thirst'; 'It is finished'; 'Father, into thy hands I commend my spirit.'

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's burial?
answer: Joseph of Arimathea wrapped his body in fine linen and laid it in a new tomb; Nicodemus brought a hundred pounds of myrrh and aloes (John 19:38-40; Matthew 27:57-60).

question: What does the Bible say about the women at Christ's tomb?
answer: On the first day of the week, the women came with spices to anoint his body and found the tomb empty, and angels announced his resurrection.

question: What does the Bible say about Christ appearing to Peter?
answer: The Lord appeared to Peter (Cephas) on resurrection day, mentioned in Luke 24:34 and 1 Corinthians 15:5.

question: What does the Bible say about Christ appearing to two on Emmaus road?
answer: He explained the Scriptures to them, and their eyes were opened in the breaking of bread (Luke 24:13-35).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's appearance to the disciples behind locked doors?
answer: He came and stood in the midst, said 'Peace be unto you,' showed his hands and side, and breathed on them, saying, 'Receive ye the Holy Spirit' (John 20:19-22).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's appearance to Thomas?
answer: Eight days later, he showed Thomas his hands and side; Thomas confessed, 'My Lord and my God' (John 20:24-29).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's breakfast on the shore?
answer: After the great catch of fish, he prepared bread and fish and ate with the disciples, restoring Peter (John 21:9-19).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's appearance to over 500 brethren?
answer: After his resurrection, he appeared to more than five hundred brethren at once, most of whom were still alive when Paul wrote (1 Corinthians 15:6).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's appearance to James?
answer: He appeared to James, his half-brother, after the resurrection (1 Corinthians 15:7); James later became leader of the Jerusalem church.

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's last appearance recorded in the Gospels?
answer: He led them out toward Bethany, lifted up his hands and blessed them, and as he blessed them was carried up into heaven (Luke 24:50-51).

question: What does the Bible say about angels at the ascension?
answer: Two men in white clothing said, 'This Jesus, who was received up from you into heaven, shall so come in like manner as ye beheld him going into heaven' (Acts 1:11).

question: What is the great mystery of godliness?
answer: He who was manifested in the flesh, justified in the spirit, seen of angels, preached among the nations, believed on in the world, received up in glory (1 Timothy 3:16).

question: What is the meaning of God's Spirit moving over the waters?
answer: At creation, the Spirit of God hovered over the deep (Genesis 1:2), pictured as preparing creation for God's order and life.

question: What is the meaning of God walking in the garden?
answer: It pictures God's intimate fellowship with man at creation, broken by sin (Genesis 3:8), and to be restored in the new creation.

question: What is the meaning of God's calling Abram?
answer: By calling Abram out of Ur, God set in motion his plan to bless all nations through Abraham's seed — fulfilled in Christ.

question: What is the meaning of God's covenant with Abraham?
answer: God promised land, descendants, and worldwide blessing — guaranteed by his oath, fulfilled supremely in Christ (Genesis 12; 15; 17).

question: What is the meaning of the substitutionary ram at Mount Moriah?
answer: It pictured a substitute taking the place of Isaac, foreshadowing the Lamb of God who would die in our place.

question: What is the meaning of Joseph's exaltation?
answer: His exaltation in Egypt pictures Christ's exaltation after suffering — to save many people alive.

question: What is the meaning of Israel's deliverance from Egypt?
answer: It pictures God's redemption of his people through the blood of the lamb — a type of Christ's deliverance from sin.

question: What is the meaning of the giving of the law?
answer: It revealed God's holy character, exposed Israel's sin, and prepared for the gospel by showing the need for a Savior.

question: What is the meaning of the wilderness wandering?
answer: It tested and humbled Israel, taught them dependence on God, and prefigured the believer's pilgrimage.

question: What is the meaning of the conquest of Canaan?
answer: It fulfilled God's promise of land, pictured the believer's inheritance, and showed God's holy judgment on persistent wickedness.

question: What is the meaning of David's reign?
answer: It established God's covenant with David and prefigured the eternal kingdom of his Son, Jesus Christ.

question: What is the meaning of Solomon's temple?
answer: It became the place of God's special presence among his people, pointing to Christ as the true temple and to the believer as God's dwelling.

question: What is the meaning of the divided kingdom?
answer: It revealed Israel's weakness and God's faithfulness despite their sin, raising longing for a perfect king.

question: What is the meaning of the exile?
answer: It was God's discipline for sin, fulfilling the warnings of the law and prophets, leading to repentance and renewed hope.

question: What is the meaning of the return from exile?
answer: It showed God's faithfulness to his promises, his ability to restore his people, and pointed forward to ultimate restoration in Christ.

question: What is the meaning of John the Baptist's ministry?
answer: He was the forerunner of Christ, calling people to repent and be baptized, preparing the way for the Lord.

question: What is the meaning of Jesus' baptism?
answer: He identified himself with sinners, fulfilled all righteousness, was anointed by the Spirit, and was affirmed by the Father (Matthew 3:13-17).

question: What is the meaning of Jesus' temptation?
answer: He overcame Satan by the Word of God, succeeded where Adam and Israel failed, and showed himself the perfect Savior.

question: What is the meaning of Jesus' choosing twelve apostles?
answer: He established the foundation of the new Israel — twelve apostles for twelve tribes — to lay the groundwork of the church.

question: What is the meaning of Jesus' parables?
answer: They unveiled kingdom truths to the receptive while veiling them from the proud, calling all to consider Jesus' teaching with care.

question: What is the meaning of Jesus' miracles?
answer: They authenticated his identity, displayed God's compassion, and gave previews of the new creation under his reign.

question: What is the meaning of the Last Supper?
answer: It instituted the new covenant in his blood, fulfilling Passover, and giving the church the ongoing meal of remembrance.

question: What is the meaning of Jesus' arrest in Gethsemane?
answer: He willingly surrendered to fulfill the Father's will, showing voluntary obedience to drink the cup of our salvation.

question: What is the meaning of Jesus' trial?
answer: It exposed the world's enmity to truth, while Jesus, though innocent, was condemned in our place to fulfill prophetic Scripture.

question: What is the meaning of Jesus' crucifixion?
answer: It was the substitutionary, atoning death by which the price of sin was paid, justice satisfied, and reconciliation accomplished.

question: What is the meaning of the centurion's confession?
answer: A Gentile soldier — at the cross, of all places — confessed Jesus as Son of God, foreshadowing the worldwide spread of the gospel.

question: What is the meaning of Jesus' burial?
answer: It confirmed his real death and prepared for his resurrection, fulfilling Isaiah 53:9 — with a rich man in his death.

question: What is the meaning of Jesus' resurrection appearances?
answer: They confirmed his bodily resurrection, restored the disciples, and commissioned the church for mission.

question: What is the meaning of Pentecost?
answer: The promised gift of the Spirit was poured out, the church was empowered for witness, and the gospel went forth to the world.

question: What is the meaning of Stephen's martyrdom?
answer: It marked the first Christian martyrdom, sparking persecution that scattered believers, and witnessed the gospel even to Saul.

question: What is the meaning of Saul's conversion?
answer: It vindicated the resurrection, showed the power of grace to save the worst of sinners, and prepared the great apostle to the Gentiles.

question: What is the meaning of the gospel's spread to Gentiles?
answer: It fulfilled Old Testament prophecy that the nations would come, broke down dividing walls in Christ, and formed the new humanity of believers.

question: What is the meaning of the Jerusalem council's decision?
answer: It affirmed the gospel of grace alone for Jew and Gentile, preserving the unity of the church under apostolic teaching.

question: What is the meaning of Paul's missionary journeys?
answer: They planted churches across the Roman world, spread the gospel to many cultures, and produced the New Testament epistles.

question: What is the meaning of the destruction of Jerusalem in A.D. 70?
answer: It fulfilled Jesus' prophecy and signaled the end of the temple system, pointing to the once-for-all sufficiency of Christ's sacrifice.

question: What is the meaning of the term 'Jehovah-Jireh'?
answer: Jehovah-Jireh means 'The Lord will provide,' the name Abraham gave to the place where God provided a ram instead of Isaac (Genesis 22:14).

question: What is the meaning of the term 'Ebenezer'?
answer: Ebenezer means 'stone of help'; Samuel set it up saying, 'Hitherto hath Jehovah helped us' (1 Samuel 7:12).

question: What was the rebellion of Korah?
answer: Korah, with Dathan, Abiram, and others, rose against Moses and Aaron's leadership; the earth swallowed them up (Numbers 16).

question: What was Israel's golden calf at Sinai?
answer: While Moses was on the mountain, Aaron made a golden calf at the people's request, and they worshiped it (Exodus 32).

question: What plague stopped at the bronze serpent?
answer: The Lord sent fiery serpents to bite the people; those who looked to the bronze serpent on the pole lived (Numbers 21:6-9).

question: What did the Israelite spies bring back from the land?
answer: A cluster of grapes so large it was carried on a pole between two men, plus pomegranates and figs (Numbers 13:23).

question: How long did Eli judge Israel?
answer: Forty years (1 Samuel 4:18).

question: How many years did Samuel judge Israel?
answer: He judged Israel all the days of his life from his youth onward (1 Samuel 7:15).

question: Who were the two main prophets in Ahab's reign?
answer: Elijah and Micaiah were the prominent prophets confronting Ahab.

question: What city did Elijah hide near during the drought?
answer: He hid by the brook Cherith, east of the Jordan, where ravens fed him (1 Kings 17:3-6).

question: Where did Elijah next go after the brook dried?
answer: To Zarephath of Sidon, to a widow whom God commanded to sustain him (1 Kings 17:8-9).

question: What was Elisha's first miracle?
answer: He divided the Jordan with Elijah's mantle, just as Elijah had (2 Kings 2:14).

question: How many miracles did Elisha perform compared to Elijah?
answer: Elisha performed about double the number of recorded miracles, fulfilling his request for a double portion of Elijah's spirit.

question: What miracle did Elisha do for the Shunammite woman?
answer: He prophesied she would have a son and later raised her son back to life when he died (2 Kings 4).

question: How was Naaman cured of his leprosy?
answer: He dipped seven times in the Jordan River as Elisha commanded, and his flesh was restored (2 Kings 5:14).

question: Who was the Aramean general who became servant of Jehovah?
answer: Naaman, after his healing, vowed to worship only the God of Israel (2 Kings 5:17-18).

question: What sign did Hezekiah receive of God's healing?
answer: The shadow on the dial of Ahaz went backward ten degrees (2 Kings 20:9-11).

question: What was Manasseh's repentance?
answer: In Babylonian captivity, Manasseh humbled himself before God, who restored him to his throne (2 Chronicles 33:11-13).

question: What did Josiah do when the Book of the Law was found?
answer: He tore his clothes, repented, and led national reform — destroying idols and renewing the covenant (2 Kings 22-23).

question: How did the temple's destruction affect Israel?
answer: It marked the climax of judgment for Israel's idolatry and led to seventy years of exile in Babylon.

question: What was Daniel's diet test?
answer: For ten days, Daniel and his friends ate only vegetables and drank water; they appeared healthier than those who ate the king's food (Daniel 1:12-15).

question: What were Nebuchadnezzar's seven years of madness?
answer: He was driven from men, ate grass like an ox, and had hair like eagles' feathers — until he acknowledged the Most High (Daniel 4:33-37).

question: What was the message of the writing on the wall?
answer: MENE, MENE, TEKEL, UPHARSIN — God numbered Babylon's days, weighed them in the balances, and divided the kingdom (Daniel 5).

question: How did Daniel survive the lions' den?
answer: God sent his angel and shut the lions' mouths, because Daniel was found innocent before him (Daniel 6:22).

question: What is the seventy weeks prophecy?
answer: Seventy weeks (likely seventy 'sevens' of years) decreed for Daniel's people, climaxing in Messiah's coming, his being cut off, and the abomination of desolation (Daniel 9:24-27).

question: What did Hosea name his children?
answer: Jezreel ('God scatters'), Lo-ruhamah ('not pitied'), and Lo-ammi ('not my people') — symbolic of Israel's state (Hosea 1).

question: What did Joel prophesy about the locusts?
answer: He compared the locust plagues to God's coming day of judgment and called the people to repentance (Joel 1-2).

question: What did Amos see in his visions?
answer: Locusts, fire, a plumb line, summer fruit, and the Lord standing by the altar (Amos 7-9), each a sign of judgment.

question: What did Obadiah prophesy about Edom?
answer: He pronounced judgment on Edom for violence against brother Jacob and announced their utter destruction.

question: What was the sign of Jonah?
answer: As Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the great fish, so the Son of Man would be in the heart of the earth (Matthew 12:40).

question: What did Micah prophesy about leaders?
answer: He condemned the unjust leaders who oppressed the poor and prophesied a ruler from Bethlehem to shepherd God's people.

question: What did Nahum prophesy about Nineveh?
answer: Nahum announced Nineveh's coming destruction, in contrast to the repentance under Jonah's ministry generations earlier.

question: What did Habakkuk struggle with?
answer: He asked why God allowed wickedness in Judah and why he would use a more wicked Babylon to judge them — answered with 'the just shall live by his faith.'

question: What did Zephaniah prophesy?
answer: He prophesied a coming day of the Lord — judgment on Judah, neighbors, and nations — and a remnant rejoicing in God.

question: What did Haggai prophesy after the return from exile?
answer: He stirred up the people to rebuild the temple and declared, 'I am with you, saith the Lord' (Haggai 1:13).

question: What did Zechariah prophesy of the King?
answer: That Zion's King would come riding on a donkey, just and bringing salvation (Zechariah 9:9).

question: What did Malachi rebuke the priests for?
answer: For offering blemished sacrifices, profaning God's name, and despising his table (Malachi 1:6-14).

question: How many wise men came to see Jesus?
answer: The Bible does not specify; tradition says three because of the three gifts (gold, frankincense, and myrrh) (Matthew 2:11).

question: How long did Joseph and Mary stay in Egypt?
answer: Until Herod the Great died — likely a few months to two years (Matthew 2:13-15, 19-21).

question: At what age did Jesus go to the temple?
answer: At twelve years old, where he sat among the teachers, both hearing and asking them questions (Luke 2:42-46).

question: What did Jesus' parents say to him in the temple?
answer: His mother said, 'Son, why hast thou thus dealt with us? Behold, thy father and I sought thee sorrowing' (Luke 2:48).

question: What did Jesus' answer in the temple suggest?
answer: 'Knew ye not that I must be in my Father's house?' showing his early awareness of his unique relationship with the Father (Luke 2:49).

question: What was Jesus' baptism by John?
answer: It was an act of identifying with sinners, fulfilling all righteousness, and being publicly anointed by the Spirit and affirmed by the Father (Matthew 3:13-17).

question: How long was Jesus tempted in the wilderness?
answer: Forty days and forty nights, after which the tempter came to him (Matthew 4:1-11).

question: What did Jesus quote to overcome temptation?
answer: He quoted Deuteronomy three times — 'Man shall not live by bread alone,' 'Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God,' and 'Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve.'

question: What was Jesus' first recorded miracle?
answer: Turning water into wine at the wedding in Cana of Galilee (John 2:1-11).

question: What was Jesus' first cleansing of the temple?
answer: Early in his ministry, Jesus drove out the money-changers and merchants from the temple courts (John 2:13-22).

question: What was Jesus' final cleansing of the temple?
answer: In the week of his crucifixion, Jesus again drove out the merchants and quoted, 'My house shall be called a house of prayer' (Matthew 21:12-13).

question: What was the Sermon on the Mount about?
answer: Kingdom character and ethics — Beatitudes, salt and light, the law fulfilled, prayer, fasting, the cross of discipleship, and warnings.

question: What did Jesus do at the wedding in Cana?
answer: He turned six stone jars of water into wine, manifesting his glory; his disciples believed in him (John 2:11).

question: What did Jesus say to Nicodemus?
answer: Except one be born anew, he cannot see the kingdom of God (John 3:3).

question: What did Jesus say to the Samaritan woman about water?
answer: Whosoever drinketh of the water that I shall give him shall never thirst (John 4:14).

question: What did Jesus do for the official's son?
answer: He spoke a word at a distance, and the boy was healed in the same hour (John 4:46-54).

question: What did Jesus do at the pool of Bethesda?
answer: He healed a man who had been infirm thirty-eight years, telling him to take up his bed and walk (John 5:1-15).

question: What did Jesus do for the multitude of 5,000?
answer: He fed them with five barley loaves and two small fish, with twelve baskets of fragments left over (John 6:9-13).

question: How did Jesus walk on the water?
answer: At the fourth watch of the night, he came to the disciples walking on the sea and stilled their fears (Matthew 14:22-33).

question: What did Jesus do for the Syrophoenician woman?
answer: After her humble persistence, he healed her demon-possessed daughter from a distance (Matthew 15:21-28).

question: What did Jesus do for the deaf and dumb man?
answer: He took him aside, put fingers into his ears, touched his tongue, looked up to heaven, and said, 'Ephphatha,' and the man heard and spoke plainly (Mark 7:31-37).

question: What did Jesus do for the multitude of 4,000?
answer: He fed them with seven loaves and a few small fish, with seven baskets of fragments left over (Matthew 15:32-39).

question: What did Jesus do at the Mount of Transfiguration?
answer: He was transfigured before Peter, James, and John, his face shining as the sun, with Moses and Elijah appearing with him (Matthew 17:1-8).

question: What did Jesus do for the man born blind?
answer: He anointed his eyes with clay made with saliva, sent him to wash in the pool of Siloam, and he came back seeing (John 9:6-7).

question: What did Jesus do for Lazarus?
answer: He raised him from the dead after he had been four days in the tomb, demonstrating he is the resurrection and the life (John 11).

question: What did Jesus tell Mary and Martha at Lazarus's tomb?
answer: I am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth on me, though he die, yet shall he live (John 11:25).

question: How was Jesus anointed for burial?
answer: Mary of Bethany anointed his feet with very costly perfume of nard and wiped them with her hair (John 12:3).

question: What did Jesus say about Mary's anointing?
answer: Let her alone: against the day of my burying hath she kept this. For the poor ye have always with you; but me ye have not always (John 12:7-8).

question: How did Jesus enter Jerusalem on Palm Sunday?
answer: Riding on a donkey's colt, with crowds spreading garments and palm branches, crying 'Hosanna!' (Matthew 21:1-11).

question: What was Jesus' great Olivet Discourse about?
answer: It addressed the destruction of the temple, signs of the end, his second coming, and parables of preparedness (Matthew 24-25).

question: What did Jesus do in the Garden of Gethsemane?
answer: He prayed in agony three times, surrendered to the Father's will, and was strengthened to face the cross (Matthew 26:36-46).

question: How was Jesus arrested?
answer: Judas led a band of soldiers and officers, identified Jesus with a kiss, and they took him away (John 18:1-12).

question: How did Peter deny Jesus?
answer: In the high priest's courtyard, Peter denied Jesus three times before the cock crowed, then went out and wept bitterly (Luke 22:54-62).

question: What did Pilate say at Jesus' trial?
answer: He said three times, 'I find no fault in him,' yet ultimately handed Jesus over to be crucified (Luke 23:4, 14, 22).

question: What did the people choose between Jesus and Barabbas?
answer: They chose to release Barabbas, a robber and murderer, and to crucify Jesus (Matthew 27:15-26).

question: How did Judas die?
answer: He hanged himself in remorse for betraying innocent blood; later his body fell and burst open (Matthew 27:5; Acts 1:18).

question: How long was Jesus on the cross?
answer: From about the third hour (9 a.m.) until he gave up his spirit at the ninth hour (3 p.m.), with darkness from the sixth to the ninth hour (Mark 15:25, 33-37).

question: What was the inscription above Jesus on the cross?
answer: In Hebrew, Latin, and Greek: 'Jesus of Nazareth, the King of the Jews' (John 19:19-20).

question: What happened in nature when Jesus died?
answer: The sun was darkened, the temple veil was torn, the earth quaked, rocks were rent, and tombs were opened (Matthew 27:51-52).

question: What signs were given at the cross?
answer: Darkness over the land, the centurion's confession, the rending of the veil, an earthquake, and the opening of tombs.

question: What did the centurion at the cross say?
answer: Truly this man was the Son of God (Mark 15:39).

question: What did Joseph of Arimathea do for Jesus?
answer: He boldly asked Pilate for Jesus' body, prepared it with linen, and laid it in his own new tomb (Matthew 27:57-60).

question: Who watched over the tomb?
answer: A Roman guard was set at the request of the chief priests, and the stone was sealed (Matthew 27:62-66).

question: On which day did Jesus rise?
answer: On the first day of the week, the third day after his death — early in the morning (Matthew 28:1; Mark 16:1-2).

question: What did the angel say at the tomb?
answer: He is not here; he is risen, even as he said. Come, see the place where the Lord lay (Matthew 28:6).

question: What did Jesus say to the disciples in the upper room?
answer: Peace be unto you. As the Father hath sent me, even so send I you (John 20:21).

question: What did Jesus give Peter the responsibility to do?
answer: Three times he asked Peter, 'Lovest thou me?' and commissioned him: 'Feed my lambs... Tend my sheep... Feed my sheep' (John 21:15-17).

question: What was the last word in the Old Testament?
answer: In English translations, the last word is 'curse' (Malachi 4:6) — fitting prelude to the gospel of grace.

question: What was the first word in the New Testament?
answer: In English translations, 'The' (Matthew 1:1: 'The book of the generation of Jesus Christ').

question: What was the last word in the New Testament?
answer: 'Amen' — the closing affirmation of Revelation 22:21.

question: What is the basic message of all biblical prophets?
answer: Repent and turn to the Lord, who is faithful, just, and merciful — and look forward to the coming Messiah and his kingdom.

question: What is the basic message of the apostles?
answer: Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God; he died for our sins, rose again, and offers eternal life to all who repent and believe.

question: What is the basic message of Christianity?
answer: That God in Christ has reconciled sinners to himself by grace through faith, and is gathering a people to glorify him forever in a new creation.

question: What does it mean Christ is our Passover?
answer: As the Passover lamb's blood spared Israel from death, Christ's blood spares us from God's judgment; he is our Passover sacrificed for us (1 Corinthians 5:7).

question: What does it mean Christ is the firstfruits?
answer: He is the first to rise to immortal life, and his resurrection guarantees the resurrection of all who belong to him (1 Corinthians 15:20-23).

question: What does it mean Christ is the head of the church?
answer: He is the Lord, life, and supreme authority of the church, providing direction and life to every member (Ephesians 4:15-16; Colossians 1:18).

question: What does it mean Christ is preeminent?
answer: In all things he has the first place — in creation, in the church, and in the new creation (Colossians 1:18).

question: What does it mean Christ is altogether lovely?
answer: He is supremely beautiful and desirable — full of grace and truth, the chief among ten thousand (Song of Solomon 5:16).

question: What does it mean Christ is our wisdom?
answer: In him are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge; he is wisdom from God for us (1 Corinthians 1:24, 30; Colossians 2:3).

question: What does it mean Christ is our sanctification?
answer: He is the source and means of our holiness, who sanctifies the church by his Word and Spirit (1 Corinthians 1:30; Hebrews 13:12).

question: What does it mean Christ is our redemption?
answer: He paid the price to set us free from sin's penalty, power, and ultimately presence (1 Corinthians 1:30; Ephesians 1:7).

question: What does it mean Christ is our example?
answer: He left an example, that we should follow his steps — in love, humility, holiness, and suffering well (1 Peter 2:21).

question: What does it mean Christ is our victory?
answer: He has conquered sin, death, and the devil; through him we have the victory (1 Corinthians 15:57; Romans 8:37).

question: What does it mean Christ is our hope?
answer: He is the focus of our future expectation — the resurrection, our glorification, and life eternal with him (1 Timothy 1:1; Colossians 1:27).

question: What does it mean Christ is our life?
answer: In him is life, and apart from him we have no spiritual life; he is our life now and forever (Colossians 3:4).

question: What does it mean Christ is our joy?
answer: In his presence is fullness of joy; we rejoice in the Lord and find our gladness in him (Psalm 16:11; Philippians 4:4).

question: What does it mean Christ is our peace?
answer: He is the bond of our peace with God and with one another, having reconciled both Jew and Gentile in his body (Ephesians 2:14-17).

question: What does it mean Christ is our strength?
answer: In him we can do all things that he calls us to do; his strength is made perfect in our weakness (Philippians 4:13; 2 Corinthians 12:9).

question: What does it mean Christ is our refuge?
answer: In him we find shelter from God's judgment and the storms of life; he is a fortress for his people (Psalm 46; Hebrews 6:18).

question: What does it mean Christ is our portion?
answer: He himself is our true inheritance — he is enough, more than wealth or power could ever be (Psalm 73:25-26).

question: What does it mean Christ is our morning?
answer: As the bright morning star and the sun of righteousness, he chases away night and brings the dawn of new life (Malachi 4:2; Revelation 22:16).

question: What does it mean Christ is our anchor?
answer: Our hope in him is the anchor of the soul, sure and steadfast, entering into the inner sanctuary (Hebrews 6:19).

question: What does it mean Christ is our captain?
answer: He is the captain of our salvation, the pioneer who has gone before us into glory (Hebrews 2:10).

question: What does it mean Christ is our forerunner?
answer: He has gone before us into the heavenly sanctuary, opening the way for us (Hebrews 6:20).

question: What does it mean Christ is our judge?
answer: All judgment has been given to him; he will judge the living and the dead in righteousness (Acts 17:31; John 5:22).

question: What does it mean Christ is our king?
answer: He reigns sovereign over all and over his redeemed people; we owe him allegiance and joyful obedience as Lord.

question: What does it mean Christ is our priest?
answer: He is the great High Priest, having offered himself once for all and now interceding for us before the Father (Hebrews 4:14-16; 7:25).

question: What does it mean Christ is our sacrifice?
answer: He is the one perfect sacrifice — the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world by his shed blood (John 1:29; Hebrews 9:26).

question: What does it mean Christ is our advocate?
answer: If any believer sins, we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous (1 John 2:1).

question: What does it mean Christ is our intercessor?
answer: He ever lives to make intercession for those who draw near to God through him (Hebrews 7:25; Romans 8:34).

question: What does it mean Christ is our husband?
answer: As the Bridegroom, he loves and cherishes the church, his bride, having given himself for her (Ephesians 5:25-27; Revelation 19:7-9).

question: What does it mean Christ is our friend?
answer: He calls us friends, has revealed the Father to us, and laid down his life for us (John 15:13-15).

question: What does it mean Christ is our brother?
answer: He is not ashamed to call us brethren, having shared our flesh and blood (Hebrews 2:11-17).

question: What does it mean Christ is the head of every man?
answer: It means he is the rightful authority over every man, calling forth devotion and obedience under God (1 Corinthians 11:3).
