question: What does the Bible say about a chosen race?
answer: Ye are an elect race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people for God's own possession (1 Peter 2:9).

question: What does the Bible say about godly conduct?
answer: Having your behavior seemly among the Gentiles; that... they may by your good works, which they behold, glorify God (1 Peter 2:12).

question: What does the Bible say about silver and gold?
answer: Silver and gold have I none; but what I have, that give I thee. In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, walk (Acts 3:6).

question: What does the Bible say about the certainty of salvation?
answer: In none other is there salvation: for neither is there any other name under heaven... wherein we must be saved (Acts 4:12).

question: What does the Bible say about obeying God rather than men?
answer: We must obey God rather than men (Acts 5:29).

question: What does the Bible say about the early church's growth?
answer: The Lord added to them day by day those that were saved (Acts 2:47).

question: What does the Bible say about prayer in the early church?
answer: And they all with one accord continued stedfastly in prayer (Acts 1:14).

question: What does the Bible say about boldness in prayer?
answer: When they had prayed, the place was shaken... and they spake the word of God with boldness (Acts 4:31).

question: What does the Bible say about disciples being rooted in Christ?
answer: As therefore ye received Christ Jesus the Lord, so walk in him, rooted and builded up in him, and established in your faith (Colossians 2:6-7).

question: What does the Bible say about the believer's freedom in Christ?
answer: For freedom did Christ set us free: stand fast therefore, and be not entangled again in a yoke of bondage (Galatians 5:1).

question: What does the Bible say about being delivered from darkness?
answer: He delivered us out of the power of darkness, and translated us into the kingdom of the Son of his love (Colossians 1:13).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's reconciling work?
answer: It was the good pleasure of the Father... through him to reconcile all things unto himself, having made peace through the blood of his cross (Colossians 1:19-20).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's sufficiency?
answer: Ye are made full in him, who is the head of all principality and power (Colossians 2:10).

question: What does the Bible say about not being deceived?
answer: Take heed lest there shall be any one that maketh spoil of you through his philosophy and vain deceit, after the tradition of men (Colossians 2:8).

question: What does the Bible say about future rewards?
answer: Whatsoever ye do, work heartily, as unto the Lord, and not unto men; knowing that from the Lord ye shall receive the recompense of the inheritance (Colossians 3:23-24).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ as our life?
answer: When Christ, who is our life, shall be manifested, then shall ye also with him be manifested in glory (Colossians 3:4).

question: What does the Bible say about putting away wickedness?
answer: Put away from your mouth all anger, wrath, malice, railing, shameful speaking out of your mouth (Colossians 3:8).

question: What does the Bible say about putting on Christ's character?
answer: Put on therefore, as God's elect, holy and beloved, a heart of compassion, kindness, lowliness, meekness, longsuffering (Colossians 3:12).

question: What does the Bible say about love as the bond of perfection?
answer: Above all these things put on love, which is the bond of perfectness (Colossians 3:14).

question: What does the Bible say about ruling peace in our hearts?
answer: Let the peace of Christ rule in your hearts, to the which also ye were called in one body; and be ye thankful (Colossians 3:15).

question: What does the Bible say about the word dwelling richly?
answer: Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly; in all wisdom teaching and admonishing one another (Colossians 3:16).

question: What does the Bible say about doing all in Christ's name?
answer: Whatsoever ye do, in word or in deed, do all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father through him (Colossians 3:17).

question: What does the Bible say about prayer with thanksgiving?
answer: Continue stedfastly in prayer, watching therein with thanksgiving (Colossians 4:2).

question: What does the Bible say about gracious speech?
answer: Let your speech be always with grace, seasoned with salt, that ye may know how ye ought to answer each one (Colossians 4:6).

question: What does the Bible say about commending faith?
answer: For your faith and love, which ye have toward all the saints, because of the hope which is laid up for you in the heavens (Colossians 1:4-5).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ in you?
answer: To whom God was pleased to make known... what is the riches of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles, which is Christ in you, the hope of glory (Colossians 1:27).

question: What does the Bible say about complete in Christ?
answer: That we may present every man perfect in Christ (Colossians 1:28).

question: What does the Bible say about loving the brethren?
answer: By this shall all men know that ye are my disciples, if ye have love one to another (John 13:35).

question: What does the Bible say about being known by love?
answer: If we love one another, God abideth in us, and his love is perfected in us (1 John 4:12).

question: What does the Bible say about loving God we cannot see?
answer: He that loveth not his brother whom he hath seen, cannot love God whom he hath not seen (1 John 4:20).

question: What does the Bible say about overcoming the world?
answer: Whatsoever is begotten of God overcometh the world: and this is the victory that hath overcome the world, even our faith (1 John 5:4).

question: What does the Bible say about the witness in believers?
answer: He that believeth on the Son of God hath the witness in him (1 John 5:10).

question: What does the Bible say about idols?
answer: My little children, guard yourselves from idols (1 John 5:21).

question: What does the Bible say about loving the world?
answer: Love not the world, neither the things that are in the world. If any man love the world, the love of the Father is not in him (1 John 2:15).

question: What does the Bible say about anointing of the Spirit?
answer: Ye have an anointing from the Holy One, and ye know all things... his anointing teacheth you concerning all things (1 John 2:20, 27).

question: What does the Bible say about confidence in his coming?
answer: Now, my little children, abide in him; that, if he shall be manifested, we may have boldness, and not be ashamed before him at his coming (1 John 2:28).

question: What does the Bible say about being children of God now?
answer: Beloved, now are we children of God, and it is not yet made manifest what we shall be... but we know that, if he shall be manifested, we shall be like him (1 John 3:2).

question: What does the Bible say about purifying ourselves?
answer: Every one that hath this hope set on him purifieth himself, even as he is pure (1 John 3:3).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ destroying the devil's works?
answer: To this end was the Son of God manifested, that he might destroy the works of the devil (1 John 3:8).

question: What does the Bible say about laying down our lives?
answer: Hereby know we love, because he laid down his life for us: and we ought to lay down our lives for the brethren (1 John 3:16).

question: What does the Bible say about loving in deed and truth?
answer: My little children, let us not love in word, neither with the tongue; but in deed and truth (1 John 3:18).

question: What does the Bible say about an evil heart of unbelief?
answer: Take heed, brethren, lest haply there shall be in any one of you an evil heart of unbelief, in falling away from the living God (Hebrews 3:12).

question: What does the Bible say about exhorting one another?
answer: Exhort one another day by day, so long as it is called Today (Hebrews 3:13).

question: What does the Bible say about laboring to enter God's rest?
answer: Let us therefore give diligence to enter into that rest, that no man fall after the same example of disobedience (Hebrews 4:11).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's compassion as our High Priest?
answer: We have not a high priest that cannot be touched with the feeling of our infirmities; but one that hath been in all points tempted like as we are, yet without sin (Hebrews 4:15).

question: What does the Bible say about pressing on?
answer: Let us press on unto perfection (Hebrews 6:1).

question: What does the Bible say about the soul's anchor?
answer: Which we have as an anchor of the soul, a hope both sure and stedfast and entering into that which is within the veil (Hebrews 6:19).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's continuing priesthood?
answer: He, because he abideth for ever, hath his priesthood unchangeable (Hebrews 7:24).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's saving to the uttermost?
answer: He is able to save to the uttermost them that draw near unto God through him, seeing he ever liveth to make intercession for them (Hebrews 7:25).

question: What does the Bible say about Christ's once-for-all offering?
answer: By one offering he hath perfected for ever them that are sanctified (Hebrews 10:14).

question: What does the Bible say about boldness to enter the holy place?
answer: Having therefore, brethren, boldness to enter into the holy place by the blood of Jesus, by the way which he dedicated for us, a new and living way (Hebrews 10:19-20).

question: What does the Bible say about drawing near?
answer: Let us draw near with a true heart in fulness of faith, having our hearts sprinkled from an evil conscience (Hebrews 10:22).

question: What does the Bible say about provoking love?
answer: Let us consider one another to provoke unto love and good works (Hebrews 10:24).

question: What does the Bible say about not forsaking assembling?
answer: Not forsaking our own assembling together, as the custom of some is, but exhorting one another (Hebrews 10:25).

question: What does the Bible say about the just living by faith?
answer: My righteous one shall live by faith: and if he shrink back, my soul hath no pleasure in him (Hebrews 10:38).

question: What does the Bible say about not shrinking back?
answer: We are not of them that shrink back unto perdition; but of them that have faith unto the saving of the soul (Hebrews 10:39).

question: What does the Bible say about pleasing God?
answer: Without faith it is impossible to be well-pleasing unto him; for he that cometh to God must believe that he is, and that he is a rewarder of them that seek after him (Hebrews 11:6).

question: What does the Bible say about strangers and pilgrims?
answer: They confessed that they were strangers and pilgrims on the earth (Hebrews 11:13).

question: What does the Bible say about a better country?
answer: They desire a better country, that is, a heavenly: wherefore God is not ashamed of them, to be called their God: for he hath prepared for them a city (Hebrews 11:16).

question: What does the Bible say about a great cloud of witnesses?
answer: Therefore let us also, seeing we are compassed about with so great a cloud of witnesses, lay aside every weight (Hebrews 12:1).

question: What does the Bible say about Jesus our pioneer?
answer: Looking unto Jesus the author and perfecter of our faith, who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising shame (Hebrews 12:2).

question: What does the Bible say about chastening?
answer: Whom the Lord loveth he chasteneth, and scourgeth every son whom he receiveth (Hebrews 12:6).

question: What does the Bible say about straight paths?
answer: Make straight paths for your feet, that that which is lame be not turned out of the way, but rather be healed (Hebrews 12:13).

question: What does the Bible say about peace and sanctification?
answer: Follow after peace with all men, and the sanctification without which no man shall see the Lord (Hebrews 12:14).

question: What does the Bible say about Mount Zion vs. Sinai?
answer: Ye are come unto mount Zion, and unto the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem, and to innumerable hosts of angels (Hebrews 12:22-23).

question: What does the Bible say about an unshakable kingdom?
answer: Wherefore, receiving a kingdom that cannot be shaken, let us have grace, whereby we may offer service well-pleasing to God with reverence and awe (Hebrews 12:28).

question: What does the Bible say about brotherly love continuing?
answer: Let love of the brethren continue (Hebrews 13:1).

question: What does the Bible say about marriage being honorable?
answer: Let marriage be had in honor among all, and let the bed be undefiled (Hebrews 13:4).

question: What does the Bible say about helpful in trouble?
answer: The Lord is my helper; I will not fear: What shall man do unto me? (Hebrews 13:6).

question: What does the Bible say about the same Christ?
answer: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday and to-day, yea and for ever (Hebrews 13:8).

question: What does the Bible say about faithful leaders?
answer: Remember them that had the rule over you, men that spake unto you the word of God; and considering the issue of their life, imitate their faith (Hebrews 13:7).

question: What does the Bible say about going to Christ outside the camp?
answer: Let us therefore go forth unto him without the camp, bearing his reproach (Hebrews 13:13).

question: What does the Bible say about the sacrifice of praise?
answer: Through him then let us offer up a sacrifice of praise to God continually, that is, the fruit of lips which make confession to his name (Hebrews 13:15).

question: What does the Bible say about doing good and sharing?
answer: To do good and to communicate forget not: for with such sacrifices God is well pleased (Hebrews 13:16).

question: What does the Bible say about obeying leaders?
answer: Obey them that have the rule over you, and submit to them: for they watch in behalf of your souls (Hebrews 13:17).

question: What does the Bible say about benediction in Hebrews 13?
answer: Now the God of peace, who brought again from the dead the great shepherd of the sheep with the blood of an eternal covenant... make you perfect in every good thing to do his will (Hebrews 13:20-21).

question: What does Revelation 1:5-6 declare?
answer: Unto him that loveth us, and loosed us from our sins by his blood; and he made us to be a kingdom, to be priests unto his God and Father; to him be the glory and the dominion forever and ever.

question: What does Revelation 4:11 declare?
answer: Worthy art thou, our Lord and our God, to receive the glory and the honor and the power: for thou didst create all things, and because of thy will they were, and were created.

question: What does Revelation 5:9-10 declare?
answer: Worthy art thou to take the book... for thou wast slain, and didst purchase unto God with thy blood men of every tribe... and madest them to be unto our God a kingdom and priests.

question: What does Revelation 5:12 declare?
answer: Worthy is the Lamb that hath been slain to receive the power, and riches, and wisdom, and might, and honor, and glory, and blessing.

question: What does Revelation 7:9 declare?
answer: After these things I saw, and behold, a great multitude, which no man could number, out of every nation and of all tribes and peoples and tongues, standing before the throne and before the Lamb.

question: What does Revelation 7:14 declare?
answer: These are they that come out of the great tribulation, and they washed their robes, and made them white in the blood of the Lamb.

question: What does Revelation 7:17 declare?
answer: The Lamb that is in the midst of the throne shall be their shepherd, and shall guide them unto fountains of waters of life: and God shall wipe away every tear from their eyes.

question: What does Revelation 11:15 declare?
answer: The kingdom of the world is become the kingdom of our Lord, and of his Christ: and he shall reign for ever and ever.

question: What does Revelation 12:11 declare?
answer: They overcame him because of the blood of the Lamb, and because of the word of their testimony; and they loved not their life even unto death.

question: What does Revelation 21:5 declare?
answer: Behold, I make all things new.

question: What does Revelation 22:3 declare?
answer: There shall be no curse any more: and the throne of God and of the Lamb shall be therein: and his servants shall serve him.

question: What does Revelation 22:4 declare?
answer: They shall see his face; and his name shall be on their foreheads.

question: What does Revelation 22:5 declare?
answer: There shall be night no more; and they need no light of lamp, neither light of sun; for the Lord God shall give them light: and they shall reign for ever and ever.

question: What does Revelation 22:7 declare?
answer: And behold, I come quickly. Blessed is he that keepeth the words of the prophecy of this book.

question: What does Revelation 22:11 declare?
answer: He that is unrighteous, let him do unrighteousness still... and he that is righteous, let him do righteousness still.

question: What does Revelation 22:12 declare?
answer: Behold, I come quickly; and my reward is with me, to render to each man according as his work is.

question: What does Revelation 22:17 declare?
answer: The Spirit and the bride say, Come. And he that heareth, let him say, Come. And he that is athirst, let him come: he that will, let him take the water of life freely.

question: How can I tell if I'm truly saved?
answer: You can tell by looking for true repentance and faith, the witness of the Spirit, fruit of new life (love, obedience, longing for God's Word), and trusting God's promises in the gospel.

question: What if I sinned the same sin again?
answer: Confess it, repent, ask the Spirit's help, take practical steps to flee, and lean again on Christ's grace; do not despair — God is faithful to forgive (1 John 1:9).

question: What does it mean to walk humbly?
answer: It means to walk with God in awareness of his greatness and your dependence — quick to listen, slow to assume, willing to serve, glad to repent.

question: What does it mean to count others better than yourself?
answer: It means letting love, not pride, shape how you regard people — looking to their interests, esteeming them as image-bearers (Philippians 2:3-4).

question: What does it mean Christ humbled himself?
answer: He emptied himself, took the form of a servant, was made in the likeness of men, and humbled himself, becoming obedient unto death, even the death of the cross (Philippians 2:7-8).

question: What does it mean every knee will bow?
answer: At Jesus' name every knee shall bow, of things in heaven and things on earth and things under the earth, and every tongue shall confess Jesus Christ is Lord (Philippians 2:10-11).

question: What does it mean to work out salvation?
answer: Live out the salvation God has given, with reverent care; for God is at work in you both to will and to work for his good pleasure (Philippians 2:12-13).

question: What does it mean to be blameless among a crooked generation?
answer: To live without complaining or fault-finding, shining as lights in the world by holding forth the word of life (Philippians 2:14-16).

question: What does it mean to know Christ?
answer: To know him means to count all things as loss for the surpassing worth of knowing him as Lord, sharing in his death, and the power of his resurrection (Philippians 3:8-10).

question: What does it mean to forget what is behind?
answer: To leave former failures and successes, refuse to be paralyzed by either, and press on toward the goal in Christ (Philippians 3:13-14).

question: What does it mean Christ will transform our bodies?
answer: He will fashion anew the body of our humiliation and conform it to the body of his glory, by his power to subject all things to himself (Philippians 3:21).

question: What does it mean to stand fast in the Lord?
answer: To remain firm in faith, holding fast to Christ, refusing to be moved by trial or temptation (Philippians 4:1).

question: What does it mean to live in agreement?
answer: To pursue unity of mind, sharing the same gospel and love, and resolving disputes humbly in the Lord (Philippians 4:2-3).

question: What does it mean to think on these things?
answer: To meditate on whatever is true, honorable, just, pure, lovely, and of good report — letting Scripture and Christ's beauty fill the mind (Philippians 4:8).

question: What does it mean Christ strengthens us?
answer: In every circumstance — abasement or abundance — Christ gives strength to be content and to do all that he calls us to (Philippians 4:13).

question: What does it mean to feed on Christ?
answer: To trust in him, meditate on his Word, and rest in his finished work as the bread of life that satisfies your soul (John 6:35, 53-58).

question: What does it mean to drink from his Spirit?
answer: To welcome the Holy Spirit's life and power, drinking deeply from God by faith, refreshed by his presence (John 7:37-39).

question: What does it mean to be slow to speak?
answer: To listen carefully and consider before speaking, restraining the tongue and not stoking conflict (James 1:19).

question: What does it mean to be a doer of the Word?
answer: Not merely hearing but obeying — letting Scripture shape life, action, and habits (James 1:22).

question: What does it mean to visit the fatherless and widows?
answer: To care actively for those in distress and to keep oneself unspotted from the world is pure religion before God (James 1:27).

question: What does it mean to fulfill the royal law?
answer: To love your neighbor as yourself, refusing partiality (James 2:8).

question: What does it mean faith without works is dead?
answer: Saving faith inevitably produces obedient love; if it is alone, it is no real, living faith at all (James 2:17, 26).

question: What does it mean tongue is a fire?
answer: It can ignite a great destruction; we are to bridle it, lest it inflame our whole life (James 3:5-8).

question: What does it mean wisdom from above is pure?
answer: It is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, easy to be entreated, full of mercy and good fruits, without partiality, without hypocrisy (James 3:17).

question: What does it mean to come near to God?
answer: To draw near in repentance, prayer, and faith — and he draws near to you (James 4:8).

question: What does it mean to humble ourselves?
answer: To lay down pride, confess sin, depend on God, and serve others — God will exalt the humble in due time (James 4:10; 1 Peter 5:6).

question: What does it mean to confess sin to one another?
answer: To bring sin into the light with trustworthy fellow believers, praying for one another and seeing healing flow through honesty (James 5:16).

question: What does it mean to obey the truth?
answer: To bring our lives in line with God's revelation in Christ, by the power of the Holy Spirit (1 Peter 1:22).

question: What does it mean to love one another fervently?
answer: To love deeply from the heart, since the Spirit has cleansed our souls in obeying the truth (1 Peter 1:22).

question: What does it mean we are like newborn babes?
answer: We must long for the pure spiritual milk of God's Word so we may grow up unto salvation (1 Peter 2:2).

question: What does it mean Christ is the chief cornerstone?
answer: He is the foundation stone the builders rejected, now made the head of the corner — the basis of the church (1 Peter 2:6-7).

question: What does it mean we are foreigners and exiles?
answer: We are pilgrims passing through, abstaining from fleshly desires that war against the soul, with hearts set on heaven (1 Peter 2:11).

question: What does it mean to be subject to authorities?
answer: To submit to every ordinance of man for the Lord's sake — kings, governors, masters — that the gospel may be commended (1 Peter 2:13-14).

question: What does it mean Christ left us an example?
answer: He suffered for us, leaving us an example, that we should follow his steps (1 Peter 2:21).

question: What does it mean Christ bore our sins?
answer: His own self bare our sins in his body upon the tree, that we, having died unto sins, might live unto righteousness (1 Peter 2:24).

question: What does it mean to sanctify Christ as Lord?
answer: To set him apart in your heart as Lord, ready to give a reason for your hope with gentleness and reverence (1 Peter 3:15).

question: What does it mean to live the rest of our time?
answer: To live not in the lusts of men but in the will of God, knowing the time past sufficed for the world's ways (1 Peter 4:1-3).

question: What does it mean to be hospitable without grudging?
answer: To welcome others gladly into our lives and homes, sharing what God has given without complaint (1 Peter 4:9).

question: What does it mean to glorify God in fiery trials?
answer: To rejoice insomuch as we partake of Christ's sufferings, that we may also rejoice with exceeding joy at his revelation (1 Peter 4:13).

question: What does it mean to entrust our souls to God?
answer: In suffering, those who suffer according to God's will commit their souls in well-doing unto a faithful Creator (1 Peter 4:19).

question: What does it mean elders shepherd God's flock?
answer: They tend the flock with willing hearts, not for shameful gain, not domineering, but as examples (1 Peter 5:2-3).

question: What does it mean younger ones submit to elders?
answer: They are to receive the godly leadership of the elders and clothe themselves with humility toward all (1 Peter 5:5).

question: What does it mean God resists the proud?
answer: He opposes the proud but gives grace to the humble; therefore humble yourself under his mighty hand (1 Peter 5:5-6).

question: What does it mean to cast all anxiety on him?
answer: To take every care to God in prayer with thanksgiving, knowing he cares for you (1 Peter 5:7).

question: What does it mean the devil walks like a roaring lion?
answer: Our adversary seeks to devour us; we are to be sober, watchful, and resist him steadfast in faith (1 Peter 5:8-9).

question: What does it mean God restores after suffering?
answer: After we have suffered a little while, the God of all grace will himself perfect, establish, and strengthen us (1 Peter 5:10).

question: What does it mean we have all things for life and godliness?
answer: His divine power has granted everything we need for life and godliness through the knowledge of him (2 Peter 1:3).

question: What does it mean we are partakers of the divine nature?
answer: By his great and precious promises, we share in his holy life, escaping the corruption that is in the world by lust (2 Peter 1:4).

question: What does it mean to add to faith?
answer: In your faith supply virtue; and in your virtue knowledge; and in your knowledge self-control; and patience; and godliness; and brotherly kindness; and love (2 Peter 1:5-7).

question: What does it mean to make our calling sure?
answer: By practicing these virtues; for if you do these things, you will never stumble, and entrance into the eternal kingdom will be richly provided (2 Peter 1:10-11).

question: What does it mean to remember your salvation in Christ?
answer: Stir up your mind by way of remembrance, and grow in grace and knowledge of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ (2 Peter 1:13; 3:18).

question: What does it mean Scripture's prophecy is no private interpretation?
answer: Holy men spake as they were moved by the Holy Spirit, not by their own will (2 Peter 1:21).

question: What does it mean false teachers will rise?
answer: They will privately bring in destructive heresies, even denying the Master that bought them, bringing swift destruction (2 Peter 2:1).

question: What does it mean the Lord's day comes as a thief?
answer: Suddenly, when not expected, the day of the Lord will come, with all the heavens passing away with great noise (2 Peter 3:10).

question: What does it mean we look for new heavens and earth?
answer: According to his promise, we look for new heavens and a new earth, wherein dwelleth righteousness (2 Peter 3:13).

question: What does it mean to be diligent for that day?
answer: Give diligence to be found in peace, without spot and blameless in his sight (2 Peter 3:14).

question: What does it mean we have heard, seen, handled the Word of life?
answer: The apostles bore witness to Christ's real life, death, and resurrection — eyewitnesses of the eternal life made manifest (1 John 1:1-3).

question: What does it mean fellowship with the Father and the Son?
answer: To share life with God through Christ — the basis of all true Christian fellowship and joy (1 John 1:3-4).

question: What does it mean to walk in the light as he is in the light?
answer: To live transparently before God, agreeing with truth, and finding fellowship and cleansing through Christ's blood (1 John 1:7).

question: What does it mean we have an Advocate?
answer: If any sin, we have an Advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous, who is the propitiation for our sins (1 John 2:1-2).

question: What does it mean to know him by keeping his commandments?
answer: True knowledge of God shows itself in obedience; he that saith 'I know him,' and keepeth not his commandments, is a liar (1 John 2:3-4).

question: What does it mean to overcome the evil one?
answer: In the Word of God abiding in us, we are strong and have overcome the evil one (1 John 2:14).

question: What does it mean lust of the flesh, lust of the eyes, and pride of life?
answer: All that is in the world — the lust of the flesh, the lust of the eyes, and the vainglory of life — is not of the Father (1 John 2:16).

question: What does it mean to abide in Christ now?
answer: My little children, abide in him; that, if he shall be manifested, we may have boldness, and not be ashamed before him at his coming (1 John 2:28).

question: What does it mean to be born of God?
answer: To be regenerated by the Spirit, marked by righteousness, love, faith, and victory over the world (1 John 2:29; 5:1, 4).

question: What does it mean Christ takes away sins?
answer: He was manifested to take away sins; and in him is no sin (1 John 3:5).

question: What does it mean to live as God's children?
answer: He that doeth righteousness is righteous, even as he is righteous; the children of God are evident by love and obedience (1 John 3:7, 10).

question: What does it mean love is laid down for us?
answer: Hereby know we love, because he laid down his life for us: and we ought to lay down our lives for the brethren (1 John 3:16).

question: What does it mean to please God in obedience?
answer: Whatsoever we ask we receive, because we keep his commandments, and do the things that are pleasing in his sight (1 John 3:22).

question: What does it mean to test the spirits?
answer: Try the spirits, whether they are of God; many false prophets are gone out into the world (1 John 4:1).

question: What does it mean to confess Jesus Christ come in flesh?
answer: This is the test of true doctrine: every spirit that confesseth Jesus Christ is come in the flesh is of God (1 John 4:2-3).

question: What does it mean greater is he in you?
answer: Greater is he that is in you than he that is in the world (1 John 4:4).

question: What does it mean love is of God?
answer: Beloved, let us love one another: for love is of God; every one that loveth is begotten of God (1 John 4:7).

question: What does it mean God sent his Son as propitiation?
answer: Herein is love, not that we loved God, but that he loved us, and sent his Son to be the propitiation for our sins (1 John 4:10).

question: What does it mean to dwell in love and in God?
answer: He that abideth in love abideth in God, and God abideth in him (1 John 4:16).

question: What does it mean we love because he first loved us?
answer: Our love is the response to God's prior, unconditional love demonstrated supremely in giving Christ (1 John 4:19).

question: What does it mean believing is overcoming?
answer: Whosoever believeth that Jesus is the Christ is begotten of God; and this is the victory that hath overcome the world, even our faith (1 John 5:1, 4).

question: What does it mean these things are written?
answer: These things have I written... that ye may know that ye have eternal life, even unto you that believe on the name of the Son of God (1 John 5:13).

question: What does it mean to confess we have sin?
answer: If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us (1 John 1:8).

question: What does it mean Christ is faithful and just?
answer: If we confess our sins, he is faithful and righteous to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness (1 John 1:9).

question: What does it mean blessed are those who hear and keep?
answer: Blessed is he that readeth, and they that hear the words of the prophecy, and keep the things that are written therein: for the time is at hand (Revelation 1:3).

question: What does it mean Christ has the keys of death and Hades?
answer: He holds authority over death and the realm of the dead — the dead and the living are in his hand (Revelation 1:18).

question: What does it mean to be the bride of the Lamb?
answer: It means the church, in all her redeemed members, is wedded to Christ in covenant love, awaiting the marriage supper (Revelation 19:7-9).

question: What does it mean Christ rules with a rod of iron?
answer: He rules the nations with absolute, righteous authority, breaking all rebellion (Revelation 19:15; Psalm 2:9).

question: What does it mean a thousand years and Satan bound?
answer: For a millennial period, Satan is bound and Christ reigns; Christians interpret the timing differently, but all see Christ's victorious rule.

question: What does it mean a new heavens and a new earth?
answer: God will renew the cosmos in righteousness, dwelling with his people forever, free from sin, sorrow, and death (Revelation 21:1-4; 2 Peter 3:13).

question: What does it mean the city has no temple?
answer: In the New Jerusalem, the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb are its temple — God himself dwells in unmediated presence with us (Revelation 21:22).

question: What does it mean nations walk by its light?
answer: The Lamb is the lamp of the city; the nations walk by his light, kings bring their glory to him (Revelation 21:23-24).

question: What does it mean nothing unclean enters?
answer: Nothing unclean shall ever enter the city, but only those whose names are written in the Lamb's book of life (Revelation 21:27).

question: What does it mean the leaves are for healing?
answer: The leaves of the tree of life are for the healing of the nations — restoration of all that sin had broken (Revelation 22:2).

question: What does it mean the curse is gone?
answer: In the new creation, the curse from the fall is removed forever; God's people are restored to full life with him (Revelation 22:3).

question: What does it mean we shall reign forever?
answer: They shall reign with Christ for ever and ever in the new creation (Revelation 22:5; 2 Timothy 2:12).

question: What does it mean to keep these words?
answer: Blessed is he that keepeth the words of the prophecy of this book — letting Christ's truth shape life today and prepare us for his coming (Revelation 22:7).

question: What does it mean Christ is the bright morning star?
answer: He is the dawn of new day, the herald of resurrection light, the desire of his people (Revelation 22:16).

question: What does it mean grace be with the saints?
answer: It is the closing benediction of Scripture — God's grace given freely to all who belong to Christ, through to eternity (Revelation 22:21).

question: How does the believer live in light of Christ's return?
answer: Faithfully, watchfully, joyfully, fruitfully — purified by hope and pressing on in love until he comes (1 John 3:3; Titus 2:11-13).

question: How does the believer face death?
answer: With confident trust, knowing that to depart is to be with Christ, which is far better, awaiting the resurrection of the body.

question: How does the gospel give purpose for our days?
answer: It tells us we are loved, redeemed, gifted, and sent — to glorify God, love others, share Christ, and look for his coming.

question: How does the gospel define success?
answer: Not by human achievement, but by faithfulness to Christ, growth in love, and fruit that lasts unto eternity (1 Corinthians 4:1-5).

question: What is the Christian's true wealth?
answer: Christ himself, the unsearchable riches of his grace, the inheritance kept in heaven for us — incorruptible and undefiled.

question: What is the Christian's truest joy?
answer: To know God in Christ, to be loved by him, to be adopted as his child, and to dwell with him forever.

question: What is the Christian's enduring hope?
answer: The blessed hope and glorious appearing of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ, who will make all things new.

question: What is the Christian's call?
answer: To know God, to love God, to glorify God, to make God known, and to dwell with God forever — through Jesus Christ our Lord.

question: What is the Christian's final song?
answer: Worthy is the Lamb that hath been slain to receive the power, and riches, and wisdom, and might, and honor, and glory, and blessing — forever and ever.

question: What does it mean to walk worthy of our calling?
answer: To live in lowliness, meekness, longsuffering, forbearing one another in love, eager to keep the unity of the Spirit (Ephesians 4:1-3).

question: What does it mean to be transformed by renewing the mind?
answer: By immersing your mind in Scripture and the Spirit's truth, your thoughts and affections are reshaped to align with God's will (Romans 12:2).

question: What does it mean to be conformed to Christ's image?
answer: To be made progressively like him in character — humility, holiness, love, and obedience — by the Spirit's work (Romans 8:29; 2 Corinthians 3:18).

question: What does it mean Christ is the head of the corner?
answer: He is the foundation and bond of the church, holding it together and giving it shape and stability (Ephesians 2:20-22).

question: What does it mean the church is one body in Christ?
answer: All believers — diverse but united — share one Lord, one faith, one baptism, and one Spirit (Ephesians 4:4-6).

question: What does it mean every member has a function?
answer: Each part of the body grows and builds itself up in love through what each member supplies, with Christ as head (Ephesians 4:15-16).

question: What does it mean to no longer walk as Gentiles?
answer: To turn from the futility, darkness, and impurity of the old life and walk in the light of new life in Christ (Ephesians 4:17-24).

question: What does it mean to be imitators of God?
answer: As beloved children, walk in love, even as Christ also loved us and gave himself up for us (Ephesians 5:1-2).

question: What does it mean to walk as children of light?
answer: To bear the fruit of light — goodness, righteousness, truth — and refuse the unfruitful works of darkness (Ephesians 5:8-11).

question: What does it mean to redeem the time?
answer: To make wise use of every opportunity for good, knowing the days are evil (Ephesians 5:15-16).

question: What does it mean wives submit to their husbands?
answer: Wives are to submit to their own husbands as to the Lord, with respect — picturing the church's reverence for Christ (Ephesians 5:22-24, 33).

question: What does it mean husbands love their wives?
answer: Husbands are to love their wives as Christ loved the church and gave himself for her — sacrificially and tenderly (Ephesians 5:25-30).

question: What does it mean children obey parents?
answer: Children, obey your parents in the Lord: for this is right; honor your father and mother — the first commandment with promise (Ephesians 6:1-3).

question: What does it mean fathers do not provoke?
answer: Fathers, provoke not your children to wrath: but nurture them in the chastening and admonition of the Lord (Ephesians 6:4).

question: What does it mean servants obey masters?
answer: Servants are to obey with sincerity of heart, as unto Christ, knowing that whatever good they do, the same they shall receive again from the Lord (Ephesians 6:5-8).

question: What does it mean masters render same to servants?
answer: Masters are to do the same toward servants — not threatening, knowing that their Master also is in heaven without partiality (Ephesians 6:9).

question: What does it mean to put on the whole armor of God?
answer: To equip yourself with truth, righteousness, the gospel of peace, faith, salvation, the Spirit's sword, and prayer — to stand against evil (Ephesians 6:10-18).

question: What does it mean to wrestle not against flesh and blood?
answer: Our struggle is against principalities and powers, the spiritual hosts of wickedness, requiring God's armor to stand (Ephesians 6:12).

question: What does it mean to pray at all seasons?
answer: With all prayer and supplication praying at all seasons in the Spirit, watching with all perseverance and supplication for all the saints (Ephesians 6:18).

question: What does it mean ambassadors in chains?
answer: Even bound, Paul represents Christ to the world; we are sent ones, no matter our circumstances (Ephesians 6:20).

question: What does it mean Christ is more than enough?
answer: In him are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge; he is the fullness of the Godhead bodily, sufficient for all of life and eternity (Colossians 2:3, 9-10).

question: What does it mean to set affections on things above?
answer: To turn our hearts toward the heavenly Christ — what is eternal — rather than the passing things of earth (Colossians 3:1-2).

question: How does Christ relate to the Old Testament law?
answer: Christ fulfills the law — its commandments, types, and prophecies — and gives the Spirit so that the law's righteous requirements are met in those who walk by him (Matthew 5:17; Romans 8:3-4).

question: How does Christ relate to Old Testament feasts?
answer: He is the substance of every feast — Passover Lamb, the Firstfruits of the resurrection, the giver of the Spirit at Pentecost, our Day of Atonement, and our coming Tabernacles.

question: How does Christ relate to Old Testament sacrifices?
answer: He is the perfect, once-for-all sacrifice that fulfills and ends them all — the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world (Hebrews 10:10-14).

question: How does Christ relate to Old Testament priesthood?
answer: He is the great High Priest after the order of Melchizedek — eternal, sinless, effective — who offered himself and now intercedes (Hebrews 7-8).

question: How does Christ relate to Old Testament prophets?
answer: He is the prophet greater than Moses, fulfilling all prophecy and being the final Word of God to humanity (Deuteronomy 18:15; Hebrews 1:1-2).

question: How does Christ relate to Old Testament kings?
answer: He is the Son of David, King of kings, fulfilling the Davidic covenant with an everlasting reign of righteousness.

question: How does Christ relate to Old Testament wisdom?
answer: He is the wisdom of God, in whom are hidden all treasures of wisdom and knowledge (1 Corinthians 1:24, 30; Colossians 2:3).

question: How does Christ relate to Old Testament temple?
answer: His own body is the true temple; in him God dwells, and through him we have access (John 2:19-22; Ephesians 2:19-22).

question: How does Christ relate to Old Testament promises?
answer: All God's promises find their 'yes' in him; in him our 'amen' is to the glory of God (2 Corinthians 1:20).

question: How does Christ relate to Old Testament covenants?
answer: He fulfills and seals them, especially establishing the new covenant in his blood for all who believe (Luke 22:20; Hebrews 8-10).

question: How does the Spirit work in believers' sanctification?
answer: He convicts of sin, applies Christ's word, produces fruit, mortifies the deeds of the body, and conforms us to Christ's image (Galatians 5:16-25; Romans 8:13).

question: What is the meaning of being a witness?
answer: To bear truthful testimony to what God has revealed in Christ, by life and word, declaring the gospel to others (Acts 1:8).

question: What is the meaning of being a servant?
answer: To imitate Christ in humble, sacrificial service of others, putting their good above your own and working for God's glory.

question: What is the meaning of being a soldier?
answer: To engage spiritual battles by faith, armed with the Spirit and the Word, enduring hardship as a good soldier of Christ (2 Timothy 2:3-4).

question: What is the meaning of being a runner?
answer: To run the Christian race with patience, fixed on Jesus, laying aside every weight and the sin which clings (Hebrews 12:1-2).

question: What is the meaning of being a builder?
answer: To build with care on the foundation of Christ, with materials that endure his refining fire (1 Corinthians 3:10-15).

question: What is the meaning of being a steward?
answer: To manage faithfully whatever God has entrusted — time, gifts, gospel, family — knowing one day we will give an account (1 Corinthians 4:1-2; 1 Peter 4:10).

question: What is the meaning of being a fisher of men?
answer: To proclaim the gospel and call others to follow Christ, just as Jesus called his fishermen disciples (Matthew 4:19).

question: What is the meaning of being a farmer of souls?
answer: To plant the gospel seed faithfully, water with prayer and care, and trust God to give the increase (1 Corinthians 3:6-8).

question: What is the meaning of being a shepherd of God's people?
answer: To care for, feed, lead, and protect those entrusted to your care under Christ the Chief Shepherd (1 Peter 5:1-4).

question: What is the meaning of being a wise virgin?
answer: To be ready and prepared for the Bridegroom's return, with oil for our lamps and faith for the day (Matthew 25:1-13).

question: What is the meaning of being faithful with talents?
answer: To use whatever God has given for his glory, multiplying his investment in lives changed and good works done (Matthew 25:14-30).

question: What is the meaning of being on Christ's right hand?
answer: To be among those whom the King welcomes as blessed, inheriting the kingdom prepared from the foundation of the world (Matthew 25:34).

question: What is the meaning of holding fast?
answer: To cling firmly to Christ and his Word, refusing to drift, resisting deception, persevering until the end (Hebrews 3:6, 14; 10:23).

question: What is the meaning of being established?
answer: To be rooted in faith, sound in doctrine, settled in Christ, not moved by every wind of teaching (Colossians 2:7; Ephesians 4:14).

question: What is the meaning of growing in love?
answer: To increase in self-giving devotion to God and others — love that overflows in works and abounds yet more (Philippians 1:9-11; 1 Thessalonians 3:12).

question: What is the meaning of growing in faith?
answer: To deepen confident trust in God's word and character through hearing his promises and walking in obedience (2 Corinthians 10:15; Romans 10:17).

question: What is the meaning of growing in knowledge?
answer: To know God in his Word and ways, increasing in understanding of who he is and what he has done (Colossians 1:9-10; 2 Peter 3:18).

question: What is the meaning of growing in holiness?
answer: To be made progressively more like Christ — holy, as he is holy — in heart, speech, and conduct (1 Peter 1:15-16; 2 Corinthians 7:1).

question: What is the meaning of growing in service?
answer: To increase in faithful, loving labor for the Lord and his people, using gifts well for the body's good (1 Corinthians 15:58).

question: What is the meaning of growing in worship?
answer: To deepen heartfelt adoration, gratitude, and obedience to God, with mind and life and song (Romans 12:1; Colossians 3:16).

question: What is the meaning of growing in prayer?
answer: To enrich communion with God — listening, asking, thanking, interceding — drawing near with confidence through Christ (Hebrews 4:16).

question: What is the meaning of growing in thankfulness?
answer: To increase in gratitude for God's grace, recognizing every good gift comes from him, and giving thanks in everything (Ephesians 5:20; 1 Thessalonians 5:18).

question: What is the meaning of growing in courage?
answer: To stand firm and speak truth without fear, encouraged by the Lord's presence and strength (Joshua 1:9; 2 Timothy 1:7).

question: What is the meaning of growing in patience?
answer: To increase in steadfast endurance under trial, longsuffering with people, sustained by hope (Romans 5:3-4; Galatians 5:22).

question: What is the meaning of growing in joy?
answer: To deepen delight in God despite circumstances — rejoicing in the Lord always (Philippians 4:4).

question: What is the meaning of growing in peace?
answer: To rest more deeply in God's care and Christ's victory, with hearts guarded by his peace (Philippians 4:7).

question: What is the meaning of growing in humility?
answer: To esteem others above yourself, depend on God more, and serve gladly out of love for him (Philippians 2:3-4; 1 Peter 5:5-6).

question: What is the meaning of growing in self-control?
answer: To increase in Spirit-empowered discipline of desires, words, and actions, refusing to be mastered by sin (Galatians 5:22-23; 1 Corinthians 9:25).

question: What is the meaning of growing in gentleness?
answer: To deepen meekness toward all — strong yet tender — even in correction (Galatians 6:1; 2 Timothy 2:24-25).

question: What is the meaning of growing in faithfulness?
answer: To increase in steady, reliable trust in God and consistent commitment to his people and his work (Galatians 5:22).

question: What is the meaning of growing in goodness?
answer: To bear active goodness — moral excellence and benevolence — that flows from union with Christ (Galatians 5:22; Ephesians 5:9).

question: What is the meaning of growing in kindness?
answer: To overflow with God's kindness toward others, doing good even to enemies (Galatians 5:22; Romans 12:20-21).

question: What is the meaning of growing in mercy?
answer: To be moved with compassion like Christ, showing mercy to the suffering and humble forgiveness to offenders (Luke 6:36).

question: What is the meaning of growing in zeal?
answer: To be fervent in spirit, serving the Lord, refusing lukewarmness, and pursuing his glory (Romans 12:11; Revelation 3:15-16).

question: What is the meaning of growing in wisdom?
answer: To increase in skillful living under God — applying truth in time, choices, and relationships (Proverbs 2; James 1:5; 3:17).

question: What is the meaning of growing in discernment?
answer: To increase in spiritual insight, judging rightly between good and evil, truth and error, light and darkness (Hebrews 5:14; Philippians 1:9-10).

question: What is the meaning of growing in obedience?
answer: To follow Christ in deeper, more consistent submission to his Word, by the Spirit's enabling power (John 14:15; Hebrews 5:8-9).

question: What is the meaning of growing in fellowship?
answer: To share life ever more deeply with believers — the Word, the table, prayer, ministry, and burdens (Acts 2:42; Galatians 6:2).

question: What is the meaning of growing in witness?
answer: To increase in courage, clarity, and compassion in sharing the gospel with all whom God brings across our path (Acts 1:8; 1 Peter 3:15).

question: What is the meaning of growing in giving?
answer: To enlarge generosity to God's work and to those in need, cheerful, sacrificial, and joyful (2 Corinthians 8-9).

question: What is the meaning of growing in suffering well?
answer: To bear trials with faith, without bitterness, learning Christ's character through them, refined like gold by fire (1 Peter 4:12-19).

question: What is the meaning of growing in hope?
answer: To be increasingly anchored in the certain promises of Christ — eternal life, his return, and the new creation (Romans 15:13; 1 Peter 1:3-9).

question: What is the meaning of growing in fellowship with Christ?
answer: To know him more, walk with him daily, and treasure him supremely as your friend, master, and Saviour (Philippians 3:8-10).

question: What is the meaning of growing in evangelism?
answer: To share the gospel more widely and faithfully — by life and lip — through the Spirit's empowering grace (Acts 4:31; 2 Timothy 4:5).

question: What is the meaning of growing in family worship?
answer: To increase in regular, intentional gatherings of the family in Scripture, prayer, and song (Deuteronomy 6:6-7; Ephesians 6:4).

question: What is the meaning of growing in church love?
answer: To love your local church, prioritize her gathering, serve her members, and rejoice and weep with the body (Hebrews 10:24-25; Romans 12:15).

question: What is the meaning of finishing well?
answer: To run faithfully to the end, persevering in love and obedience, and stand approved before Christ (2 Timothy 4:7-8).

question: What is the comfort of those who mourn in Christ?
answer: He comforts the brokenhearted, weeps with us, brings hope through resurrection, and will wipe every tear away (Psalm 34:18; Revelation 21:4).

question: What is the comfort of those who suffer for Christ?
answer: He shares our sufferings, strengthens our hearts, prepares an eternal weight of glory, and is honored in our endurance (2 Corinthians 4:17; 1 Peter 4:13).

question: What is the comfort of those persecuted for righteousness?
answer: Theirs is the kingdom of heaven; they share in the prophets' lot; great is their reward in heaven (Matthew 5:10-12).

question: What is the comfort of those who have suffered abuse?
answer: God sees, hears, and judges; he is near the brokenhearted, healing wounds and giving strength to walk in his light, surrounded by trustworthy help.

question: What is the comfort of those who feel forgotten?
answer: You are not forgotten by God; he engraves you on the palms of his hands and treasures you in his heart (Isaiah 49:15-16).

question: What is the comfort of those overwhelmed?
answer: From the end of the earth I will call unto thee, when my heart is overwhelmed; lead me to the rock that is higher than I (Psalm 61:2).

question: What is the comfort of those weary?
answer: Come unto me, all ye that labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest (Matthew 11:28).

question: What is the comfort of those poor in spirit?
answer: Theirs is the kingdom of heaven (Matthew 5:3) — God draws near to the humble who recognize their need.

question: What is the comfort of those who are afraid?
answer: Fear not, for I am with thee... I will strengthen thee, yea, I will help thee (Isaiah 41:10); perfect love casts out fear (1 John 4:18).

question: What is the comfort of those grieving sin?
answer: Godly sorrow produces repentance unto salvation; God is faithful and just to forgive when we confess (2 Corinthians 7:10; 1 John 1:9).

question: What is the comfort of those abandoned?
answer: The Lord will not leave thee nor forsake thee (Deuteronomy 31:6); when father and mother forsake, the Lord will take you up (Psalm 27:10).

question: What is the comfort of those misunderstood?
answer: He who searches all hearts knows you fully; entrust yourself to him who judges justly (1 Peter 2:23).

question: What is the comfort of those falsely accused?
answer: Blessed are ye when men shall reproach you, and persecute you, and say all manner of evil against you falsely, for my sake (Matthew 5:11).

question: What is the comfort of those facing illness?
answer: The Lord is your healer (Exodus 15:26); whether healing here or in glory, his grace is sufficient (2 Corinthians 12:9).

question: What is the comfort of those facing death?
answer: To depart and be with Christ is far better; death is gain for the believer (Philippians 1:21-23).

question: What is the comfort of those bereaved?
answer: Sorrow not, even as the rest, who have no hope (1 Thessalonians 4:13); we shall be reunited with believing loved ones at Christ's coming.

question: What is the comfort of those waiting?
answer: They that wait for Jehovah shall renew their strength (Isaiah 40:31); blessed are all they that wait for him (Isaiah 30:18).

question: What is the comfort of those weak in faith?
answer: He gives more grace; a bruised reed he will not break, and a smoking flax he will not quench (Isaiah 42:3).

question: What is the comfort of those who feel unforgivable?
answer: Christ saves the worst of sinners (1 Timothy 1:15); his blood cleanses all who come to him in repentant faith (1 John 1:7-9).

question: What is the comfort of those tempted?
answer: He has felt our temptations; he is able to succor those who are tempted; the Spirit makes a way of escape (Hebrews 2:18; 1 Corinthians 10:13).

question: What is the comfort of those tested?
answer: Trials produce steadfastness, refining faith more precious than gold; the Lord knows how to deliver his people (James 1:2-4; 2 Peter 2:9).

question: What is the comfort of those overworked?
answer: He gives his beloved sleep (Psalm 127:2); come to him for soul rest in his easy yoke (Matthew 11:28-30).

question: What is the comfort of those who feel useless?
answer: You are God's workmanship, created for good works he has prepared (Ephesians 2:10); not one is too small for his service.

question: What is the comfort of those failing in marriage?
answer: Bring it humbly before God, repent where needed, seek wise help, refuse to give up, and trust the Lord with what is broken.

question: What is the comfort of those failing as parents?
answer: God is a perfect Father where we are imperfect; cling to grace, repent and renew, and entrust your children to him in prayer.

question: What is the comfort of those failing at work?
answer: Take it to the Lord, work as unto him, learn humbly, refuse despair, and rest in God's care for tomorrow.

question: What is the comfort of those facing financial loss?
answer: My God shall supply every need of yours according to his riches in glory in Christ Jesus (Philippians 4:19); the Lord cares for his people.

question: What is the comfort of those uncertain about life?
answer: He directs our steps as we trust him; though we plan, the Lord establishes the way (Proverbs 16:9).

question: What is the comfort of those wounded by friends?
answer: Faithful are the wounds of a friend (Proverbs 27:6); but ultimately Christ is the friend who never fails.

question: What is the comfort of those facing death of dreams?
answer: His thoughts toward you are thoughts of peace; he gives a future and a hope, often beyond what we asked or thought (Jeremiah 29:11; Ephesians 3:20).

question: What is the comfort of those rejected?
answer: He was despised and rejected; he understands your pain and welcomes you to himself (Isaiah 53:3; John 6:37).

question: What is the comfort of those isolated?
answer: He is with you always (Matthew 28:20); he sets the lonely in families through the church (Psalm 68:6).

question: What is the comfort of those facing aging?
answer: Even to old age I am he, and even to hoar hairs will I carry you (Isaiah 46:4); his strength is renewed in those who wait.

question: What is the comfort of those facing chronic illness?
answer: Though our outward man is decaying, our inward man is renewed day by day (2 Corinthians 4:16); his grace is sufficient.

question: What is the comfort of those facing infertility?
answer: He is the God who hears and remembers; trust him with your story, lean on his people, and find his grace where his answers may differ from ours.

question: What is the comfort of those raising disabled children?
answer: Your child is fearfully and wonderfully made; lean on God's strength, his church family, and his good purposes (Psalm 139:14; John 9:3).

question: What is the comfort of those facing divorce trauma?
answer: God is near the brokenhearted; his grace is restoring; the body of Christ stands with you; he is the friend who will not abandon.

question: What is the comfort of those facing abuse?
answer: God sees and judges; cling to him, accept the help of trustworthy people and authorities, and rest in the Father who calls you precious.

question: What is the comfort of those facing addiction?
answer: In Christ we are not under sin's reign; he gives the Spirit of self-control; the body of Christ provides accountability and grace for the long road.

question: What is the comfort of those facing mental illness?
answer: You are deeply loved by God; he comforts the afflicted; he provides his Word, his people, and qualified help — and his presence does not depend on your feelings.

question: What is the comfort of those troubled by past sin?
answer: Christ has paid the full price; he removes our transgressions as far as the east is from the west; in him there is no condemnation (Psalm 103:12; Romans 8:1).

question: What is the comfort of those troubled by family pain?
answer: God restores broken families; he is the Father of the fatherless and welcomes you into his family even now (Psalm 68:5-6; Ephesians 1:5).

question: What is the comfort of those facing big decisions?
answer: Bring them to God in prayer with Scripture and counsel; he gives wisdom liberally to those who ask in faith (James 1:5).

question: What is the comfort of those facing setbacks?
answer: He works all things together for good to those who love him (Romans 8:28); his timing is perfect, and his hand is not shortened.

question: What is the comfort of those mocked for faith?
answer: You share in the reproach of Christ; great is your reward in heaven; the Spirit of glory rests on you (1 Peter 4:14).

question: What is the comfort of those tired in ministry?
answer: Come away to a quiet place; rest in the Lord; in due season we shall reap if we faint not (Mark 6:31; Galatians 6:9).

question: What is the comfort of those overwhelmed by sin's reach?
answer: Where sin abounded, grace did much more abound; we shall reign in life by Christ Jesus (Romans 5:20-21).

question: What is the comfort of those persecuted for the gospel?
answer: Be of good cheer; I have overcome the world (John 16:33); rejoice and be exceeding glad, for great is your reward in heaven.

question: What is the comfort of those facing martyrdom?
answer: Be faithful unto death, and I will give thee the crown of life (Revelation 2:10); the souls under the altar are remembered by Christ.

question: What is the comfort of those who feel weak in prayer?
answer: The Spirit himself maketh intercession for us with groanings which cannot be uttered (Romans 8:26).

question: What is the comfort of those who feel weak in witness?
answer: It is not by might, nor by power, but by the Spirit; he gives words and boldness in the moment (Zechariah 4:6; Mark 13:11).

question: What is the comfort of those struggling to forgive?
answer: You are not alone; ask the Spirit's help, recall how Christ forgave you, take the slow steps, and trust God with full justice.

question: What is the comfort of those who pray with no answer?
answer: He hears, and his timing is best; sometimes he answers yes, sometimes no, sometimes wait — but he is always faithful to his promises.

question: What is the comfort of those who feel small?
answer: The Lord uses the lowly to confound the wise (1 Corinthians 1:27-29); he is glorified in our weakness, magnified in faith-filled lives.

question: What is the comfort of those raising teenagers?
answer: He gives wisdom to humble parents; trust your child to God, model a real walk with him, pray often, and keep the relationship strong.

question: What is the comfort of those facing vocational change?
answer: Whatever you do, do all to the glory of God; commit your works unto the Lord, and your thoughts shall be established (1 Corinthians 10:31; Proverbs 16:3).

question: What is the comfort of those moving to a new place?
answer: He goes before you and is with you; in any city, he has people for whom you can pray and serve (Acts 18:9-10).

question: What is the comfort of those struggling with church-hurt?
answer: Christ remains the Head of the church; healthy gospel communities exist; bring your story honestly to God and seek a faithful place to heal.

question: What is the comfort of those leaving harmful religion?
answer: Look to Jesus alone — read the Gospels, meet the real Christ, and gather with believers who exalt the gospel of grace; he is the friend of sinners.

question: What is the comfort of those overwhelmed by injustice?
answer: The Judge of all the earth will do right (Genesis 18:25); the Lord defends the cause of the oppressed and will set all things right at his coming.

question: What is the comfort of those grieving their nation?
answer: Pray for kings and all in authority; God is sovereign over nations; let his kingdom be your hope and refuge (1 Timothy 2:1-2; Psalm 46).

question: What is the comfort of those amid crisis?
answer: He is a refuge in time of trouble (Psalm 46:1); let your peace be in the unchanging Christ, who calms storms with a word.

question: What is the comfort of those who feel defeated?
answer: In Christ we are more than conquerors through him who loved us; nothing can separate us from God's love (Romans 8:37-39).

question: What is the comfort of those longing for home?
answer: You are pilgrims journeying to a city whose builder and maker is God; he has prepared a place for you (Hebrews 11:10; John 14:2-3).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'Father, glorify thy name'?
answer: In John 12:28, Jesus prayed for the Father's glory to be displayed even through his coming death; the Father answered with a voice from heaven.

question: What did Jesus mean by 'Now is my soul troubled'?
answer: He spoke of his agony as his hour approached, yet refused to ask to be saved from it, knowing he came for that very purpose (John 12:27).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'I have come to save the world'?
answer: He came not to judge the world, but that the world should be saved through him (John 3:17; 12:47).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'Father, the hour is come'?
answer: He acknowledged that the appointed time of his death and glorification had arrived (John 17:1).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'I have manifested thy name'?
answer: He revealed the Father's character and being to his disciples — making God known (John 17:6).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'I have given them thy word'?
answer: He passed on the Father's truth to his disciples, who received it and believed (John 17:8).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'I am no more in the world'?
answer: He spoke of his coming departure, sending his disciples into the world while he returned to the Father (John 17:11).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'as thou didst send me, so I send them'?
answer: His disciples are sent into the world with the same mission of revealing the Father and proclaiming the gospel (John 17:18; 20:21).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'I sanctify myself'?
answer: He set himself apart for the cross, that his disciples might also be sanctified in truth (John 17:19).

question: What did Jesus mean by the disciples' future glory?
answer: He prayed that his disciples might be where he is, beholding his glory which the Father gave him from before the foundation of the world (John 17:24).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'whom seek ye?'
answer: In Gethsemane, Jesus identified himself willingly to those who came to arrest him, fulfilling Scripture (John 18:4-8).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'put up the sword'?
answer: He rebuked Peter's violent defense, willingly accepting the cup the Father had given him (John 18:11).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'thou couldest have no power'?
answer: He told Pilate that all his authority over Christ came from above; thus the greater sin lay with those who delivered him up (John 19:11).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'I thirst'?
answer: In his physical suffering and to fulfill Scripture, he expressed his thirst on the cross (John 19:28; Psalm 69:21).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'peace be unto you' after resurrection?
answer: He pronounced gospel peace on the disciples — peace with God secured by his death (John 20:19, 21, 26).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'Receive the Holy Spirit'?
answer: It was a foretaste and promise of the fuller outpouring of the Spirit at Pentecost (John 20:22).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'whose sins ye remit'?
answer: He commissioned the church to declare forgiveness to those who repent and believe — and to withhold it from those who reject (John 20:23).

question: What did Jesus mean by 'lovest thou me?'
answer: He restored Peter after his threefold denial with a threefold question, recommissioning him to feed his sheep (John 21:15-17).

question: What does it mean to be salt and light in the world?
answer: It means to live in such a way that you preserve and enhance what is good and shine the truth and love of Christ into the darkness (Matthew 5:13-16).

question: What is the meaning of love rejoicing in truth?
answer: True love rejoices not in unrighteousness but in truth (1 Corinthians 13:6) — never celebrating sin, but always honoring what is right.

question: What is the meaning of love bearing all things?
answer: Love patiently endures hurts, faults, and difficulties, holding people up rather than tearing them down (1 Corinthians 13:7).

question: What is the meaning of love believing all things?
answer: Love believes the best, refuses to assume evil, and remains hopeful in difficult relationships (1 Corinthians 13:7).

question: What is the meaning of love hoping all things?
answer: Love continues to hope for the best, even when things look dark, looking to God for change and grace (1 Corinthians 13:7).

question: What is the meaning of love enduring all things?
answer: Love perseveres through trials, hardships, and disappointments, anchored in God's love that has been poured out in our hearts (1 Corinthians 13:7).

question: What does it mean love is patient?
answer: It means love is longsuffering — slow to anger, willing to wait, gracious with people's faults (1 Corinthians 13:4).

question: What does it mean love is kind?
answer: It means love actively does good, treating others with thoughtfulness and warmth (1 Corinthians 13:4).

question: What does it mean love does not envy?
answer: It means love rejoices when others are blessed, refusing the bitter resentment of comparison (1 Corinthians 13:4).

question: What does it mean love is not boastful?
answer: It means love does not parade itself; it doesn't seek the spotlight (1 Corinthians 13:4).

question: What does it mean love is not arrogant?
answer: It means love is not puffed up; it walks humbly, esteeming others (1 Corinthians 13:4).

question: What does it mean love is not rude?
answer: It means love does not behave indecently; it acts with courtesy, honoring others' dignity (1 Corinthians 13:5).

question: What does it mean love seeks not its own?
answer: It means love does not insist on getting its way; it seeks the good of others above its own (1 Corinthians 13:5).

question: What does it mean love is not provoked?
answer: It means love is not easily irritated or angered; it bears with people's failings (1 Corinthians 13:5).

question: What does it mean love takes no account of evil?
answer: It means love does not keep score of wrongs; it refuses to harbor resentment (1 Corinthians 13:5).

question: What does it mean love rejoices in truth?
answer: It means love rejoices when truth and righteousness prevail and never delights in unrighteousness (1 Corinthians 13:6).

question: What does it mean love never fails?
answer: It means love is permanent and unconquerable; tongues, prophecies, and knowledge will pass, but love abides forever (1 Corinthians 13:8).

question: What does Hebrews 1:1-3 teach about Christ?
answer: He is the heir of all things, through whom God made the worlds; the radiance of his glory, the express image of his substance, upholding all things by his powerful word.

question: What does Hebrews 2:9 teach about Christ?
answer: He was made a little lower than the angels, that by the grace of God he should taste of death for every man.

question: What does Hebrews 2:14 teach about Christ?
answer: He shared in flesh and blood that through death he might bring to nought him that had the power of death, that is, the devil.

question: What does Hebrews 2:18 teach about Christ?
answer: In that he himself hath suffered being tempted, he is able to succor them that are tempted.

question: What does Hebrews 3:1 teach?
answer: Wherefore, holy brethren, partakers of a heavenly calling, consider the Apostle and High Priest of our confession, even Jesus.

question: What does Hebrews 4:14 teach?
answer: Having then a great high priest, who hath passed through the heavens, Jesus the Son of God, let us hold fast our confession.

question: What does Hebrews 5:14 teach about maturity?
answer: Solid food is for full-grown men, even those who by reason of use have their senses exercised to discern good and evil.

question: What does Hebrews 7:26 teach about Christ?
answer: For such a high priest became us, holy, guileless, undefiled, separated from sinners, and made higher than the heavens.

question: What does Hebrews 8:1 teach?
answer: We have such a high priest, who sat down on the right hand of the throne of the Majesty in the heavens.

question: What does Hebrews 9:14 teach?
answer: How much more shall the blood of Christ, who through the eternal Spirit offered himself without blemish unto God, cleanse your conscience from dead works.

question: What does Hebrews 9:24 teach?
answer: Christ entered not into a holy place made with hands... but into heaven itself, now to appear before the face of God for us.

question: What does Hebrews 9:28 teach?
answer: Christ was once offered to bear the sins of many, and shall appear a second time, apart from sin, to them that wait for him, unto salvation.

question: What does Hebrews 10:10 teach?
answer: By which will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.

question: What does Hebrews 10:14 teach?
answer: For by one offering he hath perfected for ever them that are sanctified.

question: What does Hebrews 11:1 teach about faith?
answer: Faith is assurance of things hoped for, a conviction of things not seen.

question: What does Hebrews 11:6 teach about faith?
answer: Without faith it is impossible to be well-pleasing unto him.

question: What does Hebrews 12:5 teach?
answer: My son, regard not lightly the chastening of the Lord, nor faint when thou art reproved of him.

question: What does Hebrews 12:11 teach?
answer: All chastening seemeth for the present to be not joyous but grievous: yet afterward it yieldeth peaceable fruit unto them that have been exercised thereby.

question: What does Hebrews 13:14 teach?
answer: For we have not here an abiding city, but we seek after the city which is to come.

question: What does Hebrews 13:16 teach?
answer: To do good and to communicate forget not: for with such sacrifices God is well pleased.

question: What does Hebrews 13:20-21 teach?
answer: The God of peace, who brought again from the dead the great shepherd of the sheep with the blood of an eternal covenant, even our Lord Jesus, make you perfect in every good thing.

question: What does James 1:12 teach?
answer: Blessed is the man that endureth temptation; for when he hath been approved, he shall receive the crown of life.

question: What does James 1:14-15 teach?
answer: Each man is tempted, when he is drawn away by his own lust, and enticed... and the lust, when it hath conceived, beareth sin.

question: What does James 2:5 teach?
answer: Did not God choose them that are poor as to the world to be rich in faith, and heirs of the kingdom?

question: What does James 2:13 teach?
answer: Mercy glorieth against judgment.

question: What does James 2:26 teach?
answer: As the body apart from the spirit is dead, even so faith apart from works is dead.

question: What does James 3:9-10 teach?
answer: With the tongue we bless God; and with it we curse men... my brethren, these things ought not so to be.

question: What does James 4:6 teach?
answer: He giveth more grace. Wherefore the scripture saith, God resisteth the proud, but giveth grace to the humble.

question: What does James 4:13-14 teach?
answer: Come now, ye that say, Today or tomorrow we will go into this city... whereas ye know not what shall be on the morrow. What is your life? For ye are a vapor.

question: What does James 5:7-8 teach?
answer: Be patient therefore, brethren, until the coming of the Lord. Behold, the husbandman waiteth for the precious fruit of the earth.

question: What does James 5:11 teach?
answer: Behold, we call them blessed that endured: ye have heard of the patience of Job.

question: What does 1 Peter 1:6-7 teach?
answer: Greatly rejoice, though now for a little while, ye have been put to grief in manifold trials, that the proof of your faith... may be found unto praise and glory.

question: What does 1 Peter 1:13 teach?
answer: Wherefore girding up the loins of your mind, be sober and set your hope perfectly on the grace that is to be brought unto you at the revelation of Jesus Christ.

question: What does 1 Peter 1:18-19 teach?
answer: Ye were redeemed... with precious blood, as of a lamb without blemish and without spot, even the blood of Christ.

question: What does 1 Peter 2:4-5 teach?
answer: Coming unto him, a living stone... ye also, as living stones, are built up a spiritual house, to be a holy priesthood.

question: What does 1 Peter 2:11-12 teach?
answer: Beloved, I beseech you as sojourners and pilgrims, to abstain from fleshly lusts, which war against the soul.

question: What does 1 Peter 3:3-4 teach?
answer: Whose adorning let it not be the outward... but let it be the hidden man of the heart, in the incorruptible apparel of a meek and quiet spirit.

question: What does 1 Peter 4:8 teach?
answer: Above all things being fervent in your love among yourselves; for love covereth a multitude of sins.

question: What does 1 Peter 4:10 teach?
answer: According as each hath received a gift, ministering it among yourselves, as good stewards of the manifold grace of God.

question: What does 1 Peter 5:8-9 teach?
answer: Be sober, be watchful: your adversary the devil, as a roaring lion, walketh about, seeking whom he may devour: whom withstand stedfast in your faith.

question: What does 2 Peter 1:5-7 teach?
answer: Adding on your part all diligence, in your faith supply virtue; and in your virtue knowledge; and in your knowledge self-control.

question: What does 2 Peter 1:19 teach?
answer: We have the word of prophecy made more sure; whereunto ye do well that ye take heed, as unto a lamp shining in a dark place.

question: What does 1 John 2:1 teach?
answer: My little children, these things write I unto you that ye may not sin. And if any man sin, we have an Advocate with the Father.

question: What does 1 John 3:1 teach?
answer: Behold what manner of love the Father hath bestowed upon us, that we should be called children of God.

question: What does 1 John 3:2 teach?
answer: Beloved, now are we children of God, and it is not yet made manifest what we shall be.

question: What does 1 John 4:4 teach?
answer: Greater is he that is in you than he that is in the world.

question: What does 1 John 4:7 teach?
